fiches.title: National Day Of Unplugging
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
13
FOCUS
•
Pile of yarn
•
Bucket
•
20 small foam balls
•
4 bowling pin
•
4 images of technology (phone,
computer….etc.)
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Day of Unplugging is a day we unplug our electronics and make time for physical 
activity and socialization with friends or family. In this lesson, we will pull the plug on 
electronics!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
DUMP THE ELECTRONICS
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place 2 bowling pins on each end line of the 
gym.  Tape a picture of some form of electronics 
on each pin such as a cell phone, computer, TV, or 
tablet.  Give each team 10 foam balls.  Place half 
the class on each half of the room and separate the 
teams by a center line.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the students will attempt to ‘dump
the electronics’ of the other team by bowling
(rolling) a ball at their competition bowling pins
(symbolizing the electronics) on the other half of
the room.
•
Each team may have one or two guards of the
pin if they want to.
•
Children may not go to the other team’s half of
the room to collect loose balls.  They can only
pick up loose balls in their half of the room to roll
at the other team’s pins.
•
The first team to knock over both pins on the
other team win the round.
Easier: Shorten the distance to roll the balls.
Harder: Increase the distance to roll the balls and use 
more pins.
UNPLUG THE CORD
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place the students in traditional relay race 
lines.  Place about 20 tangled pieces of yarn on the 
other end of the room in a neat pile to simulate 
electrical cords that need to be unplugged.  Mark off 
a start line on the floor.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in line must run to their pile
of yarn.
•
Once there, they must carefully pull out 1 piece
of yarn (simulating unplugging electronics) and
bring it back to the start line and place it in their
team’s bucket.  They need to be careful when
they pull the yarn to not make more knots in it
for their teammates.
•
Once they put the yarn into their team’s bucket,
the next kid goes.
•
The first team to finish and bring all their yarn
back wins the race.
Easier: Use jump ropes instead of yarn.
Harder: Use a lot of yarn to make it more challenging 
to untangle.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Throws underhand with the opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)

fiches.title: National Meatball Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
14
FOCUS
•
2 large wooden spoons
•
2 tennis balls
•
2 cones
•
Pool noodles
•
Hula hoops
•
Foam balls
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Meatball Day celebrates everything related to our love of meatballs.  In this lesson, 
we will play some fun PE games involving meatballs to keep you active and moving.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
MEATBALL RUN
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Place the children in 2 traditional relay race 
lines.  Give the first child in line a wooden spoon and 
a tennis ball.  The first child has to balance the tennis 
ball on the spoon.  Tape a start line to the floor and 
use cones to mark their end point.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child will walk, jog, or run with
their meatball (tennis ball) on their spoon
towards their team’s cone.
•
They need to move around their cone and
back to the start line where they hand off their
meatball and spoon to the next student.
•
The next student follows the same pattern.
•
The first team to finish wins the round.
•
If a meatball falls, the student needs to stop,
pick it up and place it back on the spoon, then
continue.  They cannot use their hand to hold the
meatball in place.
Easier: Use a larger spoon and a smaller ball.
Harder: Use a smaller spoon and a larger ball.
SPAGHETTI & MEATBALLS
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Spread out hula hoops (dinner plates) and 
small foam balls (meatballs) around your room.  
Give half the class pool noodles (spaghetti). 
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the kids will begin running around the
room.
•
The kids without a pool noodle (spaghetti) are
picking up loose balls (meatballs) off the ground
and placing them into hula hoops (dinner plates).
•
The kids with the pool noodles are running
around the room hitting the balls out of the hula
hoops.
•
Continue the game for a few minutes, then have
the kids switch roles.
Easier: Use more balls and more hula hoops.
Harder: Use fewer balls and hula hoops.
STANDARDS
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
National Meatball Day

fiches.title: St. Patrick’s Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
15
FOCUS
•
20 tennis balls
•
3 buckets
•
20-30 hula hoops
•
1 bowling pin
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
St. Patrick’s Day always falls on March 17th! This is a day that celebrates everything green 
and the luck of the Irish.  In this lesson, we will play some exciting St. Patrick’s Day games.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
LEAPING LEPRECHAUN
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the students (Leprechauns) in 
traditional relay race lines.  Spread out hula hoops 
across your room that the kids will have to jump 
through.  Place a pot of gold (bucket) at the end of 
the room filled with gold (tennis balls).
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student must hop through hula
hoops to the pot of gold and take 1 tennis ball.
•
Once they have the ball, they must hop back to
their start line.
•
Once at the start line, they must drop their ball
into their team’s bucket and the next kid goes.
•
The first team that gets 10 balls into their bucket
wins that round.
Easier: Use fewer hula hoops and shorten the 
distance of the race.
Harder: Use more hula hoops and increase the 
distance of the race.
STEAL THE POT OF GOLD
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Put your students (Leprechauns) on 2 teams.  
Place each team on an end line while giving each 
student a number that corresponds to a kid on the 
other team.  Put a pot of gold (bowling pin) in the 
middle of the room.
Instructions:
•
Each student on each team will have a number
and will line up on opposing end lines.
•
When their number is called, they must run to
the middle where the pin is located.
•
One of the students will grab the pin and
attempt to run back safely with it to their end
line.  If they do make it, they receive a point.  But,
the opposing child will run after them and try to
tag them before they make it to their end line to
steal the point.
•
The first team to 20 points wins the round.
Easier: Shorten the length of the playing area.
Harder: Increase the length of the playing area.
STANDARDS
•
Performs jumping and landing actions with balance. (S1.E3.K)
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
St. Patrick’s Day
1
1
2
2
3
3
4
4

fiches.title: National Crayon Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
FOCUS
•
Boxes of crayons
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Crayon Day falls on the last day of the month on March 31st. It is a fun day to draw 
pictures, appreciate beautiful colors, and love everything about crayons. In this lesson, play some 
color games and draw some pictures.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
YOU’RE AN ARTIST
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Put the students in traditional relay race lines. 
Give each team a box of crayons.  Tape a large piece 
of paper on the wall across from each team.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in line takes a crayon from
the box and runs down to their paper.
•
Once they get to their paper, they have 5 seconds
to begin drawing an image.
•
Once complete, they run back, put the crayon
back, and the next child follows the same pattern.
•
Each student will continue to collaborate and
contribute to their image to create the greatest
team image they can produce with a lot of
creativity and color.
•
At the end, the kids can enjoy both teams final
products.
Variations: Change the locomotor. Have the kids hop, 
crawl, or skip back and forth from the picture.
THE RAINBOW CONNECTION
Time: 7-10 minutes
Setup: Place all your students on one end line of the 
room.
Instructions:
•
The teacher will call out various colors with a 
command that follows.
•
For example, anyone wearing blue can jog across 
the room to the opposite end line.
•
All the kids in blue must jog across to the other 
side.
•
Next, a new command such as all the kids 
wearing green must skip to the opposite end line. 
All the kids in this color no matter what end line 
they are on must skip to the opposite side.
•
The teacher can be as creative as they would like 
depending on the skill level of each class when 
they call out instructions.
Variations: Add tasks that require equipment, such 
as hula hoops, jump ropes or balls.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Uses communication skills and strategies that promote a team or group dynamics. (S4.H3.L1)
National Crayon Day

fiches.title: April Fool’s Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
17
FOCUS
•
Soccer ball
•
2 bowling pins
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
April Fool’s Day is on April 1st. On this day, we can joke around and play pranks with friends or 
family.  In this lesson, we will perform some backwards and silly activities!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BACKWARD SOCCER RACE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place the class in 2 traditional relay race lines.  
Put 2 bowling pins in front of each team about 5 feet 
apart.  Tape a start line on the ground.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line will start to dribble
the soccer ball backward using the back part of
their foot above the heel.
•
Each child must dribble the ball doing a figure 8
pattern through the bowling pins.
•
If they knock down a pin, they must pick it up
and begin again from the start line.
•
Once they get around the second pin, they must
come back following the same pattern as they
pass the ball to the next kid in line.
•
The first team to finish wins the round.
Variation:  Use cones instead of pins.  This way, the 
kids do not have to restart each time a pin gets 
knocked down.
ANIMAL TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread the kids out around the room and 
designate 1 or 2 kids to be the chasers.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the chasers will begin to run around the
room chasing the other kids.
•
If a child gets tagged, the chaser will call them a
name of an animal.
•
Next, the kid who got caught must walk, crawl,
run, or skip around the room as if they were that
specific animal for 10 seconds.
•
For example, if the child who got tagged is a
turtle, maybe they will crawl like a turtle around
the room for 10 seconds.
•
After a few minutes, pick new chasers.
Variation: Instead of acting like animals, they can act 
like famous athletes. 
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Uses communication skills and strategies that promote a team or group dynamics. (S4.H3.L1)
April Fool’s Day

sections.Equipment:

family.  In this lesson, we will perform some backwards and silly activities!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BACKWARD SOCCER RACE
Time: 10-15 minutes

run, or skip around the room as if they were that
specific animal for 10 seconds.
•
For example, if the child who got tagged is a
turtle, maybe they will crawl like a turtle around
the room for 10 seconds.
•
After a few minutes, pick new chasers.
sections.Variation:
like famous athletes.

•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)

fiches.title: National Walking Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
FOCUS
•
Music
•
Floor tape
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Walking Day celebrates everything related to walking as a great daily exercise.  In 
this lesson, we will play some fun PE games involving walking skills.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
WALK TO CRABWALK
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Spread the students around the room so they 
are not too close together.  Have music ready to play 
that the children can hear.
Instructions:
•
When the teacher plays music, the students will 
begin to walk around the room, respecting the 
personal space of their peers.
•
Anytime the music stops, the children will now 
begin to crabwalk around the room.
•
When the music begins again, they will now 
begin to walk again around the room.
•
The age and skills of the students will determine 
how long the music stays off.
Variations: If crab walking is too hard, try ”crab 
scooting” - keeping your bottom sliding on the 
ground.
WALKING THE PLANK
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Spread out children around the room.  The 
students must stand on lines in the gym.  Use floor 
tape to create more lines if needed.  Have music 
ready to play.
Instructions:
•
When the music begins, the students must start
to walk around the room.
•
While they are walking, they are only allowed to
travel on the predetermined lines on the floor.
•
Children cannot walk on any part of the floor
without a line.
•
If a child passes another student, they must high-
five them as they pass each other.  Students may
leave the line for a split second while passing by
another student.
Variations: Allow the students to speed walk, hop, 
shuffle, or skip around the room.
STANDARDS
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)

fiches.title: Jackie Robinson Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
19
FOCUS
•
Pin
•
Cone
•
Whiffle ball bat
•
Whiffle ball
•
Bases
•
10 yarn balls
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Jackie Robinson Day is on April 15th. This is a day where that celebrates the legendary baseball 
player who became a hero to so many people.  In this lesson, we will play some exciting games 
related to baseball.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
GOING DEEP
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the class on 2 teams divided by a 
middle line.  Give each team 5 yarn balls to begin the 
game.  Yarn balls are safer to use, but the game can 
be modified using sponge balls.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the students will begin to throw the balls 
onto the other team’s half of the room.
•
Their objective is to get all 10 balls onto the 
opposite half of the room at the same time.
•
The first team that can accomplish this wins the 
round.
Variation: The students can play the game with 15 
balls.  Also, you can add a bowling pin on each end 
line of the room.  If one team can also knock over the 
bowling pin, they would get an automatic win.
PIN BASEBALL
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Place the students on two teams, one team 
on the field, and one team up at bat.  Give each team 
a batting order as well.  Set up bases.  Place a pin in 
the corner of the room with a throwing line about 10 
feet away.  Place a cone with a whiffle ball balanced 
on top at home plate and have a whiffle bat at home 
plate to hit the ball.
Instructions:
•
Each team bats everyone each inning.
•
The first player must hit the ball off the cone and
run all the bases with the objective of getting
home safely.
•
The fielding team must retrieve the ball and
run to the throwing line with the objective of
knocking over the pin to get the runner out
taking multiple turns until the runner crosses
home plate.
•
Points are awarded if a runner gets home safely
or if the fielding team can knock over the pin.
•
After each player gets a turn batting, teams will
switch sides.
Variations: Students can throw the ball rather 
than hit the ball off the cone.
STANDARDS
•
Throws overhand to a partner or at a target with accuracy at a reasonable distance. (S1.E14.4b)
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
Jackie Robinson Day
Ω

fiches.title: Earth Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
FOCUS
•
Cones
•
2 water bottles
•
1 hula hoop
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Earth Day always falls on April 22nd. It is a day when we celebrate our planet Earth 
and the importance of protecting the environment. In this lesson, we’ll play some fun 
games related to cleaning up and recycling.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
HUMAN WHEELBARROW
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Put the students in traditional relay race 
lines.  Place a cone on the opposite end line.  The first 
2 kids in line should set up in the wheelbarrow 
positions.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in line gets into a push-up
position as the second kid in line picks up their 
feet and holds them.
•
The 2 kids will now travel in tandem with one
child using their hands, and the other using their
feet (as shown in the image above).
•
They must travel around their cone and come
back to the start line.  Once back, the child
holding the feet now assumes the push-up
position as the third kid in line will hold the feet.
The first child goes to the end of the line.
•
The race ends when the first child finishes the
race as a foot holder.  The team that finishes first
wins the round.
Easier: Shorten the distance to travel.
Harder: Increase the distance to travel.
RECYCLE FLIP
Time: 7-10 minutes
Setup: Place all your students in 2 lines as shown in 
the image with a hula hoop in the middle of your 
room. Give each team 1 water bottle tightly closed 
and about 1/3 full of water.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line will bottle flip
towards the hula hoop.
•
Their objective is to land the bottle so it is
standing up straight to receive the point.
•
Whether they succeed or not, they must retrieve
the bottle and hand it off to the next student,
then go to the end of their line to wait for their
turn again.
•
After 10 minutes, the team with the most points
wins that round.
Easier: Allow the students to stand wherever they 
want.
Harder: Have the students stand at a further distance 
to flip the bottle.
STANDARDS
•
Throws underhand with the opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
Earth Day

fiches.title: Project ACES Day Project ACES Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
21
FOCUS
•
None
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Project ACES falls at the beginning of May.  ACES stands for All Children Exercise 
Simultaneously.  On this day, children all over the world exercise simultaneously to promote a 
healthy lifestyle.   In this lesson, we will participate in some fitness activities!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
JUMPING JACKS TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out all over your room 
and pick one child to be the chaser.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ all the children must travel around the
room while performing ‘moving’ jumping jacks.
The kids must travel by leaping forward with their
legs apart, and land with their feet together as
they bring their hands over their heads to create
a jumping jack.
•
The chasers also travel performing jumping jacks.
•
If a child gets caught, they stop in their spot and
do 10 stationary jumping jacks.  Once complete,
they can now move about the room once again
doing jumping jacks as they travel.
•
After a few minutes, switch to new chasers.
Variation:  Have multiple children be the chasers at 
one time to bring a higher level of difficulty to the 
game.
TUNNEL RUN
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place the students in two lines to create 
teams.  Have a start line and a finish line.  All the 
students must get into an ‘up’ push-up position at 
the start.  The first child is the crawler in line.
Instructions:
•
Set up a start line and finish line with a travel
path the kids can perform.
•
All the students except for the first child in line
must be in an ‘up’ push-up position.  The tunnel
kids must make sure their feet touch the kid next
to them to prevent extending the tunnel too far
apart.  On ‘go’ the first child must crawl through
their teammate’s push-up tunnel.
•
When they get to the end, they now form the
end part of the tunnel as they get into the push-
up position.
•
The next child in line then begins to crawl
through the tunnel until they get to the end
following the same pattern as the child before
them.  The first team to cross the finish line wins.
Variation: Make it easier by having the kids in a “V” 
position instead of a flat plank.
STANDARDS
•
Uses communication skills and strategies that promote team or group dynamics. (S4.H3.L1)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
Project ACES Day
Project ACES Day

fiches.title: Star Wars Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
22
FOCUS
•
2 hula hoops
•
25 pool noodles
•
25 balloons
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
May 4th is Star Wars Day. As they say, “May the fourth be with you.”  On this day, we celebrate 
everything related to Star Wars.  In this lesson, we will play some fun PE games involving rebels, 
stormtroopers, flying spaceships, and a Jedi.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
STORMTROOPER VS. REBELS
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out around your room.  
All the children are rebels except for 1 or 2 of whom 
you make stormtroopers.  Give each stormtrooper 
a hula hoop.  Pick one student to be a Jedi and give 
them a pool noodle.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the rebels will run around the room.
•
The stormtroopers will be kicking their hula hoop
(spaceship) and the rebels with the objective of
hitting them.
•
If a rebel gets hit, they are now frozen in their
spot.
•
The Jedi can run over to them and free them by
tapping them on the shoulder with their pool
noodle as the game continues.
•
After a few minutes, have the students switch
roles.
Variations: Pick multiple students to be 
stormtroopers and multiple kids to be a Jedi.
LIGHT SABER BATTLE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread out children around the room.  Give 
each student a pool noodle and balloon filled with 
air.
Instructions:
•
All the children are Jedis fighting off the evil 
empire.
•
Their pool noodle is their light saber, and the 
balloon is their energy force they must keep 
alive.
•
On ‘go’ the children must move about the room 
keeping their balloon afloat by tapping it in the 
air using their pool noodle.
•
If a balloon hits the ground, their energy ran out, 
and they must sit on the ground.
•
All the kids left after 2 minutes won their battles.
Variations: Have the skilled students use 2 balloons 
to keep afloat for more of a challenge.
STANDARDS
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
Star Wars Day

fiches.title: National Chocolate Chip Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
23
FOCUS
•
50 bean bags
•
2 hula hoops
•
2 cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Chocolate Chip Day is a day that celebrates our love for chocolate chips.  In this 
lesson, we will play some exciting games related to chocolate chips.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
CHOCOLATE CHIP RELAY
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the class in 2 traditional relay race lines.  
Give each team 25 bean bags.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student must run with a bean
bag down to their cone, go around it, and come
back to start.
•
They must hand off the bean bag to the next kid
in line, who must now run with 2 bean bags to
the cone and back.
•
With each additional kid that goes, a new bean
bag must be added to their team running.
•
Eventually, kids will have to start balancing bean
bags and carrying bean bags awkwardly to their
cone and back.
•
At any time, if a bean bag falls to the ground,
the team must start over, but they retain their
highest score which is the number of bean bags
carried at one time.  After 5 minutes, the team
with the most bags carried wins the round.
Variation: Allow teammates to help balance and load 
bean bags onto the current runner.
CC COOKIE
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Place the students in traditional relay race 
lines with a start line and a hula hoop (cookie) at the 
other end.  Give each team 25 bean bags (chocolate 
chips) stacked in a pile by their start line.  Tape the 
hula hoop to the ground to secure it.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first kid in line must toss their
chocolate chip (bean bag) at their cookie (hula
hoop) with the objective of landing a bean bag
into a hoop.
•
If they make it in, they go to the end of the line
and wait for their turn again.
•
If they miss, they must run to get the bean bag
and place it back in the pile.
•
Once completed, the next child follows the same
pattern.
•
The first team to get all 25 bean bags into their
hula hoop first wins the round.
Variations: To make it more challenging, you can try 
the same race using just one tennis ball.  The team 
that lands the ‘lone’ tennis ball into the hoop first 
wins the round.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
National Chocolate Chip Day

fiches.title: National Bike To Work Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
24
FOCUS
•
Scooters
•
Hula hoops
•
2 jump ropes
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Bike To Work Day is a day when many individuals bike to work (or even school) to 
promote a healthy lifestyle. In this lesson, play some fun ‘wheel’ activities to get you up and 
moving.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
HANDLEBAR RUN
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the students out around your room 
and give each kid a hula hoop to be used as a bicycle 
handlebar to steer.  Go over the fundamentals of 
jogging, skipping, and galloping.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the children will begin to jog around the
room using their hula hoop to steer in which
direction they travel on their bicycle.
•
When the teacher blows the whistle, they stop,
The next whistle, they begin to jog again.  The
teacher will continue this pattern for a few
minutes by blowing multiple whistles.
•
Next, the teacher will switch the skills to skipping
as the movement, followed by galloping.
•
Be as creative as you like with various
movements.
Variation: The kids can try the same movements 
but dribble a soccer ball as well to add an extra 
‘challenge’ component.
BIKE TOW
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place all your students in 2 traditional relay 
race lines.  Use floor tape to create a start line, and 
place cones at the end line.  Give each team a scooter 
with a jump rope tied to it. 
Instructions:
•
Each team’s bike has a flat tire and they need a
tow.
•
On ‘go’ the first kid in line will tow the second kid
in line around their cone and back to the start
line.
•
Once there, the kid who just towed goes to the
end of the line while the kid who just sat on the
scooter will now tow the next child in line.
•
This pattern continues until the first student in
line gets back to the beginning and is the one
being towed.  The first team to finish wins the
race.
•
For safety, the child towing is only allowed to
walk fast, not run.  The child sitting cannot help
and must have all their body parts resting on the
scooter, not the ground.
Variations: Take away the jump ropes and have the 
kids sitting on the scooters push themselves.
STANDARDS
•
Recognizes the use of the body as the resistance for developing strength. (S3.E3.2a)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
National Bike To Work Day

being towed.  The first team to finish wins the
race.
•
For safety, the child towing is only allowed to
walk fast, not run.  The child sitting cannot help
and must have all their body parts resting on the
scooter, not the ground.
sections.Variation:
but dribble a soccer ball as well to add an extra
‘challenge’ component.
BIKE TOW
Time: 10-15 minutes
sections.Variations:
kids sitting on the scooters push themselves.
STANDARDS
•
Recognizes the use of the body as the resistance for developing strength. (S3.E3.2a)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
National Bike To Work Day

fiches.title: World Milk Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
25
FOCUS
•
2 milk crates
•
100 bean bags
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
World Milk Day is June 1st. On this day, we celebrate the importance of milk as it contains 
many vitamins and minerals that help kids develop strong muscles and bones.   In this lesson, 
we will participate in some milk-related games!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
DON’T SPILL THE MILK
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out all over your room 
and pick one child to be the chaser.  Give each 
child a bean bag that symbolizes a glass of milk.
Instructions:
•
Pick one child to be the chaser.
•
All the children must balance a bean bag on top
of their head without using their hands during
the game.
•
On ‘go’ all the children must travel around the
room balancing their milk glass on top of their
head, including the chaser.
•
The chaser will try to tag other children.
•
If a child gets tagged, they must perform 10
jumping jacks and then return to the game.
•
After a few minutes, switch to new chasers.
Variation:  Have the children balance 2 bean bags on 
their heads to make it more challenging.
MILK DELIVERY
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place the students in two traditional relay 
race lines.  Place a start line on the floor and a 
shooting line.  Put milk crates at the far end of the 
room.  Give each team 50 bean bags (symbolizing 
the milk) which are placed at the start line.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first kid in line runs with a bean bag
to the shooting line at the other end of the room.
•
Once there, they must shoot their bean bag
(milk) into the milk crate to make a delivery.
•
If they make the shot, they must run back to start
so the next child can go.  If they miss, they must
pick up their bean bag and run back and drop it
in their team’s pile.
•
Once they come back, the next kid in line picks
up a bean bag and follows the same pattern.
•
The first team to deliver all their milk (bean bags)
first wins the race.
Variation: If you can find old milk cartons that are 
clean, you can substitute those instead of the bean 
bags.
STANDARDS
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without
hesitation. (S1.E6.3)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
World Milk Day

fiches.title: Global Running Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
26
FOCUS
•
Sponge balls
•
20+ cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Global Running Day not only celebrates running, but movement in general, activity, and a 
healthy lifestyle.  In this lesson, we will play some fun PE games involving running, 
jogging, and exercise.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BAND-AID
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out around your room.  
Pick one child to be the chaser. Pick another child to 
be a nurse, and a final kid to be a doctor.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the chaser will begin to run after other
kids with the objective of tagging them.
•
When a child gets tagged, they now use their
hand as a band-aid and must place their hand
over the spot they were tagged and continue to
play.
•
If they get tagged a second time, they now place
their other hand (band-aid) over the spot they
were tagged.
•
Once both hands are used, the tagged child is
now frozen in their spot until the doctor pulls
away one band-aid and the nurse pulls away the
other band-aid.  Then, the child is now able to
return to gameplay.
•
After a few minutes, have the kids switch roles in
the game.
Variation:  You can pick more than 1 child to be the 
chaser.
JOG BATTLE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place students in groups of 5 or 6 and have 
them create a circle. Place a cone between each 
child in the circle.  Give a large, sponge ball to one 
child to start the game.  Create a jogging track 
around the perimeter of your room.
Instructions:
•
In this activity, each child is a goalie and must not
let the ball go through their two cones.
•
On ‘go’ the child with the ball will punch the ball
toward another kid’s goal with the objective of
hitting the ball through the cones.
•
When a ball comes to a child, they will stop it or
hit it as it moves back towards another student’s
goal with the objective of hitting the ball through
two cones.
•
Each time a student gives up 2 goals, the game
pauses as that child must now run 1 lap around
your room.  Once completed, they come back
and the game resumes.
Variation: You can make the circle bigger and use 2 
balls at a time.
STANDARDS
•
Performs jumping and landing actions with balance. (S1.E3.K)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
Global Running Day
Chaser

fiches.title: First Day Of Summer
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
27
FOCUS
•
Pool noodle
•
Whiffle balls
•
4 cones
•
2 hula hoops
•
40 bean bags
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
The Summer Solstice (first day of Summer) takes place on June 21st.    This is a day when we 
celebrate warmer weather and no school in some areas of the world.  In this lesson, we will play 
some exciting games related to Summer.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
SUMMER HULA CORNHOLE
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the class into 2 relay race lines.  Give 
each team a pile of 20 bean bags.  Use floor tape to 
create a throwing line.  Place 2 hula hoops about 20 
feet away from the throwing line.  Secure the hoops 
to the ground with tape.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line will toss their
bean bag at their hula hoop with the objective of
getting into the hoop.  If the bean bag touches
the hoop, it does not count.
•
If they make it, the next child goes.
•
If they miss, they must retrieve the bag and place
it back in their pile.
•
When the next kid goes, they take a bean bag
from the pile and follow the same pattern.
•
The first team to get all 20 bean bags into their
hoop wins.
Variation:  You can move the hula hoop farther 
away or closer depending on the skill level of the 
class.
POOL NOODLE HOCKEY
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Place the students on 2 teams.  Give each 
child a pool noodle and place 2 balls on the court.  
Each team should have a goalie and kids on offense 
and defense.  Depending on class size, the teacher 
will determine how many kids play each position.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the children will play this game like
regular hockey.
•
Instead of hockey sticks, the kids will use a pool
noodle.
•
Instead of a hockey puck, you will use 2 whiffle
balls.
•
Two balls will keep the kids more active, but
you can always use one ball for a slower, more
controlled game.
•
Kids must stay in their designated positions.
•
After each goal, gameplay stops, and the balls are
placed back in the middle.   Then a new round
begins.
Variation: You can substitute a beach ball for the 
whiffle balls as long as the kids swing safely.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Throws underhand with opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)
First Day Of Summer

SUMMER HULA CORNHOLE

fiches.title: National Handshake Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
28
FOCUS
•
Music
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Handshake Day is June 29th.  This is a day when we show people respect and 
friendship with a kind handshake. In this lesson, we will play some fun activities involving 
handshakes or high fives.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
SILLY HANDSHAKE GAME
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the students out around your room.  
This will be a teacher-led lesson to start, so the 
instructor should be easily visible to all students.
Instructions:
•
The teacher will demonstrate a special
handshake with the class that is unique.  For
example, doing 3 jumping jacks, followed by a
chest bump, and finishing with a high five.
•
Then, on ‘go’ the students will walk around the
room for one minute doing this handshake with
everyone they pass.
•
After a minute, the teacher will now ask a student
to show the class a new and unique special
handshake.
•
The class will follow the same pattern as earlier.
•
Try to give as many kids as you can a chance
to show the class their special handshake to
perform for 1 minute.
Variation:  Try a different locomotor: hopping, 
skipping, galloping or side shuffling.
SECRET HANDSHAKE BUDDY
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Partner up the children in pairs and have 
them spread out all around the room.
Instructions:
•
Put all the children in groups of 2 and give each 
group a number to represent them.
•
Once the children are in pairs, they must create 
a secret handshake that contains 5 different 
moves.
•
Once all the groups are ready, the teacher will 
begin to play music.  While the music is on, the 
children must skip around the room by 
themselves.
•
Once the music stops, the children must find 
their partner and stand back to back.
•
The teacher then calls out one group's number. 
This group must show the class their secret 
handshake.  Then, when the music starts again, 
each group must perform the secret handshake 
and begin to skip around the room again.
Variation: Try group handshakes with 3 or 4 kids at a 
time.
STANDARDS
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
National Handshake Day

fiches.title: World UFO Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
29
FOCUS
•
4 hula hoops
•
6 cones
•
50 bean bags
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
World UFO Day is on July 2nd. On this day, we celebrate things that our ‘out of this world.’  In 
this lesson, we will participate in outer space-related games!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
MISSILE COMMAND
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Divide the class into 2 teams.  Each team 
must stay on their half of the room.  Give each team 
about 25 bean bags to start the game.  Place a hula 
hoop (UFO) at each end line of the room.
Instructions:
•
Earth is being invaded by aliens in UFOs!
•
On ‘go’ the children will fire missiles at the UFOs
(hula hoops) by sliding the bean bags on the floor
from one side of the room to the other.
•
Any time a student can pick up a bean bag on
their half of the room, they can toss it across to
the other side.
•
The first team that can get 3 bean bags into the
other team’s hula hoop, they shoot down the
UFO and win the round.
•
After each round, have the kids collect bean bags
and start a new round.
Variation:  You can allow each team to have a guard 
for the hula hoop.
BLACK HOLE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Create a circle (black hole) in the center of 
your room using cones.  Place 4 hula hoops (space 
stations) near the outside corners of the room.  Pick 
2 students to be aliens (chasers).  The rest of the 
kids are civilians.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the aliens will begin to chase the civilians
around the room.
•
If a civilian gets tagged, they must stand in the
black hole (the center of the room).
•
At any time, a civilian who is not yet tagged
can run into the black hole and release another
civilian in the black hole by grabbing their hand
and pulling them out.  Only one rescue at a time.
•
If a civilian gets tired, they may rest freely in a
space station (hula hoop) where they cannot be
tagged. They can only stay in a space station for
20 seconds max.
•
After a few minutes, have the kids switch roles.
Variation: Change the number of space stations or 
change the locomotor to skipping or hopping.
STANDARDS
•
Throws underhand with the opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
World UFO Day
Chaser
Chaser

fiches.title: National Ice Cream Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
FOCUS
•
10 bean bags
•
2 medium size cones
•
2 small size cones
•
2 tennis balls
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
On National Ice Cream Day, we celebrate everything related to ice cream.  In this lesson, we 
will play some fun PE games with scoops of ice cream and flavors of ice cream.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
HOW MANY SCOOPS?
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Place the students in 2 relay race lines.  Give 
each team about 10 bean bags (scoops of ice cream).  
Place 2 (ice cream) cones on the far end line for each 
team.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line must travel to 
their cone  with one bean bag balanced on their 
head, go around it, and come back to start.
•
The next child in line will take the first child’s
bean bag, plus a second one from the pile, and
now follow the same pattern, but with two bean
bags balanced on their head.
•
When they come back, the next child will try to
balance 3 bean bags.
•
The children can not use their hands to balance
bean bags.
•
If they fall to the ground, the entire team must
start all over again with one bean bag.
•
The first team to go back and forth balancing 5
bean bags wins the race.
Variation:  To make it challenging, have the students 
skip as the movement will make it harder to balance 
the bean bags.
ICE CREAM CONE RELAY
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Place students in two relay race lines.  Give 
each team a small cone (ice cream cone) and a 
tennis ball (ice cream scoop).  Place two larger cones 
at each end line.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line must run holding
a small cone with a tennis ball balanced on top
(ice cream cone).
•
Holding the cone, and balancing the ball, they
must move as fast as they can to the opposite
end of the room, go around a cone and come
back to the start line.
•
Once at the start line, they hand off the cone
and ball to the next child who follows the same
pattern.
•
If a child drops a ball, they must stop where they
are and pick up the ball, then balance it back on
the cone to continue.
•
The first team to finish wins the race.
Variation: You can use a larger ball to make it more 
challenging.
STANDARDS
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running, sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
National Ice Cream Day

fiches.title: World Jump Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
31
FOCUS
•
25 jump ropes
•
40 hula hoops or poly spots
•
2 cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
World Jump Day is a light-hearted day when millions of people jump at the same time to help 
promote various causes in the world.  In this lesson, we will play some exciting games related 
to jumping.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
JUMP THE CREEK
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the children in one line.  Mark off a 
jumping area (a water creek) by placing 2 jump ropes 
on the ground.  Place extra jumping ropes on the 
side part of the room.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line attempt to 
carefully jump ‘cleanly’ over the 2 jump ropes on 
the floor.
•
Each child in line will attempt the same jump.
•
Each time you finish a round, the teacher will 
spread the jump ropes farther apart to make the 
creek and the jump bigger.
•
Each time a child does not make the jump, they 
must go off to the side and practice their jump 
roping skills.
•
The distance of the creek will keep getting larger 
with each round.
•
The last child left who jumped the farthest wins 
the round.
Variation:  You can create 2 lines where the top kid 
from each group competes in the finals.
PUDDLE JUMP RELAY
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Place the students in 2 relay race lines.  Place 
hula hoops (puddles) all over the floor.  Put a cone at 
each end line.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in each line needs to get to
the opposite end line safely by their cone.
•
The only way to get there is to jump in puddles
(hoops).
•
They must jump and hop from one puddle to the
next.
•
When they get to their cone, they must touch it,
turn around and come back to the start line for
the next child to get their turn.
•
If they jump and miss or touch a hula hoop, that
individual child must start over again from the
start line.
•
The first team to finish wins the round.
Variation: You can substitute poly spots (for puddles) 
on the floor instead of hula hoops.
STANDARDS
•
Performs jumping and landing actions with balance. (S1.E3.K)
World Jump Day

fiches.title: Parent’s Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
32
FOCUS
•
Tennis rackets
•
Inflated balloons
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Parent’s Day is a day when we appreciate and respect our parents.   In this lesson, play some 
fun activities that students can learn and take home to play with their families.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BALLOON TENNIS
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the students out around your room in 
pairs.  Give each student a tennis racket.  Give each 
group a balloon.  For extra safety, mark off ‘play areas’ 
where each group must stay.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the students will begin to hit the balloon
back and forth to each other.
•
For extra safety, each group should stay in their
‘marked’ region so they do not encounter other
groups.
•
Allow each group to see how many times they
can hit the balloon back and forth with their
partner.
•
After a few minutes, have the kids rotate to a new
partner and new playing area.
Variation:  If the skill is too easy for some groups, 
have them try to hit 2 balloons back and forth to each 
other to make it more challenging.
SECRET HOPSCOTCH TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread the students around the room.  Pick 1 
or 2 kids to be the chasers.  Use floor tape to create 2 
hopscotch areas on each side of the room.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the chasers will hop on two feet around
the room with the objective of tagging other
students.
•
The other students must avoid being tagged by
hopping on two feet as well.
•
When a child gets tagged, they must go over to
one of the hopscotch areas and hop through the
pattern back and forth 2 times.
•
Once they do it twice, they may return to
gameplay.
•
After a few minutes, pick new students to
become the chasers.
Variation: You can create safety bases for the kids to 
rest if needed.
STANDARDS
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
•
Strikes an object with a long-handled implement (S1.E25.4)
Parent’s Day

sections.Equipment:
GROUP SIZE
Parent’s Day is a day when we appreciate and respect our parents.   In this lesson, play some
fun activities that students can learn and take home to play with their families.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BALLOON TENNIS
Time: 5-10 minutes

hopscotch areas on each side of the room.

pattern back and forth 2 times.
•

gameplay.
•

become the chasers.

Parent’s Day

fiches.title: National Friendship Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
33
FOCUS
•
Scarves (or similar) to safely
tie the kids’ legs together for
a 3-legged race
•
Hula hoops
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Friendship Day celebrates our friendships with people in our lives whom we care 
about.  In this lesson, we will participate in some games that require a partner!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
TWIN ALPHABET TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Divide the class into pairs.  Use scarves to 
safely tie the partners’ legs together.  Pick one group 
to be the chasers in the game.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the children will begin to run around the
room as the chaser group will try to tag the other
groups.
•
The kids always have to travel with their partners
and make sure that both kids’ inner legs are tied
together.
•
When a group gets tagged, they must stop in
their place and recite the alphabet together from
A to Z.
•
Once completed, they are allowed to come back
into the game.
•
After a few minutes, pick a new group to be the
chasing group.
Variation:  For the older groups, when they get 
tagged, they can recite the alphabet backward. Or 
sing a short song together.
HULA HOOP SHUFFLE
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Put students in groups of 2.  Give each group 
a hula hoop.
Instructions:
•
When ready, each pair of students will hold
hands and place a hula hoop between them.
•
Next, they will attempt to spin the hoop together
between their hands and see how long they can
twirl it.
•
After a few minutes, take a rest period and give
each child a new partner.
Variation: You can put the students in groups of 4 
and place a hula hoop where each student is holding 
hands.  This way, kids have to focus now on spinning 
a hoop on both sides of them.
STANDARDS
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)

fiches.title: International Cat Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
34
FOCUS
•
Bean Bags
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
On International Cat Day, we celebrate everything related to one of our favorite furry 
friends!  In this lesson, we will play some fun PE games that will require you to travel on all 
fours.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
CAT CRAB TAG
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out all over the room.  
Give each child a bean bag as well.  Pick one child to 
be the chaser.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the students will begin to crab walk
around the room.
•
While they are crab walking, they must balance
a bean bag on their belly.  The bean bag is their
energy pack.
•
If the energy pack falls to the ground, they lose
power and must quickly pick it up and place it
back on their belly as they continue to crab walk.
•
The chaser’s objective is to crab walk around the
room and tag other players.
•
If a player gets tagged, they must flip over and
do 5 push-ups, then they are allowed back in the
game.
•
After a few minutes, pick a new chaser.
Variation:  You can allow students to either crab walk 
or bear crawl around the room, whichever is easier for 
them.
CAT CRAWL RELAY
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place students in two traditional relay race 
lines.  Set up a cone for each team mid-way through 
your room.  Mark off with floor tape a start line.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first students in each line must crawl 
on all fours to their cone located mid-way 
through their room.
•
Once they get to their cone, they must crawl 
around it and get back to the start line.
•
Once back at the start line, the next child follows 
the same pattern.
•
The first team to finish wins the round.
STANDARDS
•
Recognizes the use of the body as resistance (e.g., holds body in plank position, animal walks)  for developing
strength. (S3.E3.2a)

fiches.title: National Bowling Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
35
FOCUS
•
Bowling pins
•
Balls
•
Scooters
•
Mats
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
On National Bowling Day we will celebrate the sport of bowling.  In this lesson, we will play 
some exciting games where you will have to knock over bowling pins.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
MAT BOWLING
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place the kids in relay race lines.  Use floor 
tape to create a bowling line.  Place a mat in front of 
10 bowling pins.  Give each team a ball.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line will bowl their ball
at their 10 bowling pins.
•
Their objective is to knock over all 10 pins first
•
But, there is an obstacle, which is rolling the ball
over a floor mat which is blocking their path.
Balls cannot be thrown over the mat.
•
After a kid rolls their ball, they must retrieve it
and bring it back to the next student in line.
•
Each line keeps following this pattern until all the
pins are knocked over first.
•
Once completed, reset the pins and start a new
round.  The first team to 5 wins are the champs.
Variation:  Set the pins up in different and interesting 
patterns.
SCOOTER BOWLING
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Place the students 2 traditional relay race 
lines.  Use floor tape to mark off the start line and 
pushing line.  Set up 10 bowling pins for each team 
on the opposite end.  Give each team a scooter.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in each line will sit on the 
scooter.  The second child in line will push the kid 
on the scooter to the shooting line.
•
Once at the shooting line, the pusher will roll the 
sitting student at their bowling pins attempting 
to knock them all over first.
•
The kid on the scooter in not allowed to move in 
any way.
•
After they go, they must switch spots and roll 
back to the start line as the next 2 students go.
•
The pattern continues until one team knocks
over all the pins first.
•
Once completed, reset the pins and start a new
round.  The first team to 5 wins are the champs.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Throws underhand with opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)
National Bowling Day

MAT BOWLING

fiches.title: National Roller Coaster Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
36
FOCUS
•
Floor Tape
•
Scooters
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Roller Coaster Day is a day when we celebrate the thrill and adventures of 
roller coasters.   In this lesson, we’ll play some fun activities that require you to move around 
the room on a scooter.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
ROLLER COASTER RIDE
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Place the students in traditional relay race 
lines.  Use floor tape to create identical lines on the 
floor to resemble a roller coaster. Give each team a 
scooter. 
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student sits on their team’s
scooter while the second child pushes them on
their roller coaster being careful to follow the line
pattern.
•
Once they get back to start, the child sitting goes
to the end of the line while the child who pushed
now sits on the scooter as the next child line
becomes the new pusher.
•
They follow this pattern until all the kids had a
turn to do both jobs.  The first team to finish wins
the round.
Variation:  Be creative with your roller coaster design. 
Instead of floor tape, you can mark the roller coaster 
path with cones.
FOLLOW ME
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Place the children in a single line.  They must 
put their hands on the shoulders of the person in 
front of them.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child must start moving in a
pattern of their choice trying to resemble a roller
coaster.
•
All the students behind them in line must follow
the same path keeping their hands on the
shoulders of the person in front of them.
•
If the lead person leans right, they must all
lean to the right.  If they lean left, the same rule
applies.  If they put their hands high above their
head, again, they must follow the leader.
•
For more advanced classes, have each child
balance a bean bag on their head performing
this activity.
STANDARDS
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
National Roller Coaster Day

fiches.title: National Cheese Pizza Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
37
FOCUS
•
Foam throwing discs
•
Tennis balls
•
Cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
On National Cheese Pizza Day, we celebrate our love of pizza.  In this lesson, we will participate 
in some games that center around pizza!
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
PIZZA TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Pick one child to be the chaser who starts in 
the middle of the room.  Place the rest of the kids 
on each end line.  Pick some kids to be the ‘cheese’, 
some kids the ‘crust,’ and some kids the ‘sauce’.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the chaser will either call out the word
cheese, crust, or sauce.
•
When this occurs, the children who were
designated this title must run from one end line
safely to the other end line.
•
Any child that gets caught must perform 10
jumping jacks and then return to the game.
•
Then, the chaser calls out another of the 3 words,
and the same pattern continues.
•
After a few minutes, rotate the chaser and give
the rest of the students new titles.
Variation:  Change the locomotor to skipping, 
hopping or crawling. Add an extra chaser.
PIZZA TOSS
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Put students on 2 even teams.  Place 4 cones 
in the middle of your room and place a tennis ball 
on top of each cone.  Place a lot of throwing discs 
around the room.  Each throwing disc is a pizza!
Instructions:
•
Each team must hold a disc (pizza) on their end
line.
•
On ‘go’ the kids will have the objective of
throwing their discs at the tennis balls trying to
knock them off the cone.
•
When they do not have a disc, they are allowed
to leave their end line, pick up a new disc, bring it
back and toss it again.
•
The first team to knock off 2 tennis balls per
round wins.
STANDARDS
•
Demonstrates a mature throwing pattern for a modified target game such as bowling, bocce or horseshoes.
(S1.M18.6)
•
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
National Cheese Pizza Day
Chaser

fiches.title: Read A Book Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
38
FOCUS
•	
Paper
•	
Magic marker
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
September 6th is Read A Book Day.  On this day, we celebrate everything related to reading.  In 
this lesson, we will play some fun PE games that revolve around words and the alphabet.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
WORD SCRABBLE
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Put the kids in traditional relay race lines.  
With a marker, place large letters on every piece of 
paper and place them down all over the floor.
Instructions:
•	
On ‘go’ the first student in line must run to the 
papers and pick only one up.  When they come 
back, they must place it face up so the letter 
shows.
•	
Then, the next child follows the same pattern.
•	
The object is to create a legitimate word using as 
many letters as possible.  
•	
After 5 minutes, the team with the largest word 
wins the round.
•	
Each team has to max out with 7 letters.  This 
means they will have to return paper back to the 
piles face down if they exceed 7 letters.
Variation:  To make it easier, use more common 
letters to help the students form simple words like S, 
N, T as well as many vowels.
PARTNER ALPHABET
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Place students in groups of 2 or 3 and spread 
them out around the room.
Instructions:
•	
Place students in groups of 2 or 3.
•	
Spread them out in the room.
•	
The teacher will call out a letter of the alphabet.
•	
Each group must work together to form that 
specific letter using their bodies.
•	
Once completed, the teacher will yell out ‘rotate’ 
and the kids need to find a new partner.
•	
Once this is accomplished, the teacher will call 
out another letter for the kids to form.
•	
Once again, they rotate when the task is 
completed.
STANDARDS
•	
Works cooperatively with others. (S4.E4.3a)
•	
Recognizes the value of “good health balance.” (S3.E6.2)
Read A Book Day
A D
L S
L
M

fiches.title: Talk Like A Pirate Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
39
FOCUS
•	
Floor tape
•	
Basketballs
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Talk Like A Pirate Day is September 19th.  On this day we will celebrate pirates and the way they 
talk.  In this lesson, we will play some exciting games related to pirates.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
PIRATES GOLD
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the students out around the room.  
Give all the kids a basketball (pirates gold) except for 
about 2 or 3 kids who will not have a basketball. 
Instructions:
•	
On ‘go’ the students with a basketball will dribble 
around the room.
•	
The kids without a basketball are pirates looking 
to steal gold (a basketball).
•	
The pirate will run around the room with the 
objective of stealing a basketball (gold) from 
another student.  If they successfully take the 
gold, they must now dribble the ball around the 
room.  
•	
The child who just had the ball taken from them 
now becomes a new pirate who will attempt to 
take a piece of gold (basketball) from another 
child.
Variation:  Pick more kids to be pirates (without a 
ball) to make the game more challenging. 
WALK THE PLANK
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: If you do not have existing floor lines, use floor 
tape to create lines that travel throughout the room 
(these are the “planks.”) Spread the kids out around 
the room.  Pick one kid to be the chaser.
Instructions:
•	
On ‘go’ the chaser runs after the other students 
with the objective of tagging them.  
•	
All the students (including the chaser) can only 
run on the planks (lines).
•	
If a child gets caught, they must do 10 jumping 
jacks, then they can return to the game.
•	
After a few minutes, pick a new child to be the 
chaser.
STANDARDS
•	
Dribbles and travels in general space at slow to moderate jogging speed, with control of ball and body. (S1.E17.3)
•	
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running, sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)

fiches.title: First Day of Autumn
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
40
FOCUS
•
100-200 bean bags
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Autumn begins on September 23rd.  It is a day when we celebrate the end of Summer and the 
start of Fall.   In this lesson, play some fun activities that revolve around the theme of Autumn.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
PETER, PETER PUMPKIN EATER
Time: 5-10 minutes
Setup: Pick one student to be Peter the pumpkin 
eater.  Then, the rest of the kids will either be a 
pumpkin pie or an apple pie who are waiting on the 
room end lines.  The teacher will tell the kids which 
pie they are for the game.
Instructions:
•
While the chaser (Peter) stand in the middle and 
the rest of the kids are on the end lines, Peter will 
recite, “Peter, Peter pumpkin eater, I want an 
apple pie (or pumpkin pie)."
•
When a pie is called, the children must run from 
one end line to the opposite end line. Peter is 
only allowed to run side to side on the middle 
line, but has the objective of trying to tag kids as 
they run by the middle line
•
If a child gets tagged, they now have to stay on 
the middle line and help Peter chase kids, but 
staying on the middle line as well.  Then, Peter 
will start again and recite his line but calling out a 
different pie.  The pattern continues until all the 
kids are caught.
Variation:  When a student gets tagged, they just 
replace Peter instead of helping him.
COLLECT THE ACORNS
Time: 10-12 minutes
Setup: Spread out dozens of bean bags (acorns) 
around your room.  Children (who are the squirrels) 
can start off the game wherever they would like.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the squirrels must run around the room
and pick up as many acorns as they can.  They
must carry all of them once the collect them.
•
If an acorn falls to the ground, the squirrel is
eliminated and must go to the side of the room.
•
After 1 minute, the teacher will stop the game.
The squirrel with the most acorns wins the round.
•
Once the round is completed, spread the bean
bags back out around the room and begin again.
STANDARDS
•
Runs with a mature pattern. (S1.E2.2a)
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
First Day of Autumn

sections.Equipment:
GROUP SIZE
Autumn begins on September 23rd.  It is a day when we celebrate the end of Summer and the
start of Fall.   In this lesson, play some fun activities that revolve around the theme of Autumn.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
PETER, PETER PUMPKIN EATER
Time: 5-10 minutes

can start off the game wherever they would like.

must carry all of them once the collect them.
•

eliminated and must go to the side of the room.
•

The squirrel with the most acorns wins the round.
•
Once the round is completed, spread the bean
bags back out around the room and begin again.
STANDARDS

Runs with a mature pattern. (S1.E2.2a)
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running,  sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
First Day of Autumn

Time: 10-12 minutes

fiches.title: World Animal Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
41
FOCUS
•
2 tennis balls per child
•
1 large milk crate
•
2 cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
World Animal Day is on October 4th! This is an international day of action for animal 
rights and welfare. In this lesson, we will get our bodies moving by imitating our favorite 
animal friends.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
ANIMAL FARM RELAY
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Set up the kids in 2 lines in a relay race formation.  
Place 2 cones at the far end of your room. Use tape to 
create a start line.  
Instructions:
•
Each race will be conducted as a specific animal
chosen by the teacher.
•
For example, the first race can be a bunny rabbit race
where the kids have to hop back and forth.
•
On ‘go’ the first kid in line hops to the end line and
back. When they get back, the next kid will follow the
same pattern.
•
Variations: slither like a snake, charge like a bull, crab
walk, elephant stomp, turkey trot etc.
Easier: Perform only one animal per race
Harder: Split the race into multiple legs marked by cones. 
Perform a different animal movement for each leg.
SQUIRREL IN THE TREE
Time: 20-30 minutes
Setup: Give each child 2 tennis balls (nuts) and pick 1 or 2 
other students to be the chasers without any tennis balls.  
Place a milk crate outside the play area.  All the students 
are squirrels in this activity.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ all the students need to hop around the room,
including the chasers.
•
If a chaser tags a classmate, they must drop 1 tennis
ball into the milk crate and continue playing.
•
If they get tagged again, they must put the second
ball into the crate, and then go to the sideline until the
next round.
•
Pick a new chaser every few minutes.
Easier: Just have one chaser instead of two.
Harder: Use multiple chasers and play until the last kid is 
left.
STANDARDS
•
Performs jumping and landing actions with balance. (S1.E3.K)
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without hesitation. (S1.E6.3)

fiches.title: Bully Prevention Month
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
42
FOCUS
•
Paper
•
Pencils
•
2 buckets
•
Decks of cards
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
During October, schools and communities nationwide unite together to educate and raise 
awareness of bullying prevention. In this lesson, we will use some fun PE games to get kids 
working together, breaking down social barriers and improving cooperation.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
CARD CASTLE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Split group into small teams of 2-4 kids. Each 
group gets a deck of cards and sets up on a hard floor or 
table to build a card castle.
Instructions:
•
When ready, kids have to team up with a small group
of students and work together to build a card castle.
•
Students should pick someone they do not know very
well to help them communicate with other children
and make new connections to support one another.
Easier: Build a pyramid using stacking cups or plastic 
cups instead of cards.
Harder: Build a castle with at least 4 columns on the 
bottom.
NEW FRIEND BUCKET
Time: 20-30 minutes
Setup: Divide the class into 2 groups.  Give each child a 
piece of paper and a pencil.  On the paper, they need to 
write their name and an exercise.  Once completed, they 
must fold it put the folded paper in their group’s bucket.  
Instructions:
•
The kids from Group 1 will pick a piece of paper out of
the Group 2’s bucket, then approach the kid whose 
name is on the paper.  
•
They should each ask each other an interesting
question like “what’s your favorite movie?” or “what’s
your favorite sport?”
•
Blow your whistle after about 1 minute – the kids must
perform the exercise on the paper for 20 seconds.
•
Once completed, Group 2 will now pick a piece of
paper from Group 1’s bucket. Repeat.
Easier: Have the kids perform the exercise for 10 seconds.
Harder: Have the kids perform the exercise for 30 
seconds.
STANDARDS
•
Reflects on personal social behavior in physical activity. (S4.E2.4)
•
Practices skills with minimal teacher prompting. (S4.E1.2)
Bully Prevention Month

fiches.title: National Sports Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
43
FOCUS
•
2 large sponge soccer balls
•
2 bowling pins
•
2 basketball hoops
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
National Sports Day is a day where the fans, families, and athletes of all the sports 
converge to celebrate their favorite athletic events. In this lesson, we will combine 
elements of baseball, soccer and basketball together.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
HOOP PITCHER
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Put students in 2 lines.  Place a hula hoop against 
the wall.  Secure it with tape or a heavy object.  Give the 
first student in line a ball to throw.  Use tape to create a 
throwing line on the floor.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first student in line will throw the ball with
the objective of getting the ball in the hoop.
•
After they throw it, they must retrieve the ball and
hand it off to the next student in line who follows the
same pattern.
•
The first team to 10 wins that round.
•
Variation: Bowl or pitch the ball underhand.
Easier: Mark off the throwing line closer to the hoop.
Harder: Mark off the throwing line further from the hoop.
SOCCER BASKETBALL
Time: 20-30 minutes
Setup: Divide the class into 2 groups on each end line 
and give each kid a number so they know when their turn 
is called.  Set up a bowling pin at the end of the gym, and 
have a basketball hoop at the other end.
Instructions:
•
When a student’s number is called, they must grab
the sponge ball, run over to a designated basket and
make a basket.
•
If they miss 3 times, it is the same as making a basket.
•
Once the basket is made, they must dribble the ball
soccer style towards their team pin.
•
Their goal is to knock over their pin with the ball
before the other team gets a point.
Easier: Lower the hoop to 8 or 9 feet.
Harder: Have the kids shoot a free throw or three-pointer 
instead of a layup.
STANDARDS
•
Recognizes the types of kicks needed for different games and sports situations. (S2.E5.4c)
•
Performs a sequence of locomotor skills, transitioning from one skill to another smoothly and without hesitation. (S1.
E6.3)
National Sports Day

HOOP PITCHER
Time: 10-15 minutes

fiches.title: Halloween
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
44
FOCUS
•	
1 orange playground ball
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Halloween is October 31st!  It is a fun day for kids where they dress in costumes and collect 
many treats from friends and neighbors. In this lesson, we will have fun acting like zombies and 
playing with pumpkins.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
ZOMBIE TAG
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Spread the students all around your room and 
pick one child to be your chaser who must tag other kids.
Instructions:
•	
On ‘go’ the chaser will begin running around the room 
attempting to tag other children.  
•	
If a child gets tagged, they turn into a zombie and 
must freeze in their spot with their hands over the 
head and their feet spread apart.  
•	
To get free, a child who is not tagged must crawl 
through their legs to all these students to run around 
again.
Easier: Have only one chaser.
Harder: Have multiple chasers.
PUMPKIN ROLL
Time: 20-30 minutes
Setup: Have the students stand in a straight line and pick 
2 kids to be the ball rollers who must stand on the ends of 
the line.  Give one of the rollers an orange ball.
Instructions:
•	
When ready, all the children in the middle will face the 
roller with the orange ball.  
•	
The roller will bowl the ball straight down the line 
towards the other roller trying to hit any of the kids in 
the feet. 
•	
The kids in the line have to jump or lift their feet to 
avoid getting hit.  If they get hit, they are out.  
•	
The last 2 kids left will become the next rollers.
Easier: Add more distance between the roller and the 
first kid in the line. 
Harder: Use a smaller, faster ball.
STANDARDS
•	
Demonstrates a mature throwing pattern for a modified target game such as bowling, bocce or horseshoes. (S1.M18.6)
•	
Leaps using a mature pattern. (S1.E1.3)
Halloween 
Chaser

sections.Equipment:

Easier: Add more distance between the roller and the
first kid in the line.
Harder: Use a smaller, faster ball.

Chaser

fiches.title: Halloween 2
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
45
FOCUS
•
100-200 Bean Bags
•
4 hula hoops
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
October 31st is Halloween.  It is a day when we wear funny or spooky costumes and go out 
trick or treating with our friends.   In this lesson, play some fun activities that revolve around the 
holiday of Halloween.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
TRICK OR TREAT CANDY COLLECT
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Place your class on 4 even teams and give 
each team a hula hoop (their trick or treat bag).  
Spread out 100-200 bean bags (candy) all around 
your room.  Place 4 hula hoops in each corner of the 
room.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ each student will run around the room 
and pick up one bean bag at a time.
•
Once they pick it up, they must run to their hula 
hoop and drop it in the center.  After they drop it, 
they continue to pick up more bean bags one at 
a time.
•
Each team will follow the same pattern until 
there are no more bean bags left.
•
At the end, the team with the most bean bags 
wins the round.
Variation:  Change the locomotor – students must 
hop on one foot, bunny hop, bear crawl or crab walk 
instead of running.
ZOMBIE TAG
Time: 8-10 minutes
Setup: Spread the children out around the room and 
give each child a hula hoop.  Pick one child to be the 
chaser.
Instructions:
•
In this game the children must walk around the
room with one foot in a hula hoop and dragging
that hula hoop around with the one foot inside to
travel (so they walk like zombies).
•
The objective is for the chaser zombie to touch
their hula hoop with another zombie who is not
the chaser.  The non-chasers are avoiding having
their hoops touched.
•
If the chaser succeeds in touching another hula
hoop, the student who just got tagged must
stand in their hoop and do 10 jumps in place to
go back into the game.
•
Students can only travel with one foot in a hula
hoop and dragging the hoop.
•
The game continues for a few minutes, then
switch the chasers.  To make the game more
difficult, you can pick multiple chasers.
STANDARDS
•
Balances on different bases of support, combining levels and shapes. (S1.E7.2a)
•
Hops, gallops, jogs and slides using a mature pattern. (S1.E1.1)
Halloween 2

sections.Equipment:
GROUP SIZE

WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
TRICK OR TREAT CANDY COLLECT
Time: 8-10 minutes

chaser.

In this game the children must walk around the
room with one foot in a hula hoop and dragging
that hula hoop around with the one foot inside to

•

•

switch the chasers.  To make the game more

STANDARDS

sections.Variation:

instead of running.

Time: 8-10 minutes

fiches.title: Veteran’s Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
46
FOCUS
•
Scooters
•
Whiffle balls
•
Large cones
•
Hula hoops
•
Sponge balls
•
Buckets or Crates
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
On Veteran’s Day we honor all the military who served to protect our country. In this 
lesson, we will play fun PE games to that honor veterans and the brave work they did.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BOMB’S AWAY
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Split the class into 2 teams divided by a middle 
line.  Spread out buckets on each half of the room.  
Supply many small sponge balls to use in the game.
Instructions:
•
Each team must remain in their half of the room.
•
On ‘go’ they must start tossing balls onto the other
team’s side of the room with the objective of landing
the ball (bomb) into a bucket.  If a ball goes into a
bucket, it cannot be taken out.
•
Throughout the game, kids pick up the balls and
launch them onto the other team’s half of the room.
•
After 2 minutes, whichever team got more balls into
the other team’s buckets wins that round.
•
Once completed, re-set and start a new round.
Easier: Use more buckets.
Harder: Use fewer buckets.
YANK THE TANK
Time: 20-30 minutes
Setup: Divide the class into 2 groups.  Half the class 
stands in a hula hoop.  The other half will be pushing 
around a cone resting on a scooter with a whiffle ball 
balanced on top (tanks).  Spread sponge balls all around 
your room.  
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the kids in the hoops will try to hit the tanks
(scooter, cone, whiffle ball) and take them over.  They
will safely toss sponge balls with the objective of
knocking off the whiffle ball from the top of the cone.
The tanks will be wheeled all around the room.
•
The kids in the hoops may leave their hoop at any
time to collect new balls but must return to their hoop
to throw them at the tanks.
•
If a child in a hoop does knock off a ball from the top
of the tank, they take over the tank and switch roles
with the child who was steering the tank.
Easier: Use more sponge balls in the activity.
Harder: Use fewer sponge balls in the activity.
STANDARDS
•
Throws underhand with opposite foot forward. (S1.E13.K)
•
Throws overhand, demonstrating 2 of the 5 critical elements of a mature pattern. (S1.E14.2)
Veteran’s Day

sections.Equipment:
GROUP SIZE
On Veteran’s Day we honor all the military who served to protect our country. In this
lesson, we will play fun PE games to that honor veterans and the brave work they did.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
BOMB’S AWAY
Time: 10-15 minutes
sections.Setup:
stands in a hula hoop.  The other half will be pushing
around a cone resting on a scooter with a whiffle ball
balanced on top (tanks).  Spread sponge balls all around
your room.
sections.Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the kids in the hoops will try to hit the tanks
(scooter, cone, whiffle ball) and take them over.  They
will safely toss sponge balls with the objective of
knocking off the whiffle ball from the top of the cone.
The tanks will be wheeled all around the room.
•
The kids in the hoops may leave their hoop at any
time to collect new balls but must return to their hoop
to throw them at the tanks.
•
If a child in a hoop does knock off a ball from the top
of the tank, they take over the tank and switch roles
with the child who was steering the tank.
Easier: Use more sponge balls in the activity.
Harder: Use fewer sponge balls in the activity.
STANDARDS

Veteran’s Day

fiches.title: Election Day
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
47
FOCUS
•
Bean bags for each student
•
2 milk crates
•
4 cones
EQUIPMENT
GROUP SIZE
Election Day is a day where voters get to pick a government official that they think will do 
a great job improving the quality of life of their citizens. In this lesson, we will play fun 
games with a Presidential race and voting.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
RUNNING FOR PRESIDENT - GEORGE VS. ABE
Time: 10-15 minutes
Setup: Assign half the class as George Washington and 
the other half as Abraham Lincoln. Each group starts off 
on their half of the room, divided by a middle line.  Place 
cones on the corners of the room.
Instructions:
•
Each round last 1 minute with the objective of getting
across the room to the other team’s end line without
being tagged.
•
On ‘go’, students can ‘elect’ to run across the room to
the opposite end line.  If they make it safely, they stay
on the line.  If they get tagged on the opposite side
by the opponent, they are out.
•
Whichever team has the most kids safely on the other
team’s side wins the round and election.  Any student
who did not run across before the one-minute expires
does not count.
Easier: Make the game last for 2 minutes.
Harder: Each round can last 45 seconds.
PRESIDENTIAL RACE
Time: 15-20 minutes
Setup: Set up the children in traditional relay race lines.  
Give each child a bean bag (their vote).  Place a milk crate 
or bucket on the opposite side of the room.  Tape a start 
line on the floor.
Instructions:
•
On ‘go’ the first child in line must run to the voter’s
box (milk crate) and cast their vote by tossing their
bean bag into the milk crate.
•
One the bean bag is in the milk crate, they run back
to the start line and the next child follows the same
pattern.
•
The first team to finish wins the round.
Variation: Try different locomotors like hopping, 
galloping, running, sliding, and skipping.
Easier: Shorten the distance to the milk crate.
Harder: Give each kid 2 bean bags and have them go 
twice.
STANDARDS
•
Runs with a mature pattern. (S1.E2.2a)
•
Performs locomotor skills (hopping, galloping, running, sliding, skipping) while maintaining balance. (S1.E1.K)
Election Day

sections.Equipment:
GROUP SIZE

a great job improving the quality of life of their citizens. In this lesson, we will play fun
games with a Presidential race and voting.
WARMUP ACTIVITY
MAIN ACTIVITY
RUNNING FOR PRESIDENT - GEORGE VS. ABE
Time: 10-15 minutes
