fiches.title: INTRODUCTION INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
4
INTRODUCTION
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to Minute to Win It Games. This resource includes 19 station-based games that you can use for 
physical education, class parties, at home, or any time you want to get kids up and moving!
Each game features a simple fitness skill that the kids will try to complete as many times as they can inside 
the 1-minute limit. Each repetition earns a point. The team with the most points wins!
WHAT’S INCLUDED 
•	
19 Printable Game Station Posters
•	
Game Setup Guide
•	
Printable Score Sheets
•	
Printable Award Ribbons
HOW TO PLAY
1.	 Review the Game Setup Guide and decide which games you would like to play. We recommend 
choosing several games and setting up individual stations that the kids can rotate through. But you 
can also choose a single game and have all the kids play at the same time.
2.	 Set up the game stations using the instructions and equipment list from the Game Setup Guide. Print 
out the Game Station Posters you need. Tape each poster to the wall or attach it to a large cone next 
to where the game will be played.
3.	 Split your kids into groups of 2. Give each group a copy of the Score Sheet to track their points.
4.	 Explain to the kids how the games will work and the scoring system you will use. Demonstrate the 
skills involved. They will have 1 minute to score as many points as possible. 
5.	 Divide the kids into their game stations. On your whistle, start a 60 second countdown. One partner 
will play the game and try to score points while the other partner tracks their score.
6.	 At the end of the minute, each group will record their points on the scoresheet. The partners will then 
switch roles and repeat the game station.
7.	 After both kids have completed the game station, rotate the group to a new station and repeat.
8.	 Once all the groups have been through all the game stations, add up their scores and give the print-
able Award Ribbons to the winners.
TIPS
•	
Put on some fun, upbeat music to keep the energy level high for the full 60 seconds. When you turn 
the music on, they start playing, when you turn it off, they stop.
•	
You can also search YouTube for “1 minute countdown with music” and project the video on your wall 
or smart board.
•	
With smaller groups, you can just have kids compete individually instead of partnering up.

fiches.title: CONE FLIPPERS CONE FLIPPERS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
7
CONE FLIPPERS
CONE FLIPPERS
Stand outside the circle of cones. When the clock starts, grab a 
cone and try to gently flip it so it lands on the center cone and 
sticks. Count a point for your team on every successful shot. When 
all the cones have been used, you can retrieve them and keep 
trying until time is up.

fiches.title: BEAN BAGS TOSS BEAN BAGS TOSS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
BEAN BAGS TOSS
BEAN BAGS TOSS
Stand behind the 10 foot line on the ground. When the clock starts, 
grab one bean bag at a time and toss it at the hula hoop with the 
objective of getting as many bags into the hoop as possible. Count 
a point for your team on every successful shot. Each time a bean 
bag misses or touches the hoop, you have to subtract one point.  If 
you run out of bags, you may leave the line to collect more and then 
come to the start line to begin again.

fiches.title: NOODLE DRIBBLE NOODLE DRIBBLE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
9
NOODLE DRIBBLE
NOODLE DRIBBLE
Stand by one cone with a pool noodle in your hand and a sponge 
soccer ball on the ground. When the clock starts, dribble your soccer 
ball with your pool noodle towards the opposite cone.  Once you get 
there, go around it and head towards the other cone.  Keep going 
back and forth for the one minute. Count a point for your team on 
every time you pass a cone.

fiches.title: RING TOSS RING TOSS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 10
RING TOSS
RING TOSS
Partners will stand 10 feet apart from one another. When the clock 
starts, one student will gently toss their hula hoop in the air to their 
partner. That student will attempt to ring toss catch the hoop around 
their noodle.  Once completed, they will toss it back to their partner.  
Count a point for your team on every successful catch.
10 Feet Apart

fiches.title: WALL BALL WALL BALL
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
11
WALL BALL
WALL BALL
Stand a few feet away from a wall. When the clock starts, begin 
hitting your ball to the wall as many times as you can. Count a point 
for your team on every successful shot. You can hit it in the air or on 
a bounce.  Each time you lose the ball, find it and start again adding 
more points to your existing score.
W
A
L
L

fiches.title: HULA JUMP HULA JUMP
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
HULA JUMP
HULA JUMP
When the clock starts, begin jumping in and out of your hula hoop 
while it lays flat on the ground. Count a point for your team on every 
successful jump. If you need to rest, you can begin again picking up 
where you left off on your count.

fiches.title: JUMPING ROPE JUMPING ROPE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
13
JUMPING ROPE
JUMPING ROPE
When the clock starts, begin jumping 
rope as fast as you can using any 
type of jump. Count a point for your 
team on every 
successful jump. If 
the rope stops for 
any reason or you 
need to rest, you 
can begin again 
picking up where 
you left off on your 
count.

fiches.title: CUP STACK CUP STACK
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 14
CUP STACK
CUP STACK
There are 36 cups on the floor. When the clock starts, begin 
stacking them with only 6 cups as your base. You have one minute 
to successfully stack all the cups.  Remember, only 6 cups can be on 
the bottom.  If successful, your team will receive a point for each cup 
that you use.

fiches.title: BUNNY HOP BUNNY HOP
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
15
BUNNY HOP
BUNNY HOP
Place a sponge ball between your thighs. When the clock starts, 
begin walking, bouncing or waddle towards the opposite cone.  
Go around the cone and now travel back towards the first cone.  
Count a point for your team on every successful passing by a 
cone. If the ball drops, you must stop, pick it up and then continue 
to complete the full minute.

fiches.title: TWO BALLOON JUGGLE TWO BALLOON JUGGLE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
TWO BALLOON JUGGLE
TWO BALLOON JUGGLE
Hold the two balloons in your hand. 
When the clock starts, toss them both 
into the air. Your objective is to tap 
both balloons in the air for the full 
minute.  You will get 1 point for every 
time you tap a balloon.

fiches.title: SOCCER BASKETBALL SOCCER BASKETBALL
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
17
SOCCER BASKETBALL
SOCCER BASKETBALL
Stand 10 feet away from your crate at the kicking 
line. When the clock starts, begin to self-kick your 
ball by dropping from your hands to your feet. 
Count a point for your team on every successful 
kick of the ball into the milk crate. After you kick 
you ball, you must retrieve it and kick again for one 
full minute.

fiches.title: TENNIS BALL BALANCE TENNIS BALL BALANCE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
TENNIS BALL BALANCE
TENNIS BALL BALANCE
Stand by one of the cones to start.  Balance a ping pong ball on 
top of your ping pong racket. When the clock starts, begin walking 
around the 2 cones while you balance a ball on top of the racket.  
Count a point for your team on every successful time you pass a 
cone. If the ball falls, you must pick it up from the spot it fell and 
continue.

fiches.title: PUSH-UP HIGH FIVE PUSH-UP HIGH FIVE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
19
PUSH-UP HIGH FIVE
PUSH-UP HIGH FIVE
Have 2 students get into a push-up position facing one another. 
When the clock starts, they must do push-ups in sync. Each time 
they come to the up position, they must high five each other, 
then resume doing push-ups again.  Count a point for your team 
on every successful high five.

fiches.title: BOWLING FOR BOTTLES BOWLING FOR BOTTLES
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 20
BOWLING FOR BOTTLES
BOWLING FOR BOTTLES
Stand up 10 water bottles on the end line and get ready to roll your 
ball from the bowling line. When the clock starts, begin to roll your 
ball at a bottle. After you roll your ball, you must retrieve it and do it 
again.  Count a point for your team on every successful bottle that 
gets knocked over.

fiches.title: BLOWING PING PONG BLOWING PING PONG
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
21
BLOWING PING PONG
BLOWING PING PONG
Stand by one cone with a ping pong ball in front of you on the 
floor. When the clock starts, blow the ping pong ball with your 
mouth towards the opposite cone.  Once you get there, go 
around it and head towards the other cone using your mouth to 
blow the ball to travel.  Keep going back and forth for the one 
minute.  Count a point for your team on every time you pass a 
cone.

fiches.title: ADD THE CARDS ADD THE CARDS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 22
ADD THE CARDS
ADD THE CARDS
Lay out playing cards face down on one side of your room.  Stand 
by the start line. When the clock starts, run as fast as you can to 
the playing cards.  Grab 1 card and run it back to the start line 
and place it down there.  Then, run down to the cards and pick up 
another one. After 1 minute, add up all the cards using the numbers 
on the front of the card.  Whichever team gets the most points 
wins that round.

fiches.title: WHEELBARROW  WHEELBARROW  CHALLENGE CHALLENGE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 23
WHEELBARROW 
WHEELBARROW 
CHALLENGE
CHALLENGE
One person will get into a push-up position while their partner 
holds their feet by the waist area. When the clock starts, the 
partners will have to wheelbarrow around 2 cones.  They can 
switch roles at any time. Count a point for your team on every 
pass of the cones.

fiches.title: JJ CHALLENGE JJ CHALLENGE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 24
JJ CHALLENGE
JJ CHALLENGE
When the clock starts, contestants will perform as many jumping 
jacks as they can in one minute.  You earn one point for every 
successful jumping jack.

fiches.title: CRAB BALLOON KICK CRAB BALLOON KICK
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 25
CRAB BALLOON KICK
CRAB BALLOON KICK
Get into a crab walk position on the floor. When the clock starts, 
have a friend gently toss a balloon in the air by your feet.  Start 
to crab kick the balloon into the air as many times as you can.  If 
it falls to the ground, have your friend toss it again and continue 
counting where you left off. You earn one point for every 
successful balloon kick.

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 26

fiches.title: INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
3
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Welcome to No Equipment Cardio Circuits! This resource is designed to help you 
lead fun, high-energy, and effective cardio workouts that require no equipment at all. 
Whether you’re working with students, athletes, or general fitness groups, these circuits 
offer a structured, engaging way to build endurance, improve heart health, and develop 
lifelong fitness habits through bodyweight movement.
The Importance of Cardio Training
Cardio (or aerobic) training is any type of physical activity that raises the heart rate and 
increases breathing. It plays a key role in keeping the heart, lungs, and circulatory system 
healthy. Regular cardio exercise helps improve endurance, coordination, and energy 
levels while reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
Cardio workouts are especially important for kids and teens, as they support brain 
function, mood regulation, and overall physical development. Activities like jumping, 
skipping, and running improve not only fitness but also confidence, focus, and emotional 
well-being. With no equipment required, these movements are simple, accessible, and 
ideal for school and group settings.
What’s Included?
Inside this resource, you’ll find 12 step-by-step cardio circuits, each designed to promote 
aerobic fitness, coordination, and stamina. Every workout comes with an instructor guide 
that includes:
•	
Exercise descriptions with modifications for different fitness levels
•	
Coaching tips to reinforce technique, posture, and pacing
•	
Suggested work/rest formats to keep sessions structured and efficient
As a bonus, each workout is paired with a printable Workout Journal that students can 
use to reflect on the session, track their progress, and explore fitness-related topics 
through simple research and writing prompts.
These workouts are ideal for PE classes, sports teams, or general fitness programs. 
They’re designed to be flexible, age-appropriate, and engaging—no gym, machines, or 
special setup needed.
Let’s get moving, get sweating, and build lifelong cardio fitness—anytime, anywhere!
INTRODUCTION

fiches.title: CARDIO BLAST 101
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6
6
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
An approachable, high-energy cardio workout designed to elevate your heart rate and improve 
overall endurance using fast-paced movements. Expect quick transitions and non-stop action!
Focus on light, quick footwork during Fast Feet to improve agility and reaction speed.
Keep the core engaged during High Knees and Butt Kicks to prevent excessive swaying and 
maximize control.
Land softly on your feet during Jumping Jacks to reduce impact on the knees and ankles.
COACHING TIPS
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and arms at your 
sides. Jump your feet out while raising your arms overhead, then 
return to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping.  
HARDER:  Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out 
wide.
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up to hip level while pumping your arms.
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate 
lifting your feet off the ground as if running in place but with 
minimal elevation.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed.
BUTT KICKS: Jog in place while kicking your heels toward your 
glutes, engaging the hamstrings and keeping your arms moving.
EASIER: March in place, bringing heels up slowly.
HARDER: Increase speed or add arm swings.
EXERCISES
CARDIO BLAST 101

fiches.body:

15–20 sec
HIGH KNEES  
15–20 sec
BUTT KICKS 
15–20 sec
FAST FEET 
15–20 sec
EMPHASIS
INSTRUCTIONS
Endurance, Speed
•
Perform each exercise for 15–20 seconds. 
•
Complete 2–3 rounds with a 30–60 second rest 
between rounds.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

fiches.title: TOTAL BODY SWEAT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
8
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A high-energy, full-body workout that pushes endurance, coordination, and strength. This dynamic 
circuit keeps your heart rate up while working all major muscle groups.
Control your squat depth in Squat Pulses to keep tension in the muscles for longer.
Keep your elbows tucked at a 45-degree angle during Push-Ups to protect your shoulders.
During Push-ups, focus on keeping the spine neutral and engaging your glutes to avoid sagging 
hips.
COACHING TIPS
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and arms at your 
sides. Jump your feet out while raising your arms overhead, then 
return to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping.  
HARDER:  Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out 
wide.
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. 
Return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Perform smaller steps.
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly 
wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches the ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
SQUAT PULSE: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a squat and perform small, pulsing movements at the bottom of 
the squat.
EASIER: Perform regular squats. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands. 
EXERCISES
TOTAL BODY SWEAT

SQUAT PULSE

8–10 reps.

between rounds.

fiches.title: CORE CARDIO CRUSHER
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 10
10
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout blends high-intensity cardio with core-focused exercises to build endurance and 
strengthen your midsection.
Engage your core in every movement—especially during Side Plank and Hollow Body Hold—to 
maximize core activation.
Use rhythmic breathing during Mountain Climbers to maintain endurance and control.
COACHING TIPS
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee toward 
your chest, then the other, as if running in place.
EASIER: Slow down the pace. 
HARDER: Increase speed or bring knees toward the opposite elbow 
for an oblique twist.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Stand with feet together and jump in 
place while mimicking the motion of turning a jump rope with your 
wrists.
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place.
HARDER: Increase speed or perform double-unders.
HOLLOW BODY HOLD: Lie on your back with your arms extended 
overhead and your legs straight. Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs 
off the ground, creating a hollow shape with your body.
EASIER: Keep knees bent.
HARDER: Lower body closer to the ground without touching it.
SIDE PLANK: Lie on one side with your elbow under your shoulder 
and legs stacked. Lift your hips to form a straight line from head to 
feet and hold.
EASIER: Drop your bottom knee to the ground for support.
HARDER: Raise your top leg while holding.
EXERCISES
CORE CARDIO CRUSHER

fiches.title: ATHLETIC ENDURANCE CHALLENGE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
12
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Inspired by sports training, this circuit enhances agility, power, and speed. Perfect for athletes or 
anyone wanting to move like one!
Focus on smooth landings during Lateral Hops and Tuck Jumps to protect your joints.
Stay low in Lateral Shuffles to keep your movement efficient and powerful.
Engage your core and use arm swings for momentum in Jumping Lunges to maintain control.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
LATERAL HOPS: Stand with feet together. Jump side to side, landing 
softly while maintaining balance.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase jump distance.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and 
one foot back. Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump, 
switching legs in mid-air and landing in a lunge with the opposite 
foot forward.
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without the jump.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom of each lunge before jumping.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your 
knees slightly bent. Shuffle quickly to the side, keeping your body 
low.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a slight squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees 
toward your chest.
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate 
lifting your feet off the ground, as if running in place but with minimal 
elevation.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed.
ATHLETIC ENDURANCE CHALLENGE

6–8 reps. 
•

between rounds.

fiches.title: EXPLOSIVE BURNOUT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 14
14
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A powerful lower-body circuit designed to improve explosive power, agility, and endurance. Perfect 
for building strength and speed.
Use your arms to generate momentum during Broad Jumps and Power Skips to maximize distance 
and height.
Land softly on Squat Jumps and Tuck Jumps to absorb impact and protect your knees.
Engage your core and keep your chest lifted during Plyo Lunges to maintain balance.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
BROAD JUMPS: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Swing your 
arms back and jump forward as far as possible, landing softly.
EASIER: Perform a smaller jump.
HARDER: Increase the jump distance or add a squat at landing.
POWER SKIPS: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Skip forward, 
driving one knee up while pushing off the ground for height.
EASIER: Perform regular skips.
HARDER: Increase the height and distance.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position. Lower your back knee 
toward the ground, then explosively jump, switching legs in mid-air.
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without jumping.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom before jumping.
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a slight squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees 
toward your chest.
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
SQUAT JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into 
a squat, then explode upward, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping.
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
EXPLOSIVE BURNOUT

4–6 reps.

between rounds.

fiches.title: HIIT ‘EM UP
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 16
16
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A high-intensity interval workout designed to torch calories and boost metabolism. Quick bursts of 
movement followed by short rests keep the heart rate high for maximum fat burn.
Keep a strong core during Star Jumps and Mountain Climbers to maintain good posture and 
prevent unnecessary strain.
Focus on explosive movement for Lateral Hops while keeping the landing soft and controlled.
During Plank with Arm Reach, avoid shifting your hips too much—engage your core for stability.
COACHING TIPS
STAR JUMPS: Stand with your feet together. Bend your knees 
slightly, then explode into a jump, spreading your arms and legs 
into a star shape. Land softly and return to the start.
EASIER: Perform a half-jump instead of full extension.
HARDER: Increase speed or jump higher.
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee toward 
your chest, then the other, as if running in place.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase speed or bring knees toward opposite elbow for 
an oblique twist.
PLANK WITH ARM REACH: Start in a forearm plank position. Extend 
one arm forward, hold briefly, then switch arms while keeping your 
hips stable.
EASIER: Perform on your knees.
HARDER: Hold each reach for a longer duration.
BUTT KICKS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jog in place 
while kicking your heels up towards your glutes.
EASIER: March in place, bringing heels up slowly.
HARDER: Increase speed or add arm swings.
EXERCISES
HIIT ‘EM UP

fiches.title: SPEED & AGILITY BOOST
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
18
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A dynamic workout focused on foot speed, coordination, and reaction time, perfect for athletes and 
anyone looking to improve quickness.
Focus on light, quick movements for Toe Taps and Imaginary Jump Rope to improve foot speed.
Maintain balance and control during Lateral Hops to prevent wobbling and maximize efficiency.
Stay on the balls of your feet during Star Jumps to maintain momentum, improve balance, and 
allow for quicker transitions.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
TOE TAPS: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your toes on 
an elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase speed or use a higher surface.
LATERAL HOPS: Stand with feet together. Jump side to side, 
landing softly while maintaining balance.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase jump distance.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Stand with feet together and mimic 
jumping rope, rotating your wrists as if holding a real rope.
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place.
HARDER: Increase speed or perform double-unders.
STAR JUMPS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your 
sides. Jump up, spreading your arms and legs out wide like a star, 
then return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform jumping jacks instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a tuck jump at the end.
LATERAL HIGH KNEES: Run in place, bringing your knees up while 
moving side to side in a controlled motion.
EASIER: March in place with high knees.
HARDER: Increase speed and lateral movement.
SPEED & AGILITY BOOST

fiches.title: LOWER BODY POWER CIRCUIT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 20
20
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Designed to build strong, explosive legs while improving balance and endurance. This 
lower-body-focused cardio session is great for developing power and stamina.
Keep your front knee aligned over your ankle during Lunges to maintain proper form and balance.
Engage your glutes and avoid arching your lower back during Single-Leg Glute Bridge Hold.
Stay low and controlled during Wall Sit to maximize muscle activation.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. Return 
to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Perform smaller steps.
HARDER: Hold weights in each hand.
SINGLE-LEG GLUTE BRIDGE HOLD: Lie on your back with one knee 
bent and the other leg extended. Press through the heel of the 
bent leg and lift your hips towards the ceiling. Hold.
EASIER: Perform a standard glute bridge with both feet.
HARDER: Add a resistance band or hold longer.
WALL SIT: – Stand with your back against a wall and lower into a 
seated position, keeping your thighs parallel to the floor. Hold the 
position.
EASIER: Reduce duration of the hold.
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
BROAD JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend 
your knees and swing your arms backward. Jump forward as far as 
you can, landing softly on your feet.
EASIER: Perform smaller jumps.
HARDER: Increase distance or add a squat at landing.
SQUAT PULSE: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into 
a squat and perform small, pulsing movements at the bottom of the 
squat.
EASIER: Perform regular squats. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands. 
LOWER BODY POWER CIRCUIT

fiches.title: FULL - BODY CARDIO CHALLENGE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 22
22
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout is designed to improve overall endurance while engaging all major muscle groups. 
Expect a mix of strength and cardio moves to keep your heart rate up!
During Squat with Overhead Reach, use your legs to drive the movement upward and avoid relying 
solely on your arms.
Maintain a steady rhythm in Imaginary Jump Rope to improve coordination and foot speed.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
SQUAT WITH OVERHEAD REACH: Stand with your feet 
shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, then as you rise, extend 
your arms overhead as if pressing a weight.
EASIER: Perform the squat without the arm movement.
HARDER: Hold a weight or pause at the bottom of the squat.
PLANK WITH ARM AND LEG LIFT: Start in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Lift one arm and the opposite leg 
simultaneously, hold for a moment, then switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Hold the lifted position longer or add a push-up between 
lifts.
JUMPING JACKS:  Stand with your feet together and your arms at 
your sides. Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms 
overhead, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out 
wide.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Stand with feet together and mimic 
jumping rope, rotating your wrists as if holding a real rope.
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place.
HARDER: Increase speed or perform double-unders.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly 
wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches the ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
FULL-BODY CARDIO CHALLENGE

fiches.title: CARDIO CORE INFERNO
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 24
24
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A hybrid circuit that strengthens the core while keeping the heart rate elevated. Perfect for improving 
endurance and core stability at the same time.
Focus on slow, controlled movements for Plank with Shoulder Taps to prevent unnecessary swaying.
Keep knees high during Lateral High Knees while focusing on controlled lateral movement.
Engage your obliques during Side Plank and try to hold a straight, aligned body position.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your toes 
on an elevated surface, alternating feet. 
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead. 
HARDER: Increase speed or use a higher surface.
LATERAL HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in 
place, bringing your knees up towards your chest while moving 
laterally.
EASIER: March in place with high knees.  
HARDER: Increase speed and lateral movement.
SIDE PLANK: Lie on one side with your elbow under your shoulder 
and legs stacked. Lift your hips to form a straight line from head to 
feet and hold.
EASIER: Drop your bottom knee to the ground for support.
HARDER: Raise your top leg while holding.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Begin in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite 
hand, alternating sides while keeping your body stable. 
EASIER: Perform on knees.  
HARDER: Increase speed or add a push-up between taps.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and 
one foot back. Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump, 
switching legs in mid-air and landing in a lunge with the opposite 
foot forward.
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without the jump.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom of each lunge before jumping.
CARDIO CORE INFERNO

fiches.title: AGILITY & STRENGTH FINISHER
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 26
26
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A well-rounded workout blending agility drills and strength exercises to build balance, coordination, 
and endurance.
Keep movements light and quick during Forward & Backward Hops to improve agility and coordination.
Stay low and move rapidly during Fast Feet to develop quick reflexes.
Engage your core during Wall Sit to maintain a stable and strong position.
COACHING TIPS
EXERCISES
FORWARD & BACKWARD HOPS: Stand with feet hip-width apart. 
Hop forward a few inches, then hop back. 
EASIER: Step forward and back instead of hopping. 
HARDER: Increase speed and distance.
SQUAT WITH OVERHEAD REACH: Stand with your feet 
shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, then as you rise, extend 
your arms overhead as if pressing a weight.
EASIER: Perform the squat without the arm movement. 
HARDER: Hold a weight or pause at the bottom of the squat.
WALL SIT: Stand with your back against a wall and lower into a 
seated position, keeping your thighs parallel to the floor. Hold the 
position.
EASIER: Reduce duration of the hold. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
BUTT KICKS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jog in place 
while kicking your heels up towards your glutes. 
EASIER: March in place, bringing heels up slowly. 
HARDER: Increase speed or add arm swings.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate 
lifting your feet off the ground, as if running in place but with minimal 
elevation.
EASIER: Slow down the pace. 
HARDER: Increase the speed.
AGILITY & STRENGTH FINISHER

fiches.title: AGILITY & STRENGTH FINISHER
fiches.body:
DIFFICULTY
FORWARD & BACKWARD 
HOPS 
40–45 sec
FAST FEET 
40–45 sec
WALL SIT 
35–40 sec
SQUAT WITH OVERHEAD 
REACH
40–45 sec
EMPHASIS
INSTRUCTIONS
Agility, Strength
•
Perform each exercise for 40–45 seconds. 
•
Complete 4–5 rounds with 20–30 seconds of 
rest between rounds.
BUTT KICKS 
40–45 sec

fiches.title: SPRINT & POWER CIRCUIT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 28
28
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
A combination of sprint drills and power exercises to enhance explosive strength, endurance, and 
athletic performance.
Pump your arms and drive your knees high during Sprints to maximize speed and efficiency.
Focus on balance and control while doing Single-Leg Hops—avoid wobbling by keeping your core 
engaged.
Land softly on Jump Squats to protect your knees and maintain explosive power.
COACHING TIPS
SINGLE-LEG HOPS: Stand on one foot and hop forward, then back 
to the start. Switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform small hops in place.   
HARDER: Increase distance and height of hops.
LATERAL LUNGES: Stand with your feet wider than hip-width. Shift 
your weight to one side, bending the knee while keeping the other 
leg straight.
EASIER: Perform smaller side steps instead of deep lunges.
HARDER: Add weight or increase the depth of the lunge.
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a squat, then explosively jump up, reaching your arms 
overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
PLANK WITH ARM AND LEG LIFT: Start in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Lift one arm and the opposite leg 
simultaneously, hold for a moment, then switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.  
HARDER: Hold the lifted position longer or add a push-up between 
lifts.
EXERCISES
SPRINT & POWER CIRCUIT

fiches.title: SPRINT & POWER CIRCUIT
fiches.body:
DIFFICULTY
SINGLE-LEG HOPS  
40–45 sec
LATERAL LUNGES 
8–10 per leg
JUMP SQUATS 
10–12 reps
PLANK WITH ARM AND 
LEG LIFT  
35–40 sec
EMPHASIS
INSTRUCTIONS
Speed, Strength
•
Perform each exercise for 40–45 seconds or 
8–12 reps. 
•
Complete 4–5 rounds with 20–30 seconds of 
rest between rounds.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 29
SPRINT & POWER CIRCUIT

fiches.title: © AmericanCoachingAcademy.com  36
fiches.body: © AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 36

fiches.title: EASIER:  Hold onto a wall or chair for support.  HARDER:  Close your eyes or perform calf raises.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 37
BALANCE ON TOES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Raise your heels off 
the ground and balance on your toes. Hold the position, engaging your core to 
maintain stability. 
EASIER: Hold onto a wall or chair for support. 
HARDER: Close your eyes or perform calf raises.
BEAR CRAWLS: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Lift your knees a few inches off the ground. Move 
forward by simultaneously moving your right hand and left foot, then your left 
hand and right foot.
EASIER: Keep your knees on the ground.  
HARDER: Increase the speed or crawl backward.
BEAR CRAWL HOLD: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders 
and knees under your hips. Lift your knees a few inches off the ground and hold 
the position. 
EASIER: Keep your knees on the ground. 
HARDER: Increase the duration of the hold or add arm and leg movements.
BIRD DOGS: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and 
your knees under your hips. Extend your right arm forward and your left leg 
backward simultaneously. Hold for a moment, then return to the starting position 
and switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise without extending your arm.
HARDER: Add a crunch under your body.
BOXER SQUAT PUNCH: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat position while keeping your fists up in front of your face, as if ready to 
box. As you rise from the squat, punch forward with one arm, alternating arms 
with each punch. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Add light weights to your hands.
BROAD JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees 
and swing your arms backward. Jump forward as far as you can, landing softly 
on your feet. 
EASIER: Perform a smaller jump.
HARDER: Increase the distance of your jumps or add a squat at the landing.
BUTT KICKS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jog in place, bringing your 
heels up to touch your glutes with each step. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise at a walking pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
BURPEES: Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, place your hands on the ground, and jump your feet back into a plank 
position. Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back towards your hands, and 
explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead. 
EASIER: Skip the push-up.
HARDER: Add a tuck jump at the end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 37

fiches.title: EASIER:  Sit on a cushion for support.   HARDER:  Lean forward to deepen the stretch.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 38
BUTTERFLY STRETCH: Sit on the floor with your feet together and your knees 
bent out to the sides. Hold your feet with your hands and gently press your 
knees towards the ground. 
EASIER: Sit on a cushion for support.  
HARDER: Lean forward to deepen the stretch.
CAT-COW STRETCH: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders 
and knees under your hips. Inhale, arch your back, and look up (Cow Pose). 
Exhale, round your back, and tuck your chin to your chest (Cat Pose). 
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.  
HARDER: Hold each position for a longer duration.
CHILD'S POSE: Kneel on the floor with your big toes touching and knees spread 
apart. Sit back on your heels and extend your arms forward, lowering your torso 
between your thighs. 
EASIER: Keep your knees together. 
HARDER: Reach your arms further forward for a deeper stretch.
CLOSE-GRIP PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands closer together, directly under your shoulders. Lower your body until your 
chest nearly touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
COBRA POSE: Lie face-down on the floor with your hands under your 
shoulders and your elbows close to your body. Press into your hands to lift 
your chest off the floor, keeping your hips grounded. 
EASIER: Lift only to a comfortable height. 
HARDER: Extend your arms fully to increase the stretch.
CROSS JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms extended out to the 
sides. Jump and cross your legs and arms in front of your body, then jump back 
to the starting position.
EASIER: Step instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the speed of the movements.
DIAMOND PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index 
fingers. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches your hands, then push 
back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
DECLINE PUSH-UPS: Start in a push-up position with your feet elevated on a 
bench or step. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the ground, then 
push back up.
EASIER: Lower the height of the elevation. 
HARDER: Increase the height of the elevation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 38

fiches.title: EASIER:  Keep your knees slightly bent.  HARDER:  Push your heels down to the floor for a deeper stretch.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 39
DOWNWARD DOG: Start on all fours, with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Lift your hips towards the ceiling, straightening your legs 
and forming an inverted V shape with your body. 
EASIER: Keep your knees slightly bent. 
HARDER: Push your heels down to the floor for a deeper stretch.
EAGLE POSE: Stand with your feet together. Bend your knees slightly and lift 
one leg, wrapping it around the other. Cross your arms in front of your body and 
try to wrap your palms together. 
EASIER: Use a wall for support. 
HARDER: Hold the position longer or lower your hips further.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate lifting your 
feet off the ground, as if running in place but with minimal elevation. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace. 
HARDER: Increase the speed.
FIGURE FOUR STRETCH: Sit or lie on the floor with your legs extended. 
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee, forming a figure four shape. Lean 
forward slightly to increase the stretch.
EASIER: Keep your back straight. 
HARDER: Pull your crossed leg closer to your body.
FIRE HYDRANT: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Keeping your knee bent, lift one leg out to the side, 
mimicking the motion of a dog at a fire hydrant. Lower your leg back down and 
repeat.
EASIER: Reduce the height of the lift. 
HARDER: Add a resistance band around your thighs.
FORWARD AND BACKWARD HOPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jump 
forward a small distance, then jump backward to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform small hops. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each hop.
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, bringing your 
knees up towards your chest as high as possible with each step. 
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height. 
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
GLUTE BRIDGES: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the 
ground. Press into your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling, then lower back 
down.
EASIER: Perform the exercise with a smaller range of motion. 
HARDER: Hold the lifted position for a longer duration or add weight to your hips.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 39

fiches.title: EASIER:  Keep the stretch gentle.  HARDER:  Increase the depth of the stretch or lift your back knee off the ground.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 40
HIP FLEXOR STRETCH: Kneel on one knee with the other foot in front of you, 
creating a 90-degree angle with both legs. Shift your weight forward, feeling a 
stretch in your hip flexor. 
EASIER: Keep the stretch gentle. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the stretch or lift your back knee off the ground.
HOLLOW BODY HOLD: Lie on your back with your arms extended overhead and 
your legs straight. Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs off the ground, creating a 
hollow shape with your body. 
EASIER: Keep your knees bent. 
HARDER: Lower your body closer to the ground without touching it.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Mimic the motion of jumping rope, rotating your wrists 
as if holding a rope and jumping in place. 
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or perform double-unders.
ISOMETRIC PUSH-UP HOLD: Start in a standard push-up position. Lower your 
body halfway down and hold the position. 
EASIER: Perform the hold on your knees.
HARDER: Hold for a longer duration or add pulses.
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, 
then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead. 
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your sides. 
Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms overhead, then jump back 
to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out wide.
LATERAL LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to the side, 
lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other leg straight. Return to the 
starting position and switch sides. 
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the lunge.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
LATERAL HOPS: Stand with your feet together. Jump to the side, landing softly 
on both feet, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 40

fiches.title: EASIER:  Reduce the duration of the hold.   HARDER:  Hold a weight in your hands.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 41
LATERAL LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to the 
side, lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other leg straight. Hold 
the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the duration of the hold.  
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
LATERAL HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up towards your chest while moving laterally. 
EASIER: March in place with high knees.  
HARDER: Increase the speed and distance of lateral movement.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your knees 
slightly bent. Shuffle quickly to the side, keeping your body low. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace.  
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and lower your 
hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. Return to the starting 
position and switch sides.  
EASIER: Perform smaller steps. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and lower 
into a lunge, holding the position. 
EASIER: Shorten the duration of the hold. 
HARDER: Hold weights in each hand.
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands under your 
shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee towards your chest, then the 
other, as if running in place. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace.  
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a twist by bringing your knee towards the 
opposite elbow.
PIKE PUSH-UPS: Start in a downward dog position with your hips raised and 
your hands shoulder-width apart. Bend your elbows to lower your head towards 
the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups with your knees slightly bent.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
NECK ROTATIONS: Stand or sit comfortably with your back straight. Slowly 
rotate your head in a circular motion, first clockwise, then counterclockwise. 
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.  
HARDER: Increase the duration of each rotation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 41

fiches.title: EASIER:  Perform the plank on your knees.  HARDER:  Lift one leg or one arm off the ground.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 42
PLANK: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows under your shoulders 
and your body in a straight line. Hold the position, engaging your core.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift one leg or one arm off the ground.
PLANK WITH ARM AND LEG LIFT: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Lift one arm and the opposite leg simultaneously, hold for 
a moment, then switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Hold the lifted position longer or add a push-up between lifts.
PLANK WITH LEG LIFTS: Start in a plank position with your hands or elbows 
under your shoulders. Lift one leg off the ground, hold for a moment, then 
switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift both legs simultaneously or add a push-up between lifts.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Start in a plank position with your hands under 
your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand, alternating sides while 
keeping your body stable.  
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a push-up between taps.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and one foot back. 
Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump, switching legs in mid-air and 
landing in a lunge with the opposite foot forward. 
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without the jump.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom of each lunge before jumping.
PLYO PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position. Lower your body to the 
ground, then explosively push up, lifting your hands off the ground.  
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.  
HARDER: Clap your hands together at the top of each push-up.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than 
shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the 
ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
PLYO TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands 
gripping the edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower your body by bending 
your elbows. Explosively push up, lifting your hands off the bench momentarily. 
EASIER: Perform regular triceps dips without the jump. 
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 42

fiches.title: EASIER:  Perform the push-ups on your knees.  HARDER:  Add a weight in your lifting hand.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 43
PUSH-UP WITH ROTATION: Start in a high plank position with your hands 
slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body into a push-up, then 
rotate your body to one side, lifting one arm towards the ceiling. Return to the 
starting position and repeat on the other side.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Add a weight in your lifting hand.
POWER SKIPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Skip forward, driving your 
knees high and using your arms to propel yourself upwards.  
EASIER: Perform regular skips. 
HARDER: Increase the height and distance of each skip.
REVERSE PLANK: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands 
placed behind you, fingers pointing towards your feet. Lift your hips off the 
ground, forming a straight line from your head to your heels. 
EASIER: Bend your knees and keep your feet flat on the ground. 
HARDER: Lift one leg off the ground.
SIDE PLANK: Lie on your side with your elbow under your shoulder and your legs 
stacked. Lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your head to 
your feet.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift your top leg off the ground.
SINGLE-LEG BALANCE: Stand on one leg, lifting the other foot off the ground. 
Hold the position, engaging your core to maintain balance. 
EASIER: Use a wall or chair for support. 
HARDER: Close your eyes or move your lifted leg in different directions.
SINGLE-LEG HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Hop forward, 
backward, or side to side on the same leg, landing softly with control. Repeat the 
hops in a consistent rhythm.  
EASIER: Perform smaller hops or use both legs. 
HARDER: Hop in different directions each time.
SINGLE-LEG ROMANIAN DEADLIFT: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in your 
knee. Hinge at the hips, lowering your torso while extending the opposite leg 
backward. Return to the starting position.  
EASIER: Use a wall or chair for balance. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SINGLE-LEG GLUTE BRIDGE HOLD: Lie on your back with your knees bent and 
feet flat on the ground. Lift one leg off the ground and extend it straight. Press 
into your supporting foot to lift your hips towards the ceiling and hold the 
position.
EASIER: Perform a regular glute bridge with both feet on the ground. 
HARDER: Add pulses at the top of the hold.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 43

fiches.title: EASIER:  Hold onto a wall or chair for balance. HARDER:  Hold a weight in your hands.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 44
SINGLE-LEG SQUAT: Stand on one leg with your other leg extended in front of 
you. Lower your body into a squat, keeping your extended leg off the ground, 
then return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Hold onto a wall or chair for balance.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SKATER HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Jump laterally to 
the opposite leg, landing softly and balancing before jumping back. 
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
SQUAT: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your hips back and 
down as if sitting into a chair, keeping your chest up and knees over your toes. 
Return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat.  
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SQUAT WITH OVERHEAD REACH: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. 
Lower your hips back and down into a squat while reaching your arms overhead. 
Return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SQUAT JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, 
then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
SQUAT PULSE: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat 
and perform small, pulsing movements at the bottom of the squat.  
EASIER: Perform regular squats. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
STAR JUMPS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your sides. Jump 
up, spreading your arms and legs out wide like a star, then return to the starting 
position. 
EASIER: Perform jumping jacks instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a tuck jump at the end.
STANDING HAMSTRING STRETCH: Stand with your feet together. Extend one 
leg forward, keeping it straight, and hinge at the hips to reach towards your toes.
EASIER: Bend your knees slightly.
HARDER: Flex your foot to increase the stretch.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 44

fiches.title: STATIC LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and  lower into a lunge, holding the position.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 45
STATIC LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower into a lunge, holding the position. 
EASIER: Shorten the duration of the hold.
HARDER: Hold weights in each hand.
STATIC SQUAT HOLD: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat and hold the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
SUMO SQUATS: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your 
toes turned out. Lower into a squat, keeping your chest up and knees tracking 
over your toes, then return to the starting position.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat.   
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your toes on an 
elevated surface, alternating feet. 
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
TOUCH AND HOP: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat 
and reach down to touch the ground with one hand. As you rise, hop on the 
opposite leg, lifting your other knee up towards your chest. Repeat on the other 
side. 
EASIER: Perform the movement without the hop, just standing up instead.  
HARDER: Increase the speed and height of the hop.
TREE POSE: Stand on one leg with your other foot placed against your inner 
thigh or calf. Bring your hands together at your chest or overhead, holding the 
position. 
EASIER: Use a wall for support.  
HARDER: Close your eyes or reach your arms higher.
TRICEPS STRETCH: Stand or sit with your back straight. Reach one arm 
overhead and bend your elbow, bringing your hand down your back. Use your 
other hand to gently press on your bent elbow, increasing the stretch. 
EASIER: Reduce the pressure on your elbow. 
HARDER: Reach further down your back.
TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands gripping the 
edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower your body by bending your elbows, 
then push back up. 
EASIER: Keep your feet flat on the ground. 
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 45

fiches.title: EASIER:  Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees. HARDER:  Increase the height of your jump.
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 46
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a slight 
squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees towards your chest. 
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
WALKING LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step forward with one foot 
and lower your hips into a lunge. Push off the back foot to bring it forward and 
repeat the lunge on the other side. 
EASIER: Perform stationary lunges. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
WALL SIT: Stand with your back against a wall and your feet shoulder-width 
apart. Slide down the wall until your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle, 
holding the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the duration of the hold.   
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
WARRIOR POSE (WARRIOR I): Stand with your feet wide apart. Turn one foot 
out and bend your knee to form a 90-degree angle. Extend your arms overhead, 
keeping your hips squared towards the front. Hold the position, engaging your 
core and focusing on your balance. 
EASIER: Shorten your stance. 
HARDER: Deepen the bend in your front knee.
WIDE GRIP PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly 
touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 46

fiches.title: INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
3
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Staying active doesn’t have to require expensive equipment or a gym membership. 
Our No Equipment Exercise Posters provide a comprehensive collection of 77 effective 
bodyweight exercises that can be performed anywhere—whether at home, in a classroom, 
in the gym, or outdoors.
Each poster features a single exercise with clear, concise instructions on how to perform 
it correctly. Many exercises also include modifications for different fitness levels, offering 
easier and harder variations to accommodate all abilities. 
The collection includes a mix of strength, power, flexibility, balance, and endurance 
exercises, ensuring a well-rounded approach to fitness.
These posters are designed to be visual, accessible, and versatile, making them ideal for:
•	
PE teachers who want to create engaging movement stations in their classes
•	
Fitness instructors looking for structured, equipment-free workout options
•	
Fitness enthusiasts who want to build a personal exercise library for at-home or 
travel workouts
Here are some ways you can use these posters:
•	
Create Exercise Stations: Attach posters to cones, walls, or whiteboards to designate 
different movement stations around the gym or classroom. Students can rotate 
through each station for a dynamic workout.
•	
Warm-Up & Cool-Down Routines: Use posters featuring flexibility and mobility 
exercises to guide warm-ups and cool-downs before and after activities.
•	
Self-Paced Challenges: Allow students to choose their own exercise from a set of 
posters and track their progress over time.
•	
Circuit Training Workouts: Combine multiple posters to build a structured, full-body 
workout.
•	
Group Fitness Classes: Assign different exercises to participants and have them 
switch stations at timed intervals.
•	
At-Home Workouts: Pick 5–10 posters and create your own daily workout routine 
with no need for extra equipment.
Whether you’re leading a structured class or simply looking for easy-to-follow fitness 
guidance, these posters provide everything you need for an engaging, effective, and 
equipment-free fitness experience.
INTRODUCTION

fiches.title: BALANCE ON TOES
fiches.body:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart. 
Raise your heels off the ground and balance on your toes. 
Hold the position, engaging your core to maintain stability.
INSTRUCTIONS
EASIER
HARDER
Hold onto a wall or chair 
for support.  
Close your eyes or 
perform calf raises. 
BALANCE

fiches.title: BEAR CRAWLS
fiches.body:
Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders 
and knees under your hips. 
Lift your knees a few inches off the ground. 
Move forward by simultaneously moving your right hand 
and left foot, then your left hand and right foot.
INSTRUCTIONS
EASIER:
HARDER
Keep your knees on the 
ground.  
Increase the speed or 
crawl backward. 
ENDURANCE

fiches.body:

Extend your right arm forward and your left leg backward

Hold for a moment, then return to the starting position and 
switch sides. 
INSTRUCTIONS

HARDER
Perform the exercise 
without extending your 
arm.  
Add a crunch under 
your body.

fiches.body:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. 
Lower into a squat position while keeping your fists up in 
front of your face, as if ready to box. 
As you rise from the squat, punch forward with one arm, 
alternating arms with each punch. 
INSTRUCTIONS
EASIER:
HARDER
Perform a partial squat.   
Add light weights to 
your hands.

fiches.body:
Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat, place your hands on the ground, and 
jump your feet back into a plank position. 
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back towards 
your hands, and explosively jump up, reaching your arms 
overhead.
INSTRUCTIONS
EASIER
HARDER
Skip the push-up. 
Add a tuck jump at the 
end.
ENDURANCE

fiches.title: FAST FEET
fiches.body:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart. 
Quickly alternate lifting your feet off the ground, as if 
running in place but with minimal elevation.

HARDER
Slow down the pace. 
Increase the speed.

Lean forward slightly to increase the stretch.

HARDER

shoulders and knees under your hips. 
Keeping your knee bent, lift one leg out to the side,

Lower your leg back down and repeat.

HARDER

the lift.

Jump forward a small distance, then jump backward

INSTRUCTIONS
EASIER
HARDER
Perform small hops.  
Increase the distance of 
each hop.

fiches.body:
Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat

Press into your heels to lift your hips towards the

HARDER
Perform the exercise with

fiches.body:

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

Run in place, bringing your knees up towards your 
chest as high as possible with each step.

HARDER

fiches.body:
Kneel on one knee with the other foot in front of you, 
creating a 90-degree angle with both legs. 
Shift your weight forward, feeling a stretch in your hip

HARDER

HARDER

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

INSTRUCTIONS

HARDER

HARDER

fiches.body:

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

Lower into a squat, then explosively jump up, reaching

INSTRUCTIONS

HARDER

without jumping.    
Increase the depth of the 
squat or add weights.

sides. 
Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms

HARDER

feet are out wide.

HARDER

sections.Instructions:

fiches.body:
Stand with your feet together. 
Step one foot to the side, lowering your hips into a

EASIER
HARDER

of jumping.   
Increase the distance of 
each jump.

Step one foot to the side, lowering your hips into a

HARDER

sections.Instructions:

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and 
one foot back. 
Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump,

HARDER

HARDER

fiches.body:

Lift one leg off the ground and extend it straight. 
Press into your supporting foot to lift your hips 
INSTRUCTIONS

sections.Instructions:

fiches.title: INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
3
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
Welcome to No Equipment HIIT Circuits! This resource is designed to bring high-energy, 
effective, and versatile workouts to your students without the need for any equipment. 
Whether you’re leading a gym class, working out at home, coaching a fitness program, 
or enhancing physical activity in any setting, these circuits are tailored to deliver results 
while keeping workouts accessible and engaging.
What is HIIT?
HIIT, or High-Intensity Interval Training, is a workout format that alternates between short 
bursts of intense activity and periods of rest or lower-intensity movement. This approach 
maximizes calorie burn, builds endurance, and improves cardiovascular health—all in a 
time-efficient manner. HIIT workouts are perfect for boosting fitness levels, as they are 
adaptable to all skill levels and can be completed in minimal time.
What’s Included?
This resource features 12 unique HIIT circuits, each with its own focus, structure, and 
intensity level. Alongside these circuits, you’ll find instructor guides that provide clear 
instructions, exercise descriptions (with easier and harder modifications), and coaching 
tips to ensure every session runs smoothly.
Additionally, this resource includes a Workout Journal with prompts for reflection and 
learning. These sheets encourage students to think critically about their performance, 
set goals, and research fitness concepts, promoting both physical and mental growth.
How to Use This Resource
1.
Choose the Right Circuit: Select a workout that aligns with your students’ fitness
levels and the goals for your session. Circuits vary in focus, from cardio and strength
to speed and recovery.
2. Prepare the Space: Since no equipment is needed, ensure a safe and open area for
movement.
3. Follow the Instructor Guide: Use the descriptions and coaching tips to lead the
workout confidently, offering modifications as needed.
4. Encourage Reflection: Print and distribute the Workout Journal sheets after each
session. They provide students with an opportunity to reflect on their performance,
learn about fitness concepts, and set goals.
With No Equipment HIIT Circuits, you’re equipped to deliver engaging, effective, and 
purposeful fitness sessions that inspire progress and build confidence. Let’s get moving!
INTRODUCTION

WORKOUTS

fiches.title: DYNAMIC WARM - UP
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6
6
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This warm-up circuit is designed to prepare the body for exercise by gradually increasing heart rate, 
loosening up muscles, and activating key movement patterns. The exercises are dynamic and 
engaging, making it a perfect start to any workout while reducing the risk of injury.
Focus on smooth, steady movements to gradually elevate the heart rate and increase mobility.
Encourage participants to work through their full range of motion for maximum benefit.
Remind participants to stay relaxed and breathe naturally during the warm-up.
COACHING TIPS
CAT-COW STRETCH: Start on all fours with your hands under your 
shoulders and knees under your hips. Inhale, arch your back, and 
look up (Cow Pose). Exhale, round your back, and tuck your chin to 
your chest (Cat Pose). 
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.  
HARDER: Hold each position for a longer duration.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Mimic the motion of jumping rope, 
rotating your wrists as if holding a rope and jumping in place. 
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or perform double-unders.
FIRE HYDRANT: Start on all fours with your hands under your 
shoulders and knees under your hips. Keeping your knee bent, lift 
one leg out to the side, mimicking the motion of a dog at a fire 
hydrant. Lower your leg back down and repeat.
EASIER: Reduce the height of the lift. 
HARDER: Add a resistance band around your thighs.
LATERAL LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to 
the side, lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other 
leg straight. Return to the starting position and switch sides. 
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the lunge.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
EXERCISES
DYNAMIC WARM-UP

fiches.title: HIIT FOUNDATION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
8
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This beginner-friendly pyramid workout combines simple strength and cardio exercises to build 
endurance and overall fitness. With only three exercises and increasing/decreasing time intervals, it’s 
an accessible introduction to pyramid-style training that promotes steady effort and proper 
technique.
Focus on maintaining consistent form, even as fatigue sets in during the longer intervals.
Encourage participants to work at their own pace while gradually increasing intensity.
Remind participants to engage their core during each exercise for better stability and control.
COACHING TIPS
SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your 
hips back and down as if sitting into a chair, keeping your chest up 
and knees over your toes. Return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at 
your sides. Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms 
overhead, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out 
wide.
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up towards your chest as high as possible with 
each step.
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height. 
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
EXERCISES
HIIT FOUNDATION

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

HIIT FOUNDATION

fiches.title: BODYWEIGHT BLITZ
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 10
10
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout focuses on building strength and control using bodyweight exercises. Participants will 
target both upper and lower body muscles, improving endurance and overall stability. With fewer 
exercises, this circuit allows for more focused effort and form corrections.
Emphasize slow and controlled movements to maximize muscle engagement and improve strength.
Encourage participants to breathe steadily, exhaling during effort and inhaling during recovery 
phases.
Highlight proper alignment during each exercise to prevent strain and ensure effectiveness.
COACHING TIPS
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly 
wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches the ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.  
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. 
Return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Perform smaller steps.
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Start in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite 
hand, alternating sides while keeping your body stable.
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a push-up between taps.
SUMO SQUATS: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width 
apart and your toes turned out. Lower into a squat, keeping your 
chest up and knees tracking over your toes, then return to the 
starting position.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
EXERCISES
BODYWEIGHT BLITZ

fiches.title: CARDIO INFERNO
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
12
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout is all about building your endurance and improving how fast and agile you can move. It 
uses quick and explosive exercises to get your heart pumping and your whole body working. The 
goal is to push yourself while staying in control, and it's easy to adjust for different fitness levels by 
using the modifications.
Remind participants to stay in control and focus on good form, even when moving fast.
Encourage soft landings during jumps to protect their knees and joints.
Keep the energy high and help participants stay motivated by counting reps or giving timing cues.
COACHING TIPS
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up towards your chest as high as possible with 
each step.
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height.  
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
BURPEES: Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart. 
Lower into a squat, place your hands on the ground, and jump your 
feet back into a plank position. Perform a push-up, then jump your 
feet back towards your hands, and explosively jump up, reaching 
your arms overhead.
EASIER: Skip the push-up.
HARDER: Add a tuck jump at the end.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your 
toes on an elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
STAR JUMPS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your 
sides. Jump up, spreading your arms and legs out wide like a star, 
then return to the starting position.
EASIER: Perform jumping jacks instead.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a tuck jump at the end.
EXERCISES
CARDIO INFERNO

BETWEEN SETS
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

CARDIO INFERNO

fiches.title: ENDURANCE EVOLUTION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 14
14
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout is built to push cardiovascular fitness through high-energy, dynamic movements. The 
combination of jumping, skipping, and continuous motion will challenge stamina while keeping 
participants engaged. It's perfect for boosting endurance and burning calories.
Encourage participants to maintain a steady pace throughout the circuit, focusing on consistent 
effort rather than speed alone.
Remind participants to land softly during jumping movements to reduce impact on the joints.
Emphasize proper breathing, inhaling during recovery phases and exhaling during exertion.
COACHING TIPS
CROSS JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms 
extended out to the sides. Jump and cross your legs and arms in 
front of your body, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the speed of the movements.
EXERCISES
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Mimic the motion of jumping rope, 
rotating your wrists as if holding a rope and jumping in place.
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or perform double-unders.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your 
toes on an elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
BUTT KICKS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jog in place, 
bringing your heels up to touch your glutes with each step.
EASIER: Perform the exercise at a walking pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
POWER SKIPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Skip forward, 
driving your knees high and using your arms to propel yourself 
upwards.
EASIER: Perform regular skips.
HARDER: Increase the height and distance of each skip.
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a slight squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees 
towards your chest.
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
ENDURANCE EVOLUTION

fiches.title: CORE CRUSHER
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 16
16
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout is designed to strengthen your core and improve overall stability. By targeting the 
muscles around your torso, this circuit helps build a stronger foundation for other movements and 
activities. 
Remind participants to engage their core throughout each exercise to protect their lower back.
Encourage slow, controlled movements, especially during balancing exercises, to improve stability 
and effectiveness.
Demonstrate modifications clearly so everyone can participate, regardless of fitness level.
COACHING TIPS
PLANK: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows under 
your shoulders and your body in a straight line. Hold the position, 
engaging your core.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees.
HARDER: Lift one leg or one arm off the ground.
EXERCISES
SIDE PLANK: Lie on your side with your elbow under your shoulder 
and your legs stacked. Lift your hips off the ground, forming a 
straight line from your head to your feet.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift your top leg off the ground.
BIRD DOGS: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your 
shoulders and your knees under your hips. Extend your right arm 
forward and your left leg backward simultaneously. Hold for a 
moment, then return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Perform the exercise without extending your arm 
HARDER: Add a crunch under your body.
HOLLOW BODY HOLD: Lie on your back with your arms extended 
overhead and your legs straight. Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs 
off the ground, creating a hollow shape with your body.­
EASIER: Keep your knees bent.
HARDER: Lower your body closer to the ground without touching it.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Start in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite 
hand, alternating sides while keeping your body stable.
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a push-up between taps.
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee towards 
your chest, then the other, as if running in place.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a twist by bringing your knee 
towards the opposite elbow.
CORE CRUSHER

fiches.title: UPPER BODY BLAST
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
18
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout targets the upper body, focusing on strength and endurance for the arms, chest, 
shoulders, and back. By incorporating a variety of bodyweight exercises, participants will build 
muscle control and improve functional strength.
Encourage participants to perform slow, controlled movements to maximize muscle engagement 
and prevent strain.
Remind participants to keep their core engaged during exercises to support proper alignment and 
reduce pressure on the lower back.
Emphasize the importance of proper hand placement and wrist alignment, especially during 
push-up variations.
COACHING TIPS
WIDE GRIP PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but 
place your hands wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body 
until your chest nearly touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands 
gripping the edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower your 
body by bending your elbows, then push back up.
EASIER: Keep your feet flat on the ground.
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
PIKE PUSH-UPS: Start in a downward dog position with your hips 
raised and your hands shoulder-width apart. Bend your elbows to 
lower your head towards the ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups with your knees slightly bent. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
DIAMOND PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but 
place your hands close together, forming a diamond shape with 
your thumbs and index fingers. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches your hands, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
EXERCISES
UPPER BODY BLAST
