by 10 seconds of rest. Complete 2-4 rounds of one
exercise before moving to the next.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
7
TABATA 101

fiches.title: TABATA FOUNDATION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
8
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This medium-intensity Tabata workout keeps the body moving with a mix of strength, balance, and 
endurance exercises. It’s perfect for those looking to build stability and coordination while keeping 
their heart rate up.
Encourage students to keep their core tight while punching in the Boxer Squat Punch to avoid 
unnecessary twisting. The punches should be quick and strong, not just arm movements.
In Lateral Lunges, emphasize pushing through the heel of the working leg to return to standing 
instead of relying on momentum.
Push-ups with Shoulder Taps require keeping the hips stable—no rocking from side to side! 
Encourage a wide stance with the feet for better balance.
COACHING TIPS
BOXER SQUAT PUNCH: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. 
Lower into a squat position while keeping your fists up in front of 
your face, as if ready to box. As you rise from the squat, punch 
forward with one arm, alternating arms with each punch.
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Add light weights to your hands.
LATERAL LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to 
the side, lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other 
leg straight. Return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the lunge.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly 
wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate 
lifting your feet off the ground, as if running in place but with 
minimal elevation.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed.
EXERCISES
TABATA FOUNDATION

fiches.title: TABATA POWER SURGE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 10
10
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This high-intensity Tabata workout is designed to push strength, endurance, and explosive power to 
the next level. These exercises will get hearts pumping and muscles burning, making it perfect for 
those looking to step up their game.
Broad Jumps should focus on landing softly—cue students to bend their knees upon landing to 
absorb impact instead of landing stiff-legged.
In Plyo Lunges, remind participants to keep their chest up and engage their core to prevent 
wobbling when switching legs.
Plyo Push-ups should be about explosiveness, not just speed—if students can’t get their hands off 
the floor, have them focus on a strong push-off to build power over time.
COACHING TIPS
BROAD JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend 
your knees and swing your arms backward. Jump forward as far as 
you can, landing softly on your feet.
EASIER: Perform a smaller jump.
HARDER: Increase the distance of your jumps or add a squat at the 
landing.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and 
one foot back. Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump, 
switching legs in mid-air and landing in a lunge with the opposite 
foot forward.
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without the jump.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom of each lunge before jumping.
PLYO PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position. Lower your 
body to the ground, then explosively push up, lifting your hands off 
the ground.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Clap your hands together at the top of each push-up.
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower 
into a slight squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees 
towards your chest.
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
EXERCISES
TABATA POWER SURGE

fiches.title: FULL THROTTLE TABATA
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
12
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This fast-paced circuit-style Tabata workout keeps things fresh by switching exercises every round instead of 
completing all rounds of one movement before moving on. It’s a total-body challenge that builds endurance, 
coordination, and strength while keeping energy levels high.
Jump Squats should focus on a controlled landing—remind participants to absorb impact by bending their 
knees and keeping their chest up.
During Lateral Shuffles, encourage participants to stay low to the ground for better agility and control rather 
than standing upright.
Reverse Plank is all about engaging the glutes and shoulders—make sure students don’t let their hips sag as 
they hold the position.
COACHING TIPS
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping.
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
EXERCISES
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands under your 
shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee towards your chest, then the 
other, as if running in place.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a twist by bringing your knee towards the 
opposite elbow.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your knees 
slightly bent. Shuffle quickly to the side, keeping your body low.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than 
shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the 
ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your toes on an 
elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
REVERSE PLANK: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands 
placed behind you, fingers pointing towards your feet. Lift your hips off the 
ground, forming a straight line from your head to your heels.
EASIER: Bend your knees and keep your feet flat on the ground.
HARDER: Lift one leg off the ground.
CROSS JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms extended out to 
the sides. Jump and cross your legs and arms in front of your body, then jump 
back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the speed of the movements.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand, alternating 
sides while keeping your body stable.
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a push-up between taps.
FULL THROTTLE TABATA

fiches.title: TABATA STACK
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 14
14
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This Tabata workout ramps up intensity round by round by stacking exercises. It starts with just one movement, 
then adds a new exercise each round while repeating all previous ones. By the final round, participants will be 
performing a full sequence of six exercises in a row without stopping!
Remind participants that each round builds on the last—encourage pacing and energy conservation early on 
to keep up through the full stack.
Lateral Hops should focus on quick footwork and soft landings—emphasize stability rather than distance for 
beginners.
For Burpees, focus on efficiency—jumping back into plank smoothly and minimizing extra movement will 
conserve energy for the final rounds.
COACHING TIPS
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, bringing your 
knees up towards your chest as high as possible with each step.
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
EXERCISES
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than 
shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the 
ground, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
LATERAL HOPS: Stand with your feet together. Jump to the side, landing 
softly on both feet, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
PLANK WITH LEG LIFTS: Start in a plank position with your hands or elbows 
under your shoulders. Lift one leg off the ground, hold for a moment, then 
switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift both legs simultaneously or add a push-up between lifts.
BURPEES: Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, place your hands on the ground, and jump your feet back into a plank 
position. Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back towards your hands 
and explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Skip the push-up.
HARDER: Add a tuck jump at the end.
TABATA STACK

DIFFICULTY
EMPHASIS
INSTRUCTIONS
Progressive Endurance, 
Strength, Power
Start with one exercise in round 1. Each round, add a new 
exercise to the stack while repeating all previous ones. By 
round 6, all six exercises will be performed in sequence 
before resting.
Round 1: High Knees – 20s work > 10s rest­
Round 2: High Knees + Jump Squats – 20s work each > 10s rest after both­
Round 3: High Knees + Jump Squats + Push-ups – 20s work each > 10s rest after all three­
Round 4: High Knees + Jump Squats + Push-ups + Lateral Hops – 20s work each > 10s rest after all four­
Round 5: High Knees + Jump Squats + Push-ups + Lateral Hops + Plank with Leg Lifts – 20s work each > 10s rest after all five­
Round 6: High Knees + Jump Squats + Push-ups + Lateral Hops + Plank with Leg Lifts – 20s work each > 10s rest after all six

fiches.title: TABATA LADDER
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 16
16
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This workout ramps up the intensity by changing the work/rest ratio as you climb the ladder, then 
easing back down. The challenge starts with short bursts, increases to longer work periods, and then 
descends, testing both endurance and recovery ability.
Since work time increases in the middle rounds, remind participants to pace themselves early so 
they don’t burn out before the longest intervals.
Lunges should focus on form and control—no rushing, and ensure knees stay aligned with toes.
Skater Hops should be about controlled landings—focus on balance rather than speed, especially as 
fatigue sets in.
COACHING TIPS
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. 
Return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Perform smaller steps.
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
PLYO TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your 
hands gripping the edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower 
your body by bending your elbows. Explosively push up, lifting your 
hands off the bench momentarily.
EASIER: Perform regular triceps dips without the jump.
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your 
toes on an elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
SKATER HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Jump 
laterally to the opposite leg, landing softly and balancing before 
jumping back.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
EXERCISES

fiches.title: TABATA TAG TEAM
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
18
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This partner-based Tabata workout keeps things fun and interactive. While one person works at full intensity, 
the other performs an active rest movement—then they switch! It’s a team challenge that keeps both 
participants engaged the entire time.
Remind participants to push their hardest during work time because their partner is counting on them!
Encourage communication—partners should motivate each other and ensure they’re transitioning smoothly.
Active rest exercises should be controlled—even though they’re "resting," form still matters, especially in 
movements like planks and wall sits.
COACHING TIPS
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping.
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
EXERCISES
BALANCE ON TOES: Stand with feet hip-width apart, lift heels, and balance 
on toes. 
EASIER: Hold onto a wall or chair for support.
HARDER: Close your eyes or perform calf raises.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than 
shoulder-width apart. Lower body until chest nearly touches the ground, then 
push back up. 
EASIER: Perform on knees.
HARDER: Elevate feet on a bench or step.
WALL SIT: Stand against a wall, slide down until knees are at a 90-degree 
angle, and hold. 
EASIER: Reduce the duration of the hold.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent. 
Shuffle quickly side to side, keeping body low. 
EASIER: Slow down pace.
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
PLANK HOLD: Start in a forearm plank, keeping body in a straight line. 
EASIER: Perform on knees.
HARDER: Lift one leg or one arm off the ground.
TOE TAPS: Stand with feet hip-width apart, quickly tap toes on an elevated 
surface, alternating feet. 
EASIER: Tap toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase speed or use a higher surface.
LATERAL LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to the 
side, lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other leg straight. 
Return to the starting position and switch sides.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the lunge.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
TABATA TAG TEAM

fiches.title: TABATA STRENGTH
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 20
20
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This Tabata workout focuses on building strength rather than speed. Instead of explosive movements, 
the goal is controlled, powerful reps that maximize muscle engagement. Slower, intentional 
movements make every second count!
Strength-focused Tabata isn’t about speed—remind participants to move slowly and with control, 
focusing on form.
Single-leg Romanian Deadlifts require balance—if someone is struggling, they can keep their back 
leg lightly touching the ground for stability.
For Reverse Plank Holds, shoulders should stay down and away from the ears—engage the core and 
glutes to prevent sagging.
COACHING TIPS
SUMO SQUATS: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width 
apart and your toes turned out. Lower into a squat, keeping your 
chest up and knees tracking over your toes, then return to the 
starting position.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
DIAMOND PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but 
place your hands close together, forming a diamond shape with 
your thumbs and index fingers. Lower your body until your chest 
nearly touches your hands, then push back up.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
SINGLE-LEG ROMANIAN DEADLIFT: Stand on one leg with a slight 
bend in your knee. Hinge at the hips, lowering your torso while 
extending the opposite leg backward. Return to the starting 
position.
EASIER: Use a wall or chair for balance.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
REVERSE PLANK HOLD: Sit on the floor with your legs extended 
and your hands placed behind you, fingers pointing towards your 
feet. Lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your 
head to your heels.
EASIER: Bend your knees and keep your feet flat on the ground.
HARDER: Lift one leg off the ground.
EXERCISES
TABATA STRENGTH

fiches.title: TABATA FLOW
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 22
22
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This mobility-focused Tabata is designed to improve flexibility, joint range of motion, and stability 
while keeping the body moving. Instead of high-intensity cardio or strength work, these exercises 
focus on controlled movement and deep muscle activation.
Encourage slow, steady breathing—holding poses requires focus and controlled breath work to 
maintain balance and flexibility.
For Tree Pose, remind participants to engage their core to stay stable rather than gripping the floor 
with their toes.
Downward Dog should be about alignment, not just getting the heels to the floor—press hands 
firmly into the mat and lengthen the spine.
COACHING TIPS
CAT-COW STRETCH: Start on all fours with your hands under your 
shoulders and knees under your hips. Inhale, arch your back, and 
look up (Cow Pose). Exhale, round your back, and tuck your chin to 
your chest (Cat Pose).
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.
HARDER: Hold each position for a longer duration.
TREE POSE: Stand on one leg with your other foot placed against 
your inner thigh or calf. Bring your hands together at your chest or 
overhead, holding the position.
EASIER: Use a wall for support.
HARDER: Close your eyes or reach your arms higher.
DOWNWARD DOG: Start on all fours, with your hands under your 
shoulders and knees under your hips. Lift your hips toward the 
ceiling, straightening your legs and forming an inverted V shape 
with your body.
EASIER: Keep your knees slightly bent.
HARDER: Push your heels down to the floor for a deeper stretch.
FIGURE FOUR STRETCH: Sit or lie on the floor with your legs 
extended. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee, forming a figure 
four shape. Lean forward slightly to increase the stretch.
EASIER: Keep your back straight.
HARDER: Pull your crossed leg closer to your body.
EXERCISES
TABATA FLOW

fiches.title: TABATA TEMPO
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 24
24
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This high-energy, cardio-focused Tabata is all about maximizing heart rate, endurance, and agility. 
The exercises are designed to keep participants moving quickly while engaging multiple muscle 
groups for a full-body sweat session.
Encourage quick, light steps—for High Knees and Lateral Shuffles, keeping feet fast and movements 
controlled will boost agility.
Jumping Jacks should be full range—hands should touch overhead and feet should fully extend for 
maximum engagement.
Power Skips aren’t just for height—remind participants to drive with their arms and core for 
explosive movement.
COACHING TIPS
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up towards your chest as high as possible with 
each step.
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your 
knees slightly bent. Shuffle quickly to the side, keeping your body 
low.
EASIER: Slow down the pace.
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at 
your sides. Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms 
overhead, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out 
wide.
POWER SKIPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Skip forward, 
driving your knees high and using your arms to propel yourself 
upwards.
EASIER: Perform regular skips.
HARDER: Increase the height and distance of each skip.
EXERCISES
TABATA TEMPO

fiches.title: QUICK FEET TABATA
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 26
26
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This Speed, Agility, and Quickness (SAQ)-focused Tabata is all about fast footwork, reaction time, and 
body control. The exercises emphasize multi-directional movement to improve overall athletic ability 
while keeping the heart rate high.
Encourage light, quick feet—for Forward and Backward Hops and Toe Taps, remind participants to 
stay on the balls of their feet to react faster.
Lateral High Knees should emphasize controlled movement—staying low and balanced while moving 
side to side will improve agility.
Skater Hops require both power and control—landing softly with knees bent prevents injury and 
helps maintain rhythm.
COACHING TIPS
FORWARD AND BACKWARD HOPS: Stand with your feet hip-width 
apart. Jump forward a small distance, then jump backward to the 
starting position.
EASIER: Perform small hops.
HARDER: Increase the distance of each hop.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your 
toes on an elevated surface, alternating feet.
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead.
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
LATERAL HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in 
place, bringing your knees up towards your chest while moving 
laterally.
EASIER: March in place with high knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed and distance of lateral movement.
SKATER HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Jump 
laterally to the opposite leg, landing softly and balancing before 
jumping back.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping.
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
EXERCISES
QUICK FEET TABATA

fiches.title: CORE CRUSHER TABATA
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 28
28
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
This core-focused Tabata strengthens the abs, obliques, and lower back while improving stability and 
control. The exercises target different angles of the core, ensuring a well-rounded abdominal workout 
that builds both strength and endurance.
Hollow Body Hold is all about core engagement—remind participants to press their lower back into 
the floor and avoid arching.
In the Plank with Arm and Leg Lift, balance is key—if rocking too much, they should widen their 
stance.
Bear Crawls should be smooth and controlled—keeping the knees low to the ground helps activate 
the core more effectively.
COACHING TIPS
HOLLOW BODY HOLD: Lie on your back with your arms extended 
overhead and your legs straight. Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs 
off the ground, creating a hollow shape with your body.
EASIER: Keep your knees bent.
HARDER: Lower your body closer to the ground without touching it.
PLANK WITH ARM AND LEG LIFT: Start in a plank position with your 
hands under your shoulders. Lift one arm and the opposite leg 
simultaneously, hold for a moment, then switch sides.
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Hold the lifted position longer or add a push-up between 
lifts.
BEAR CRAWLS: Begin on all fours with your hands under your 
shoulders and knees under your hips. Lift your knees a few inches 
off the ground. Move forward by simultaneously moving your right 
hand and left foot, then your left hand and right foot.
EASIER: Keep your knees on the ground.
HARDER: INCREASE THE SPEED OR CRAWL BACKWARD.
SIDE PLANK: Lie on your side with your elbow under your shoulder 
and your legs stacked. Lift your hips off the ground, forming a 
straight line from your head to your feet.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees.
HARDER: Lift your top leg off the ground.
EXERCISES
CORE CRUSHER TABATA

Welcome to Strength & Conditioning Cheat Sheets: No Equipment Workouts.
This comprehensive resource was designed to meet the diverse needs of PE 
teachers, sports coaches, strength & conditioning coaches, personal trainers, 
athletes, and parents of athletes. 
Whether you’re looking to enhance your physical education curriculum, 
develop training programs for teams, or create dynamic workouts for personal 
fitness, this book has everything you need to achieve your goals—without the 
need for any special equipment.
This resource is thoughtfully organized into two main sections. 
The first section features 30 No Equipment Workouts, categorized into eight 
key areas of fitness: Cardiovascular Endurance, Muscular Strength, Muscular 
Endurance, Agility, Coordination, Power, Speed, and Balance & Flexibility. 
Each workout is designed as a circuit that can be completed in just 5-10 minutes, 
making them perfect for quick sessions or for combining into longer training 
routines. We recommend performing 2-5 circuits, with short rest periods 
between each one, to maximize the benefits of these workouts.
The second section is a bonus exercise library, which includes detailed 
descriptions of 98 no-equipment exercises organized in alphabetical order. 
Each exercise comes with modifications to make them easier or harder, allowing 
you to tailor the workouts to the specific needs and abilities of your athletes or 
students. 
Whether you’re working with beginners or advanced athletes, this library 
provides the flexibility to adjust the intensity and challenge of each movement.
This book is designed to be a versatile tool in your fitness arsenal, offering 
effective, time-efficient workouts that can be performed anywhere, by anyone. 
Get ready to explore a world of fitness possibilities, all within the pages of this 
comprehensive guide.

BALANCE ON TOES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Raise your heels off 
the ground and balance on your toes. Hold the position, engaging your core to 
maintain stability. 
EASIER: Hold onto a wall or chair for support. 
HARDER: Close your eyes or perform calf raises.
BEAR CRAWLS: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Lift your knees a few inches off the ground. Move 
forward by simultaneously moving your right hand and left foot, then your left 
hand and right foot.
EASIER: Keep your knees on the ground.  
HARDER: Increase the speed or crawl backward.
BEAR CRAWL HOLD: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders 
and knees under your hips. Lift your knees a few inches off the ground and hold 
the position. 
EASIER: Keep your knees on the ground. 
HARDER: Increase the duration of the hold or add arm and leg movements.
BIRD DOGS: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and 
your knees under your hips. Extend your right arm forward and your left leg 
backward simultaneously. Hold for a moment, then return to the starting position 
and switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise without extending your arm.
HARDER: Add a crunch under your body.
BOXER SQUAT PUNCH: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat position while keeping your fists up in front of your face, as if ready to 
box. As you rise from the squat, punch forward with one arm, alternating arms 
with each punch. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Add light weights to your hands.
BROAD JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees 
and swing your arms backward. Jump forward as far as you can, landing softly 
on your feet. 
EASIER: Perform a smaller jump.
HARDER: Increase the distance of your jumps or add a squat at the landing.
BUTT KICKS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jog in place, bringing your 
heels up to touch your glutes with each step. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise at a walking pace.
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
BURPEES: Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat, place your hands on the ground, and jump your feet back into a plank 
position. Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back towards your hands, and 
explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead. 
EASIER: Skip the push-up.
HARDER: Add a tuck jump at the end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 37

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 38
BUTTERFLY STRETCH: Sit on the floor with your feet together and your knees 
bent out to the sides. Hold your feet with your hands and gently press your 
knees towards the ground. 
EASIER: Sit on a cushion for support.  
HARDER: Lean forward to deepen the stretch.
CAT-COW STRETCH: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders 
and knees under your hips. Inhale, arch your back, and look up (Cow Pose). 
Exhale, round your back, and tuck your chin to your chest (Cat Pose). 
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.  
HARDER: Hold each position for a longer duration.
CHILD'S POSE: Kneel on the floor with your big toes touching and knees spread 
apart. Sit back on your heels and extend your arms forward, lowering your torso 
between your thighs. 
EASIER: Keep your knees together. 
HARDER: Reach your arms further forward for a deeper stretch.
CLOSE-GRIP PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands closer together, directly under your shoulders. Lower your body until your 
chest nearly touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
COBRA POSE: Lie face down on the floor with your hands under your shoulders 
and your elbows close to your body. Press into your hands to lift your chest off 
the floor, keeping your hips grounded. 
EASIER: Lift only to a comfortable height. 
HARDER: Extend your arms fully to increase the stretch.
CROSS JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms extended out to the 
sides. Jump and cross your legs and arms in front of your body, then jump back 
to the starting position.
EASIER: Step instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the speed of the movements.
DIAMOND PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index 
fingers. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches your hands, then push 
back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
DECLINE PUSH-UPS: Start in a push-up position with your feet elevated on a 
bench or step. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the ground, then 
push back up.
EASIER: Lower the height of the elevation. 
HARDER: Increase the height of the elevation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 38

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 39
DOWNWARD DOG: Start on all fours, with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Lift your hips towards the ceiling, straightening your legs 
and forming an inverted V shape with your body. 
EASIER: Keep your knees slightly bent. 
HARDER: Push your heels down to the floor for a deeper stretch.
EAGLE POSE: Stand with your feet together. Bend your knees slightly and lift 
one leg, wrapping it around the other. Cross your arms in front of your body and 
try to wrap your palms together. 
EASIER: Use a wall for support. 
HARDER: Hold the position longer or lower your hips further.
FAST FEET: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly alternate lifting your 
feet off the ground, as if running in place but with minimal elevation. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace. 
HARDER: Increase the speed.
FIGURE FOUR STRETCH: Sit on the floor with your legs extended. Cross one 
ankle over the opposite knee, forming a figure four shape. Lean forward slightly 
to increase the stretch.
EASIER: Keep your back straight. 
HARDER: Pull your crossed leg closer to your body.
FIRE HYDRANT: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and 
knees under your hips. Keeping your knee bent, lift one leg out to the side, 
mimicking the motion of a dog at a fire hydrant. Lower your leg back down and 
repeat.
EASIER: Reduce the height of the lift. 
HARDER: Add a resistance band around your thighs.
FORWARD AND BACKWARD HOPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Jump 
forward a small distance, then jump backward to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform small hops. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each hop.
HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, bringing your 
knees up towards your chest as high as possible with each step. 
EASIER: March in place, lifting your knees to a comfortable height. 
HARDER: Increase the speed to a sprint.
GLUTE BRIDGES: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the 
ground. Press into your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling, then lower back 
down.
EASIER: Perform the exercise with a smaller range of motion. 
HARDER: Hold the lifted position for a longer duration or add weight to your hips.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 39

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 40
HIP FLEXOR STRETCH: Kneel on one knee with the other foot in front of you, 
creating a 90-degree angle with both legs. Shift your weight forward, feeling a 
stretch in your hip flexor. 
EASIER: Keep the stretch gentle. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the stretch or lift your back knee off the ground.
HOLLOW BODY HOLD: Lie on your back with your arms extended overhead and 
your legs straight. Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs off the ground, creating a 
hollow shape with your body. 
EASIER: Keep your knees bent. 
HARDER: Lower your body closer to the ground without touching it.
IMAGINARY JUMP ROPE: Mimic the motion of jumping rope, rotating your wrists 
as if holding a rope and jumping in place. 
EASIER: Perform small jumps or march in place. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or perform double-unders.
ISOMETRIC PUSH-UP HOLD: Start in a standard push-up position. Lower your 
body halfway down and hold the position. 
EASIER: Perform the hold on your knees.
HARDER: Hold for a longer duration or add pulses.
JUMP SQUATS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, 
then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead. 
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
JUMPING JACKS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your sides. 
Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms overhead, then jump back 
to the starting position.
EASIER: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a squat when your feet are out wide.
LATERAL LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to the side, 
lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other leg straight. Return to the 
starting position and switch sides. 
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the lunge.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
LATERAL HOPS: Stand with your feet together. Jump to the side, landing softly 
on both feet, then jump back to the starting position.
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 40

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 41
LATERAL LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot to the 
side, lowering your hips into a lunge while keeping your other leg straight. Hold 
the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the duration of the hold.  
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
LATERAL HIGH KNEES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Run in place, 
bringing your knees up towards your chest while moving laterally. 
EASIER: March in place with high knees.  
HARDER: Increase the speed and distance of lateral movement.
LATERAL SHUFFLES: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your knees 
slightly bent. Shuffle quickly to the side, keeping your body low. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace.  
HARDER: Add a touch to the ground with each step.
LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and lower your 
hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. Return to the starting 
position and switch sides.  
EASIER: Perform smaller steps. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and lower 
into a lunge, holding the position. 
EASIER: Shorten the duration of the hold. 
HARDER: Hold weights in each hand.
MOUNTAIN CLIMBERS: Start in a plank position with your hands under your 
shoulders. Quickly alternate bringing one knee towards your chest, then the 
other, as if running in place. 
EASIER: Slow down the pace.  
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a twist by bringing your knee towards the 
opposite elbow.
PIKE PUSH-UPS: Start in a downward dog position with your hips raised and 
your hands shoulder-width apart. Bend your elbows to lower your head towards 
the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups with your knees slightly bent.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
NECK ROTATIONS: Stand or sit comfortably with your back straight. Slowly 
rotate your head in a circular motion, first clockwise, then counterclockwise. 
EASIER: Reduce the range of motion.  
HARDER: Increase the duration of each rotation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 41

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 42
PLANK: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows under your shoulders 
and your body in a straight line. Hold the position, engaging your core.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift one leg or one arm off the ground.
PLANK WITH ARM AND LEG LIFT: Start in a plank position with your hands 
under your shoulders. Lift one arm and the opposite leg simultaneously, hold for 
a moment, then switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Hold the lifted position longer or add a push-up between lifts.
PLANK WITH LEG LIFTS: Start in a plank position with your hands under your 
shoulders. Lift one leg off the ground, hold for a moment, then switch sides. 
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift both legs simultaneously or add a push-up between lifts.
PLANK WITH SHOULDER TAPS: Start in a plank position with your hands under 
your shoulders. Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand, alternating sides while 
keeping your body stable.  
EASIER: Perform the exercise on your knees.
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a push-up between taps.
PLYO LUNGES: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and one foot back. 
Lower your body into a lunge, then explosively jump, switching legs in mid-air and 
landing in a lunge with the opposite foot forward. 
EASIER: Perform regular lunges without the jump.
HARDER: Add a pause at the bottom of each lunge before jumping.
PLYO PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position. Lower your body to the 
ground, then explosively push up, lifting your hands off the ground.  
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.  
HARDER: Clap your hands together at the top of each push-up.
PUSH-UPS: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than 
shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the 
ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees.
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
PLYO TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands 
gripping the edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower your body by bending 
your elbows. Explosively push up, lifting your hands off the bench momentarily. 
EASIER: Perform regular triceps dips without the jump. 
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 42

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 43
PUSH-UP WITH ROTATION: Start in a high plank position with your hands 
slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body into a push-up, then 
rotate your body to one side, lifting one arm towards the ceiling. Return to the 
starting position and repeat on the other side.
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Add a weight in your lifting hand.
POWER SKIPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Skip forward, driving your 
knees high and using your arms to propel yourself upwards.  
EASIER: Perform regular skips. 
HARDER: Increase the height and distance of each skip.
REVERSE PLANK: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands 
placed behind you, fingers pointing towards your feet. Lift your hips off the 
ground, forming a straight line from your head to your heels. 
EASIER: Bend your knees and keep your feet flat on the ground. 
HARDER: Lift one leg off the ground.
SIDE PLANK: Lie on your side with your elbow under your shoulder and your legs 
stacked. Lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your head to 
your feet.
EASIER: Perform the plank on your knees. 
HARDER: Lift your top leg off the ground.
SINGLE-LEG BALANCE: Stand on one leg, lifting the other foot off the ground. 
Hold the position, engaging your core to maintain balance. 
EASIER: Use a wall or chair for support. 
HARDER: Close your eyes or move your lifted leg in different directions.
SINGLE-LEG HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Hop forward, 
backward, or side to side on the same leg, landing softly with control. Repeat the 
hops in a consistent rhythm.  
EASIER: Perform smaller hops or use both legs. 
HARDER: Hop in different directions each time.
SINGLE-LEG ROMANIAN DEADLIFT: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in your 
knee. Hinge at the hips, lowering your torso while extending the opposite leg 
backward. Return to the starting position.  
EASIER: Use a wall or chair for balance. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SINGLE-LEG GLUTE BRIDGE HOLD: Lie on your back with your knees bent and 
feet flat on the ground. Lift one leg off the ground and extend it straight. Press 
into your supporting foot to lift your hips towards the ceiling and hold the 
position.
EASIER: Perform a regular glute bridge with both feet on the ground. 
HARDER: Add pulses at the top of the hold.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 43

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 44
SINGLE-LEG SQUAT: Stand on one leg with your other leg extended in front of 
you. Lower your body into a squat, keeping your extended leg off the ground, 
then return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Hold onto a wall or chair for balance.
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SKATER HOPS: Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Jump laterally to 
the opposite leg, landing softly and balancing before jumping back. 
EASIER: Step side to side instead of jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the distance of each jump.
SQUAT: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your hips back and 
down as if sitting into a chair, keeping your chest up and knees over your toes. 
Return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat.  
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SQUAT WITH OVERHEAD REACH: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. 
Lower your hips back and down into a squat while reaching your arms overhead. 
Return to the starting position. 
EASIER: Perform a partial squat. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
SQUAT JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, 
then explosively jump up, reaching your arms overhead.
EASIER: Perform regular squats without jumping. 
HARDER: Increase the depth of the squat or add weights.
SQUAT PULSE: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat 
and perform small, pulsing movements at the bottom of the squat.  
EASIER: Perform regular squats. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
STAR JUMPS: Stand with your feet together and your arms at your sides. Jump 
up, spreading your arms and legs out wide like a star, then return to the starting 
position. 
EASIER: Perform jumping jacks instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or add a tuck jump at the end.
STANDING HAMSTRING STRETCH: Stand with your feet together. Extend one 
leg forward, keeping it straight, and hinge at the hips to reach towards your toes.
EASIER: Bend your knees slightly.
HARDER: Flex your foot to increase the stretch.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 44

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 45
STATIC LUNGE HOLD: Stand with your feet together. Step one foot forward and 
lower into a lunge, holding the position. 
EASIER: Shorten the duration of the hold.
HARDER: Hold weights in each hand.
STATIC SQUAT HOLD: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a 
squat and hold the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat. 
HARDER:HOLD A WEIGHT IN YOUR HANDS.
SUMO SQUATS: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your 
toes turned out. Lower into a squat, keeping your chest up and knees tracking 
over your toes, then return to the starting position.
EASIER: Reduce the depth of the squat.   
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
TOE TAPS: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Quickly tap your toes on an 
elevated surface, alternating feet. 
EASIER: Tap your toes on the ground instead. 
HARDER: Increase the speed or use a higher surface.
TOUCH AND HOP: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat 
and reach down to touch the ground with one hand. As you rise, hop on the 
opposite leg, lifting your other knee up towards your chest. Repeat on the other 
side. 
EASIER: Perform the movement without the hop, just standing up instead.  
HARDER: Increase the speed and height of the hop.
TREE POSE: Stand on one leg with your other foot placed against your inner 
thigh or calf. Bring your hands together at your chest or overhead, holding the 
position. 
EASIER: Use a wall for support.  
HARDER: Close your eyes or reach your arms higher.
TRICEPS STRETCH: Stand or sit with your back straight. Reach one arm 
overhead and bend your elbow, bringing your hand down your back. Use your 
other hand to gently press on your bent elbow, increasing the stretch. 
EASIER: Reduce the pressure on your elbow. 
HARDER: Reach further down your back.
TRICEPS DIPS: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands gripping the 
edge. Slide your hips off the edge and lower your body by bending your elbows, 
then push back up. 
EASIER: Keep your feet flat on the ground. 
HARDER: Extend your legs straight out in front of you.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 45

© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 46
TUCK JUMPS: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a slight 
squat and explosively jump up, tucking your knees towards your chest. 
EASIER: Perform regular jumps without tucking your knees.
HARDER: Increase the height of your jump.
WALKING LUNGES: Stand with your feet together. Step forward with one foot 
and lower your hips into a lunge. Push off the back foot to bring it forward and 
repeat the lunge on the other side. 
EASIER: Perform stationary lunges. 
HARDER: Hold a weight in each hand.
WALL SIT: Stand with your back against a wall and your feet shoulder-width 
apart. Slide down the wall until your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle, 
holding the position. 
EASIER: Reduce the duration of the hold.   
HARDER: Hold a weight in your hands.
WARRIOR POSE (WARRIOR I): Stand with your feet wide apart. Turn one foot 
out and bend your knee to form a 90-degree angle. Extend your arms overhead, 
keeping your hips squared towards the front. Hold the position, engaging your 
core and focusing on your balance. 
EASIER: Shorten your stance. 
HARDER: Deepen the bend in your front knee
WIDE GRIP PUSH-UPS: Start in a standard push-up position, but place your 
hands wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly 
touches the ground, then push back up. 
EASIER: Perform the push-ups on your knees. 
HARDER: Elevate your feet on a bench or step.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com 46

fiches.title: INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
3
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to PE Curriculum Maps for Grades 3-5, your ultimate resource for planning engaging, effective, 
and fun physical education units! 
Whether you’re a PE teacher, coach, parent, fitness instructor, or after-school program director, this 
resource provides you with ready-to-go unit plans designed to spark movement, build skills, and inspire 
teamwork among students.
While these curriculum maps are specifically created for grades 3-5, they are flexible and can easily 
be adapted for younger or older kids. Each unit includes age-appropriate activities that combine skill 
development, physical fitness, and social-emotional learning, ensuring all students are active, challenged, 
and having fun.
The curriculum is organized into five key sections to provide variety and structure throughout the 
school year:
1.	 Seasonal Units – Themed units that connect movement to seasons, holidays, and fun traditions.
2.	 Holiday Units - Fun, festive activities that celebrate holidays while building skills and encouraging 
movement.
3.	 Sports Skills Units – Fundamental sports skills like soccer, basketball, and volleyball to build 
confidence and coordination.
4.	 Movement Skills Units – Activities that develop speed, agility, balance, and rhythm through creative 
movement.
5.	 SEL & Teamwork Units – Challenges that foster teamwork, communication, leadership, and 
sportsmanship.
6.	 Health & Fitness Units – Units designed to promote lifelong healthy habits, strength, endurance, 
and mindfulness.
Each curriculum map is carefully structured into the following components:
•	
Learning Objectives – Five clear goals for the unit.
•	
Instant Activities – Five quick, engaging warm-ups to get students moving right away.
•	
Skill Builders – Three focused activities to teach and develop key skills.
•	
Large Group Game – A fun and active culminating game that reinforces the skills of the unit while 
encouraging teamwork and strategy.
With 30 comprehensive units, this resource provides everything you need to create an exciting year of 
PE classes. The lessons are designed to be flexible and adaptable for different spaces, group sizes, and 
abilities, giving you the tools to keep students engaged, active, and excited about physical education.
Let’s get started and make this year of PE your best yet! Turn the page and dive into the first unit—
there’s fun, fitness, and skill-building ahead!

fiches.title: BACK TO SCHOOL: TEAM FITNESS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, bean bags, hula hoops, jump 
ropes, small balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
COOPERATION
COMMUNICATION 
COORDINATION 
CONFIDENCE   
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
MEET & GREET 
TAG 
Students jog around the 
space. When tagged, 
they stop, introduce 
themselves, and share a 
fun fact.
HUMAN KNOT 
CHALLENGE  
Groups form a 
circle, grab 
random hands, 
and work together 
to untangle 
without letting go.
FOLLOW THE 
LEADER
One student leads 
the group through a 
path (e.g., hopping, 
crawling, skipping) 
while others follow.
NAME TOSS   
In a circle, students 
toss a ball to a 
classmate while 
calling their name. 
Add a second ball 
to increase 
challenge.
GROUP FREEZE 
DANCE     
Play music while 
students dance in 
groups. When the music 
stops, groups freeze in 
funny poses together.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
TEAM BALANCE 
LINE  
BEAN BAG 
PASS
PE CHARADES
  
 
Students stand on a 
taped line or beam, 
helping each other 
stay balanced as 
they move along.
 
 
Teams sit in a circle 
and pass a bean bag 
using only speciﬁc 
body parts (e.g., 
elbows, knees).
 
In small groups, one 
student acts out a 
sport or exercise 
while their 
teammates guess 
what it is.
  
LARGE GROUP GAME  
TEAM MAZE WALK
Set up a simple cone maze. Divide 
students into small teams. One 
teammate is blindfolded, while the 
others guide them through the maze 
using verbal directions. Teams rotate 
roles until everyone has had a turn as 
the "navigator." 
This activity builds trust, 
communication, and cooperation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6

fiches.title: PUMPKIN PATCH GAMES
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, small balls (pumpkins), bean 
bags, hula hoops, pool noodles
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE
AGILITY
COORDINATION 
FUN & 
ENGAGEMENT    
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
PUMPKIN RUN 
RELAY
Teams take turns 
carrying a small ball 
(pumpkin) across the 
gym and back without 
dropping it.
PATCH WEAVE  
Students weave 
through cones 
(pumpkins in a 
patch) while 
performing various 
movements like 
skipping or 
hopping.
PUMPKIN 
BALANCE 
WALK
Students balance a 
“pumpkin” (small 
ball) on their hand 
or head while 
walking along a 
straight or curved 
path.
PUMPKIN TOSS 
Students toss bean 
bags (pumpkin 
seeds) into hula 
hoops (pumpkin 
patches) from 
increasing 
distances.
HARVEST HOP   
Students perform 
controlled side-to-side 
or forward hops over 
pool noodles (pumpkin 
vines) spread across the 
ﬂoor.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
PUMPKIN ROLL 
CHALLENGE 
PUMPKIN 
PICK-UP
PATCH 
BALANCE POSE
 
Students work in 
pairs to roll a small 
ball (pumpkin) across 
the gym using only 
pool noodles.
 
 Students race to 
gather scattered 
bean bags (pumpkins) 
and carry them back 
to a hula hoop 
(basket) one at a 
time.
 Students hold a 
one-legged balance 
pose while holding a 
“pumpkin” (small 
ball). Add movement 
like arm stretches for 
a challenge.
  
LARGE GROUP GAME  
PUMPKIN PATCH PICK-UP
Divide students into teams. Scatter 
small balls (pumpkins) and bean bags 
(seeds) across the gym. Each team’s 
goal is to “harvest” the pumpkins and 
seeds one at a time and return them 
to their “patch” (hula hoop). Once all 
items are collected, teams must sort 
their “harvest” by type—balls in one 
hoop and bean bags in another. 
This game builds teamwork, strategy, 
and movement control.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
7
PUMPKIN PATCH GAMES

fiches.title: WINTER OLYMPIC GAMES
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, pool noodles, hula hoops, bean 
bags, jump ropes, small balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE
AGILITY
COORDINATION 
ENDURANCE
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
SKI JUMP 
CHALLENGE
Students perform 
forward “ski jumps” over 
pool noodles, landing 
with knees bent and 
arms forward.
ICE SKATER 
SLIDES
Students slide 
side-to-side in a 
controlled motion, 
pretending to 
skate across ice. 
Add a clap on each 
slide.
SNOWBALL 
TOSS
Students toss small 
balls (snowballs) 
into hula hoops set 
at varying 
distances. Increase 
challenge with time 
limits.
BOBSLED DASH 
Students run in 
pairs while holding 
onto a pool noodle 
(bobsled), steering 
around cones as a 
team.
OLYMPIC TORCH 
RELAY  
Teams take turns 
running while carrying 
a small cone (torch) 
above their head and 
passing it to the next 
teammate.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
SPEED SKATER 
STEPS
CURLING 
CHALLENGE
SLALOM CONE 
WEAVE
 
Students step 
laterally in quick 
movements, tapping 
a cone on each side 
to mimic speed 
skaters.
 
Students slide bean 
bags (stones) across 
the gym ﬂoor toward 
a target (hula hoop) 
to practice controlled 
force.
Students weave 
through a line of 
cones while 
performing controlled 
“ski” side steps or 
quick zig-zag runs.
  
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
WINTER GAMES CIRCUIT
Set up four Olympic-themed stations:
•
Ski Jumps – Students perform 
repeated jumps over pool noodles.
•
Snowball Toss – Students aim 
snowballs (small balls) into hoops 
from a distance.
•
Bobsled Race – Teams dash while 
steering pool noodles around cones.
•
Speed Skating – Students slide 
side-to-side between two cones as 
fast as possible.
Teams rotate through the stations in 2-3 
minute intervals. End with a “medal 
ceremony” where everyone celebrates 
completing the games.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
WINTER OLYMPIC GAMES

fiches.title: SPRING INTO FITNESS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, pool noodles, hula hoops, bean 
bags, jump ropes, small balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
OUTDOOR 
ACTIVITY 
BALANCE
AGILITY
COORDINATION 
ENDURANCE
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
GARDEN SPRINT 
RELAY
Students run short 
sprints to “plant seeds” 
(bean bags) in hula 
hoops across the ﬁeld.
BUTTERFLY 
SKIP
Students skip 
across a ﬁeld or 
open space while 
ﬂapping their 
“butterﬂy wings” 
(arms).
SUNSHINE 
HOPSCOTCH
Use chalk to draw 
hopscotch grids. 
Students hop 
through while 
adding fun 
challenges (e.g., 
spin on squares).
FLOWER 
PATCH 
BALANCE 
Place hoops around 
the area as “ﬂower 
patches.” Students 
balance on one foot 
inside a hoop for 10 
seconds.
RAINDROP DODGE
Scatter small balls 
(raindrops) across the 
ground. Students move 
quickly without 
stepping on or touching 
them.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
TRAIL RUN 
CHALLENGE
BALANCE 
BEAM WALK
BEAN BAG 
TARGET TOSS
 
Students follow a 
marked outdoor path 
(chalk or cones) using 
controlled jogging 
and quick directional 
changes.
Use chalk or ropes to 
create lines (logs). 
Students walk 
heel-to-toe with arms 
extended for balance.
Students toss bean 
bags into hula hoops 
(ﬂower targets) at 
varying distances to 
practice accuracy.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
SPRING SCAVENGER DASH
Divide students into teams. Scatter 
“spring items” (bean bags, cones, or 
natural objects like leaves or rocks) 
around the outdoor space. Each team 
is assigned a list of items to collect. 
Students run, gather items one at a 
time, and return them to their team’s 
“basket” (a hula hoop). The ﬁrst team 
to collect all items wins. Encourage 
teams to communicate and strategize.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
9
SPRING INTO FITNESS

fiches.title: SUMMER SPORTS FEST
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
10
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, pool noodles, hula hoops, bean 
bags, jump ropes, small balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
OUTDOOR 
ACTIVITY 
FUN & 
ENGAGEMENT 
AGILITY
COORDINATION 
ENDURANCE
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BEACH BALL DASH
Students run while 
balancing a beach ball 
between their arms or 
legs, racing to a ﬁnish 
line without dropping it. 
NOODLE 
LIMBO
Use a pool noodle 
as the limbo bar. 
Students take turns 
leaning back to 
move under 
without touching it.
CONE ISLAND 
HOPS
Place cones or 
hoops (“islands”) on 
the ﬁeld. Students 
hop from island to 
island without 
touching the 
“water.”
RELAY JUMP 
ROPES
Teams take turns 
completing short 
distances with jump 
ropes. Add 
variations like 
skipping or side 
steps.
WATERMELON 
TOSS
Students toss small 
balls or bean bags 
(“watermelons”) into 
hoops from increasing 
distances.
SKILL BUILDERS  
NOODLE 
SOCCER 
DRIBBLE
HULA HOOP 
SPIN 
CHALLENGE
CONE WEAVE 
SPRINTS
 
Students use pool 
noodles to dribble 
small balls across a 
marked path, 
controlling the ball 
with gentle taps.
Students practice 
spinning hula hoops 
around their waists, 
arms, or ankles. Add 
timed challenges for 
fun.
Students weave 
quickly through a 
series of cones while 
carrying or gently 
tossing a bean bag 
between hands.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
SUMMER SPORTS CIRCUIT
Set up ﬁve summer-themed stations:
1.
Beach Ball Tap – Students tap a 
beach ball back and forth with a 
partner using their hands.
2. Limbo Line – Students limbo under a 
pool noodle held at decreasing 
heights.
3. Jump Rope Sprint – Students jump 
rope across a short marked path.
4. Hula Hoop Station – Students 
perform a timed hula hoop challenge 
(waist, arm, or ankle spins).
5. Noodle Obstacle Course – Students 
navigate a short course with cones, 
pool noodles, and quick hops.
Rotate teams through stations every 2-3 
minutes. Finish with a group cheer to 
celebrate participation and effort.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
10
SUMMER SPORTS FEST

fiches.title: HOLIDAY UNITS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
11
HOLIDAY UNITS

fiches.title: SPOOKTACULAR HALLOWEEN FUN
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, bean bags, small balls, hula 
hoops, pool noodles, scarves
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY  
FUN & 
CREATIVITY 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
PUMPKIN PATCH 
SPRINT
Students race to 
collect bean bags 
(“pumpkins”) 
scattered across the 
gym and place them 
in their team’s 
“patch.”   
WITCH’S HAT 
TOSS   
Students toss bean 
bags (“magic 
stones”) into hula 
hoops or onto cones 
decorated as witch 
hats.
GHOST TAG
The “ghost” (tagger) 
moves slowly but 
can only tag 
students while 
walking in straight 
lines. Avoid the 
ghost to stay free! 
 
CREEPY 
CRAWLERS 
RELAY 
 Students 
bear-crawl or 
crab-walk across a 
short course, 
picking up small 
“spiders” (scarves 
or bean bags). 
ZOMBIE FREEZE 
DANCE 
Students dance like 
zombies to 
Halloween-themed 
music. When the 
music stops, they 
freeze in a spooky 
pose. 
SKILL BUILDERS  
SPIDERWEB 
BALANCE PATH  
BAT WING 
WEAVE 
HAUNTED 
HOOP TOSS  
Use tape or chalk to 
create a “spiderweb” 
path. Students walk 
along the lines 
balancing bean bags 
on their heads.
 
 Students hold 
scarves (wings) and 
weave through a line 
of cones using 
smooth, controlled 
movements.
 
Students throw small 
balls into hoops 
arranged at different 
distances, earning 
points for each 
“cauldron” they hit.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
ESCAPE THE HAUNTED 
MAZE 
 Set up a spooky maze with cones, pool 
noodles, and hula hoops as obstacles. 
Students work in teams to navigate the 
maze: hop over “pumpkin vines” (cones), 
crawl under “spiderwebs” (noodles), and 
carefully step across the “ghost bridge” (a 
taped line). At the end of the maze, each 
team collects one “pumpkin” (small ball) 
and races back to tag the next teammate. 
First team to ﬁnish wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
SPOOKTACULAR HALLOWEEN FUN

fiches.title: TURKEY TROT PE
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, bean bags, small balls, hula 
hoops, pool noodles, jump ropes
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY  
ENDURANCE 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
TURKEY TAG
One student is the 
“farmer” and tries to 
tag the “turkeys.” 
Turkeys freeze when 
tagged and must be 
freed by another.
FEATHER 
BALANCE RELAY
Students balance a 
bean bag (“feather”) 
on their head while 
walking or hopping 
to a cone and back.
PUMPKIN 
PATCH PICK-UP
Students race to 
collect scattered 
bean bags 
(pumpkins) and 
return them to their 
team’s hula hoop 
“patch.”
TURKEY 
WADDLE RACE 
Students place a 
small ball between 
their knees and 
“waddle” like a 
turkey to a ﬁnish 
line without 
dropping it.
  
GRATITUDE 
FREEZE DANCE
Students dance to 
music. When the 
music stops, they 
freeze and share one 
thing they’re 
thankful for with a 
partner.
SKILL BUILDERS  
GOBBLE RUN 
RELAY 
FEATHER TOSS 
CHALLENGE
TURKEY 
BALANCE WALK 
Teams take turns 
running a short 
distance while 
making turkey gobble 
sounds. The next 
runner starts when 
tagged.
Students toss bean 
bags (“feathers”) into 
hula hoops placed at 
different distances to 
practice accuracy 
and control.
 
 Students balance on 
a pool noodle 
“beam” while 
ﬂapping their arms 
like wings. If they 
lose balance, they 
start over.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
TURKEY TROT CHALLENGE
Divide students into teams and set up a 
relay-style obstacle course. Students 
complete tasks like hopping over “corn 
stalks” (cones), crawling under “barn 
beams” (pool noodles), and balancing a 
“feather” (bean bag) on their hand. At the 
end, they “gobble” loudly and run back to 
tag the next teammate. The goal is for the 
entire team to complete the course quickly 
and with controlled movements. Celebrate 
with a group “Turkey Gobble Cheer” at the 
end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
13
TURKEY TROT PE

tag the next teammate. The goal is for the
entire team to complete the course quickly
and with controlled movements. Celebrate
with a group “Turkey Gobble Cheer” at the
end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
13
TURKEY TROT PE

fiches.title: HOLIDAYS AROUND THE WORLD
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
14
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, bean bags, hula hoops, 
scarves, small balls, jump ropes
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY  
CULTURAL 
AWARENESS  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
DIWALI LIGHT 
RUN
Students balance a 
bean bag (“light”) on 
their palm as they 
weave through cones 
to light up “stations” 
(hoops).
DREIDEL SPIN 
RELAY
Students take turns 
spinning like a 
dreidel, running to 
tag the next 
teammate. First 
team to ﬁnish wins.
ST. LUCIA 
CANDLE WALK
Students balance a 
bean bag (“candle”) 
on their head while 
walking along a 
straight or curved 
path.
 
CHRISTMAS 
TREE TOSS
Students toss bean 
bags (ornaments) 
onto hula hoops or 
cones arranged as 
“Christmas trees” 
from increasing. 
KWANZAA 
KINARA PASS
Students carefully 
pass a small ball 
(“candle”) down a 
line of teammates 
without dropping it, 
emphasizing control 
and teamwork.
SKILL BUILDERS  
MENORAH 
CANDLE RELAY
SANTA SACK 
RELAY
LANTERN TOSS 
(CHINESE NEW 
YEAR) 
Students run to place 
“candles” (bean 
bags) into hula hoops 
arranged as a 
menorah. Teams race 
to light up their 
menorah ﬁrst.
Students hop across 
the gym holding a 
bean bag (“gift”) 
while keeping 
balance. Switch 
teammates for the 
return trip.
 Students toss 
scarves or bean bags 
into hoops 
representing lanterns, 
focusing on 
controlled and 
accurate throws.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
HOLIDAY TREASURE HUNT
Scatter “treasures” (bean bags, scarves, or 
small balls) around the gym, each 
representing a holiday tradition (e.g., Diwali 
light, Christmas gift, Kwanzaa candle, 
Hanukkah dreidel). Assign teams a speciﬁc 
holiday “treasure” to collect. Teams can 
only pick up one treasure at a time and 
must place it in their “holiday zone” (hula 
hoop). Once all treasures are gathered, 
teams work together to sort and organize 
them. Add a challenge: teams must move in 
speciﬁc ways (e.g., skipping, hopping) as 
they collect.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com

fiches.title: VALENTINE PARTNER PLAY
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Equipment: Cones, bean bags, hula 
hoops, small balls, balloons, chalk
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY  
COMMUNICATION   
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CUPID'S ARROW 
TOSS
Teams toss colored 
bean bags into hula 
hoops in the center of 
the gym, symbolizing 
Cupid’s arrows 
ﬁnding “true love.”
LOVE LINES 
RELAY
Students race to 
draw connecting 
lines on a 
heart-shaped outline 
(chalk or tape), 
passing the chalk to 
their teammate like 
a baton.
VALENTINE’S 
BALLOON RACE
Partners tap a 
balloon in the air 
while moving it to a 
designated area 
(e.g., a crate). They 
can’t hold or catch 
the balloon.
 
HEART-TO-HE
ART PASS
Partners stand 
back-to-back and 
pass a small ball 
(“heart”) around 
their waists without 
dropping it. Switch 
directions after 30 
seconds.
BEAN BAG 
DELIVERY
Partners work 
together to carry a 
bean bag (“love 
letter”) using only 
their elbows, 
delivering it to a hula 
hoop target without 
dropping it.
SKILL BUILDERS  
HEART 
BALANCE WALK
  
BALLOON POP 
RELAY
HULA HOOP 
HUG RACE
  
Partners balance a 
small ball (“heart”) 
on a pool noodle 
while walking 
carefully along a 
winding path
 of cones
Pairs run to a 
“Valentine station” 
and pop a balloon by 
sitting on it together 
before racing back to 
tag the next pair.
 
 Partners stand inside 
a shared hula hoop 
and work together to 
move across the gym 
while holding the 
hoop in place.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
VALENTINE’S CARD 
DELIVERY
Divide students into two teams. Set up 
“delivery stations” (cones) across the gym, 
representing mailboxes. Each team works 
in pairs to deliver “Valentine cards” (bean 
bags) one at a time to each mailbox. 
Partners can only move while holding 
hands or linking arms. After delivering a 
card, they return for another and continue 
until all mailboxes are ﬁlled. The ﬁrst team 
to successfully deliver all their cards wins. 
This game emphasizes teamwork, 
communication, and strategy while keeping 
students moving.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
15
VALENTINE PARTNER PLAY

fiches.title: SHAMROCK SHUFFLE
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, bean bags, hula hoops, 
scarves, small balls, jump ropes
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY  
ACCURACY   
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
LUCKY CLOVER 
HOP
Lay four hula hoops 
in a clover shape. 
Students hop with 
both feet into each 
hoop, then back out. 
Add challenges like 
hopping on one foot.
TIC-TAC-TOSS
Students toss “gold 
coins” (can be 
plastic coins or bean 
bags) into a 
tic-tac-toe grid and 
try to get 3 in a row.
LEPRECHAUN 
TAG
Two students are 
Leprechauns 
(taggers) trying to 
catch the “gold 
coins” (students). 
Tagged students 
perform 10 jumping 
jacks before 
rejoining.
RAINBOW 
RELAY
Students carry bean 
bags (rainbow 
“colors”) one at a 
time and place 
them into 
matching-colored 
cones across the 
gym.
GOLD COIN 
BALANCE
Students balance a 
bean bag (“gold 
coin”) on their heads 
while walking along 
a curved cone path 
without dropping it.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
OVER THE 
RAINBOW 
CLOVER 
CRAWL 
CHALLENGE
GOLD GRAB 
RELAY
 
Students take turns 
jumping over low 
obstacles (pool 
noodles) placed as 
“rainbows” on the 
ﬂoor. Increase height 
for added challenge.
Students crawl under 
a line of pool noodles 
or cones set up as 
arches, staying low 
and maintaining 
control.
 
 Teams race to collect 
bean bags (gold) from 
the center of the gym 
one at a time and 
bring them back to 
their team’s “pot” 
(hula hoop).
LARGE GROUP GAME  
LEPRECHAUN’S TREASURE 
HUNT
Set up cones, hula hoops, and obstacles 
around the gym to create a “treasure hunt” 
course. Scatter small balls, bean bags, or 
paper “gold coins” throughout the space. 
Students work in teams to navigate the 
course and collect as much treasure as 
possible while avoiding the “Leprechauns” 
(two students acting as taggers). If a player 
gets tagged, they must perform an exercise 
(e.g., 10 squats) before returning to play. 
The team with the most treasure at the end 
wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
SHAMROCK SHUFFLE

fiches.title: SOCCER SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Soccer balls, cones, hula hoops, 
pinnies, small goals (or cones for goals)
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
DRIBBLING
PASSING 
ACCURACY 
COORDINATION
TEAMWORK 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CONE DRIBBLE 
CHALLENGE    
Students weave a 
soccer ball through a 
line of cones using 
short, controlled 
touches with their 
feet.
PASS AND 
MOVE    
Partners pass the 
ball back and 
forth. After each 
pass, they move 
to a new position.
GOALIE TARGET 
PRACTICE
Students take turns 
aiming a ball at a 
cone or small goal, 
working on 
controlled kicks.  
SOCCER 
FREEZE TAG    
One student is “it” 
and chases players. 
Students can freeze 
themselves by 
stopping the ball 
under their foot. 
CIRCLE DRIBBLE
Students dribble 
their ball around a 
large circle (marked 
by cones), focusing 
on maintaining 
control while staying 
inside the boundary.
SKILL BUILDERS  
PARTNER 
PASSING 
DRIBBLE AND 
SHOOT
TRAP AND PASS 
  
 
Partners stand a set 
distance apart and 
pass the ball back 
and forth using the 
inside of their foot. 
Increase distance for 
a challenge.
 
Students dribble a 
soccer ball through a 
cone path and take a 
shot at a small goal. 
Focus on accuracy 
and control.
 
One partner gently 
rolls the ball to the 
other, who traps it 
with their foot and 
returns the pass. 
Switch roles after a 
few rounds.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
4-CORNER SOCCER 
CHALLENGE  
Divide students into four teams, each 
starting at a different corner of the gym. 
Place a small goal or cone target in each 
corner. The game begins with a ball in the 
center. Teams send one player at a time 
to dribble, pass, or shoot the ball toward 
their team’s goal while defending against 
others. Players rotate in after each turn. 
Teams earn points for successfully scoring 
in their designated corner goal. This 
game focuses on teamwork, dribbling, 
and strategic movement.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
SOCCER SKILLS & DRILLS

fiches.title: BASKETBALL FUNDAMENTALS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Soccer balls, cones, hula hoops, 
pinnies, small goals (or cones for goals)
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
DRIBBLING
PASSING 
SHOOTING
COORDINATION
TEAMWORK 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
DRIBBLE FREEZE  
Students dribble 
around the gym. 
When the teacher 
calls “freeze,” they 
stop and hold the ball 
in place, focusing on 
control.
FIGURE-8 
DRIBBLES   
Students dribble 
the ball in a 
ﬁgure-8 motion 
around their legs, 
staying in a squat 
position to 
improve control.
HOOP 
SHOOTING 
SPOTS
Place poly spots 
around the gym. 
Students take shots 
from different spots 
using a proper 
shooting form.
BOUNCE PASS 
RELAY  
Teams line up and 
practice bounce 
passes while 
moving forward, 
passing to the next 
teammate until 
reaching the ﬁnish 
line.
BALL CONTROL 
CHALLENGE
Students dribble in 
place, alternating 
hands when the 
teacher says “switch” 
or adding challenges 
like dribbling while 
kneeling.
SKILL BUILDERS  
CONE WEAVE 
DRIBBLE  
PARTNER 
CHEST PASS
WALL 
SHOOTING 
 
Students dribble the 
ball through a series 
of cones, maintaining 
control and using 
their ﬁngertips. Focus 
on keeping heads up.
awareness.  
 
Partners stand at 
increasing distances 
and practice chest 
passes, focusing on 
hand placement, 
stepping into the 
pass, and accuracy.
 
One partner gently 
rolls the ball to the 
other, who traps it 
with their foot and 
returns the pass. 
Switch roles after a 
few rounds.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
BASKETBALL BOWLING BLITZ 
Divide the students into small teams. 
Place cones or bowling pins in a row at 
one end of the gym (1 set per team). Each 
team starts at the opposite end with a 
basketball. On “go,” one player from 
each team dribbles to a designated line 
and takes a shot (or roll) to knock down 
their team’s cones. If they miss, they 
retrieve their ball, dribble back, and tag 
the next teammate to try. The game 
continues until one team knocks over all 
their cones.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
19
BASKETBALL FUNDAMENTALS

fiches.title: VOLLEYBALL BASICS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Volleyballs (lightweight options like beach balls 
for beginners), cones, hula hoops, poly spots, 
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
PASSING 
SERVING
ACCURACY
COORDINATION
TEAMWORK 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
VOLLEY UP 
CHALLENGE  
Students work in 
pairs to volley a 
beach ball back and 
forth, counting how 
many hits they can 
keep it in the air.
SERVING 
LINE-UP  
Students practice 
underhand 
serving from a 
starting line, 
aiming to get the 
ball into a hoop 
or designated 
target on the 
opposite side.
CONE TARGET 
PASS
Students stand at a 
distance and aim to 
pass the ball (bump 
or set) to knock over 
a cone placed 
across from them.
HULA HOOP 
ACCURACY
Students bump or 
volley the ball to a 
partner who must 
catch it while 
standing in a hula 
hoop. Emphasize 
controlled passes.
BALLOON BUMP 
RELAY
Teams keep a balloon 
in the air using 
bumping motions 
while moving it to a 
ﬁnish line. If it 
touches the ground, 
they restart.
SKILL BUILDERS  
BUMP AND 
CATCH 
WALL SET 
DRILL
SERVE AND 
CHASE 
 
Students practice 
bumping the ball 
(forearm pass) to 
themselves and 
catching it. Increase 
the number of bumps 
before catching. 
 
Students stand a 
short distance from a 
wall and practice 
setting the ball to hit 
a taped target on the 
wall.
 
Students serve the 
ball underhand to a 
speciﬁc target, then 
run to retrieve it as 
quickly as possible. 
Focus on proper form 
and control.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
VOLLEYBALL TARGET 
CHALLENGE 
Divide the class into small teams. Set up 
targets (hula hoops or poly spots) on one 
side of the gym. Teams stand behind a 
line on the opposite side and take turns 
bumping, setting, or underhand serving 
the ball toward the targets. Each target is 
worth a speciﬁc number of points based 
on difﬁculty (e.g., closer hoops = 1 point, 
further hoops = 3 points). Players retrieve 
their balls after each turn and pass them 
to the next teammate. The team with the 
most points at the end wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
VOLLEYBALL BASICS

sections.Equipment:
3-5
Volleyballs (lightweight options like beach balls
for beginners), cones, hula hoops, poly spots,
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
PASSING
SERVING
ACCURACY
COORDINATION
TEAMWORK
INSTANT ACTIVITIES
VOLLEY UP
CHALLENGE
Students work in
pairs to volley a
beach ball back and
forth, counting how
many hits they can
keep it in the air.
SERVING
LINE-UP
Students practice
underhand
serving from a
starting line,
aiming to get the
ball into a hoop
or designated
target on the
opposite side.
CONE TARGET
PASS
Students stand at a
distance and aim to
pass the ball (bump
or set) to knock over
a cone placed
across from them.
HULA HOOP
ACCURACY
Students bump or
volley the ball to a
partner who must
catch it while
standing in a hula
hoop. Emphasize
controlled passes.
BALLOON BUMP
RELAY
Teams keep a balloon
in the air using
bumping motions
while moving it to a
ﬁnish line. If it
touches the ground,
they restart.
SKILL BUILDERS
BUMP AND
CATCH
WALL SET
DRILL
SERVE AND
CHASE
Students practice
bumping the ball
(forearm pass) to
themselves and
catching it. Increase
the number of bumps
before catching.
Students stand a
short distance from a
wall and practice
setting the ball to hit
a taped target on the
wall.
Students serve the
ball underhand to a
speciﬁc target, then
run to retrieve it as
quickly as possible.
Focus on proper form
and control.
LARGE GROUP GAME
VOLLEYBALL TARGET
CHALLENGE
Divide the class into small teams. Set up
targets (hula hoops or poly spots) on one
side of the gym. Teams stand behind a
line on the opposite side and take turns
bumping, setting, or underhand serving
the ball toward the targets. Each target is
worth a speciﬁc number of points based
on difﬁculty (e.g., closer hoops = 1 point,
further hoops = 3 points). Players retrieve
their balls after each turn and pass them
to the next teammate. The team with the
most points at the end wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
VOLLEYBALL BASICS

fiches.title: INTRO TO RACKET GAMES
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Foam paddles or lightweight rackets, balloons, 
small foam balls, cones, hula hoops, poly spots
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
 HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION 
CONTROL 
ACCURACY
AGILITY
BALANCE
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BALLOON TAP 
CHALLENGE  
Students use paddles 
to tap a balloon into 
the air while moving 
around the gym. 
Emphasize control 
and keeping the 
balloon up.
RACKET 
DRIBBLE  
Students bounce 
a small foam ball 
on their paddle as 
they walk or 
weave through 
cones. Focus on 
control and 
balance.
HULA HOOP 
RALLY
Pairs stand on 
opposite sides of a 
hula hoop and aim 
to hit a foam ball 
back and forth into 
the hoop.
TARGET SERVE
Students practice 
serving (hitting) a 
ball from behind a 
line, aiming to land 
it into designated 
targets like cones 
or hoops.
SWITCH AND HIT
Students pair up and 
gently hit a foam ball 
back and forth. After 
every hit, they switch 
positions or sides to 
keep moving.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
WALL RALLY 
PADDLE 
BALANCE 
PARTNER TAP 
CHALLENGE
 
Students hit a foam 
ball against a wall 
using controlled 
motions. Focus on 
keeping the ball low 
and under control. 
 
 
Students balance a 
ball on their paddle 
while walking along a 
cone or poly spot 
path. Add turns for a 
challenge.
 
Partners stand close 
together and tap a 
balloon or foam ball 
back and forth, 
increasing distance 
as they improve.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
RACKET RELAY RALLY
Divide the class into teams and set up a 
relay-style course. Each student must 
carry a ball on their paddle through a 
series of cones or obstacles without 
dropping it. When they reach the end, 
they lightly hit the ball into a designated 
“goal” (hula hoop or cone target). They 
then return to tag the next teammate in 
line. If a student drops the ball, they must 
reset to the starting point. The ﬁrst team 
to have all players complete the course 
wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
21
INTRO TO RACKET GAMES
