fiches.title: BALANCE & COORDINATION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
28
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Bean Bags, Balance Beams, Cones, 
Agility Ladders
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
AGILITY
BODY 
AWARENESS 
CONTROL  
  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CRAB WALK 
PATH  
Set up cones to form 
a path. Students 
crab-walk around the 
cones, maintaining a 
steady pace and 
controlling their 
movements to avoid 
tipping.
SIDEWAYS SHUFFLE  
Students move sideways 
along a taped line or beam, 
focusing on small, 
controlled steps without 
crossing their feet. Builds 
lateral coordination and 
balance.
ONE-LEG 
BALANCE  
Students stand on 
one foot, holding 
their balance for 
ﬁve seconds. 
Repeat on the other 
foot, gradually 
increasing the hold 
time to improve 
control.  
  
SINGLE-LEG 
HOPSCOTCH
Create a hopscotch 
pattern and 
challenge students 
to hop through on 
one foot without 
losing balance, 
switching feet 
halfway. Emphasizes 
single-leg strength 
and coordination.
  
  
TIGHTROPE WALK  
Use a piece of tape as a 
“tightrope” across the gym. 
Students walk heel-to-toe 
with arms out, as if on a 
high wire, focusing on slow, 
deliberate steps.
SKILL BUILDERS  
AGILITY 
LADDER STEPS  
BALANCE 
BEAM 
CHALLENGE  
CIRCLE SKIPS  
 
Students step in and 
out of each square in 
an agility ladder, 
focusing on fast, 
controlled footwork. 
Experiment with 
different patterns, like 
single-leg hops or 
sideways steps.  
Set up balance beams 
at different heights. 
Students walk across, 
focusing on keeping 
their eyes forward and 
arms out for stability.  
 
 
 
 Place cones in a 
large circle. Students 
skip around the circle, 
coordinating their 
movements and 
maintaining a 
consistent rhythm.  
 
KEEP YOUR BODY 
STEADY  
FOCUS ON 
CONTROLLED 
MOVEMENTS  
MAINTAIN 
POSTURE AND 
BALANCE
LARGE GROUP GAME  
BALANCE RELAY  
 Divide students into teams. Each student walks 
across a balance beam while balancing a small 
object (like a bean bag) on their head. Upon 
ﬁnishing, they pass the object to the next 
teammate. The goal is to complete the relay with 
balance and patience.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
28

fiches.title: FLEXIBILITY & STRETCHING
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Yoga Mats (optional,) Cones  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
FLEXIBILITY
BODY AWARENESS 
CONTROL  
RELAXATION 
FOCUS  
  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
REACH FOR THE 
SKY  
Students stand with 
feet hip-width apart 
and slowly reach both 
arms up to the sky, 
then lower arms down 
and repeat. Focus on 
controlled breathing.  
TOE TOUCH 
STRETCH  
 Students stand 
tall, slowly bend 
forward to touch 
their toes, and 
hold for ﬁve 
seconds. 
Emphasize 
keeping legs 
straight and 
relaxed.  
BUTTERFLY SIT  
Students sit with 
soles of feet 
together and gently 
press knees down 
toward the ﬂoor, 
holding the stretch 
for a few breaths.  
 
 
SIDE BEND 
STRETCH  
Students stand 
with feet apart and 
reach one arm 
overhead to the 
side, stretching the 
side of their body. 
Repeat on the 
other side.  
 
  
CAT-COW STRETCH  
Students start on hands and 
knees, arching their backs up 
like a “cat,” then dipping 
down like a “cow.” Repeat 
slowly, focusing on 
breathing.  
SKILL BUILDERS  
FORWARD 
FOLD WALK  
SEATED 
FORWARD 
REACH  
SHOULDER 
ROLLS  
Students bend 
forward and “walk” 
hands forward into a 
stretch, then back to 
their toes. Helps 
lengthen hamstrings 
and promotes 
ﬂexibility in the spine.  
Students sit with legs 
extended in front and 
reach toward their 
toes, keeping their 
backs straight. 
Encourage them to 
reach a little further 
with each breath.  
 
Students stand and 
roll their shoulders 
forward, then 
backward in slow, 
controlled circles. 
Builds shoulder 
ﬂexibility and 
releases tension.  
STRETCH GENTLY 
WITHOUT 
BOUNCING  
BREATHE 
DEEPLY AND 
SLOWLY  
FOCUS ON 
LENGTHENING 
EACH MUSCLE
LARGE GROUP GAME  
STRETCH CIRCLE  
Students form a circle and take turns leading a 
stretch. Each student introduces a stretch, and the 
group follows, encouraging participation and 
creativity in movement.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
29
FLEXIBILITY & STRETCHING

fiches.title: TUMBLING & ROLLING
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Mats, Cones  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
LOG ROLLS  
Students lie on their 
sides and roll across the 
mat like a log, keeping 
their bodies straight. 
Encourage controlled 
rolling to build spatial 
awareness.  
FORWARD 
ROLL  
Students crouch 
down, tuck their 
chins, and gently 
roll forward over 
their heads, using 
their hands to help 
guide. Focus on 
keeping the roll 
smooth.  
 
 
EGG ROLLS  
 Students hug their 
knees tightly and 
rock back and forth 
on their backs 
without tipping over. 
Promotes core 
control and 
balance.  
BEAR WALK  
Students move 
across the mat on 
hands and feet, 
with hips high like a 
bear. Reinforces 
coordination and 
builds upper body 
strength.  
 
SIDE ROLLS  
 Students lie on their 
sides, arms extended 
overhead, and roll from 
side to side without 
bending their bodies, 
practicing controlled 
side movements.  
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
BACKWARDS 
ROLL  
CARTWHEEL 
STEPS  
BALANCE BEAM 
CRAWL  
Students sit with 
hands on the mat, 
tuck their chins, and 
roll gently backward 
over their shoulders, 
keeping hands in 
position for control.  
 
Students practice the 
basic steps of a 
cartwheel by placing 
hands on the mat and 
stepping feet over in 
a controlled “half” 
cartwheel motion.  
 
Place a low balance 
beam or line of tape. 
Students crawl 
across, focusing on 
steady movement 
and body control 
without tipping off.  
MOVE WITH 
CONTROL AND 
FOCUS  
KEEP YOUR 
BODY TIGHT 
AND ALIGNED  
  
USE HANDS FOR 
SUPPORT WHEN 
NEEDED
LARGE GROUP GAME  
TUMBLE RELAY  
Divide students into teams. Each student performs 
a simple tumbling move (e.g., forward roll, bear 
walk) down the mat and tags the next teammate. 
Teams work together to complete the relay, 
encouraging body control and coordination.  
BODY 
CONTROL 
COORDINATION
SPATIAL 
AWARENESS  
CONFIDENCE  
   
   
BALANCE
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
TUMBLING & ROLLING

fiches.title: JUMP ROPE FUN
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Jump Ropes, Cones  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
 COORDINATION 
RHYTHM 
ENDURANCE  
BALANCE
BODY 
CONTROL  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BASIC BOUNCE  
Students shake the 
parachute edges to bounce 
bean bags (popcorn) into 
the air, aiming to keep 
them on the parachute as 
they pop up and down
 
SIDE SWING 
PRACTICE  
Students swing the 
rope from side to 
side without 
jumping, getting 
used to controlling 
the rope’s 
movement and 
rhythm.  
 
HIGH KNEES 
JUMP  
Students jump rope 
while lifting their 
knees high with 
each jump. This 
variation helps build 
coordination and 
strengthens leg 
muscles.  
BUDDY ROPE WAVE  
  In pairs, one student 
swings a long rope on the 
ground in a wave pattern 
while the other jumps over 
it, working on timing and 
cooperation.  
  
SINGLE FOOT 
HOP  
Students jump rope 
on one foot, 
alternating feet 
halfway through. 
Focus on balance 
and control with 
each hop.  
SKILL BUILDERS  
DOUBLE 
BOUNCE  
BACKWARD 
JUMP  
SIDE-TO-SIDE 
JUMPS  
Students try a double 
bounce for each 
jump, practicing 
timing and rhythm. 
Start slowly, then 
gradually increase 
speed as they get 
comfortable.  
Students turn the 
rope backward and 
jump over it in 
reverse. Focus on 
timing the backward 
swing and controlling 
the jump.  
  
Students jump rope 
while moving 
side-to-side with each 
jump. This adds 
lateral movement, 
improving agility and 
coordination.  
KEEP A STEADY 
RHYTHM  
FOCUS ON 
TIMING THE 
JUMP  
HOLD HANDLES 
FIRMLY FOR 
CONTROL
LARGE GROUP GAME  
JUMP ROPE RELAY  
Divide students into teams. Each student runs to 
the end of the gym and completes a set number of 
jumps, then returns and passes the rope to the 
next teammate. Teams work together to complete 
the relay quickly.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
31
JUMP ROPE FUN

fiches.title: ANIMAL MOVEMENTS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
32
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Cones, Hoops  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
COORDINATION
BALANCE 
AGILITY 
BODY 
AWARENESS  
STRENGTH  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BEAR CRAWL IN 
PLACE  
 Students get on hands 
and feet with hips high 
and crawl in place. 
Focus on stability and 
balanced movement in 
a stationary position.
FROG JUMP 
FORWARD  
Students squat and 
leap forward, 
landing softly in a 
squat again, like a 
frog. Repeat in short, 
controlled hops.
CRAB SIDE 
SHUFFLE  
Students sit with 
hands and feet on 
the ground, lifting 
hips up, then shufﬂe 
side-to-side like a 
crab. Focus on 
coordination.
BUNNY HOPS  
Students keep feet 
together and make 
small hops in place 
like a bunny, aiming 
for quick, light jumps 
with controlled 
landings.
  
BUTTERFLY FLAP  
Students hold arms out 
and gently ﬂap them up 
and down, as if ﬂying. 
This simple movement 
can help warm up 
shoulder muscles and 
improve body control.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
SNAKE SLITHER 
PATH  
ELEPHANT 
STOMP  
LEAPING 
GAZELLE  
Set up cones in a 
winding path. 
Students lie on their 
stomachs and slither 
through the path 
using only their arms, 
developing core 
strength and control.
Students hold a cone 
or small object as 
their “trunk,” bend 
forward, and walk in 
heavy, stomping 
steps to a marker. 
Builds rhythm and 
controlled movement.
 
 
Students practice 
running a few steps, 
then leaping as high 
and far as they can, 
like a gazelle. Focus 
on leg power, soft 
landings, and 
graceful movements.
 
MOVE WITH 
CONTROL AND 
BALANCE  
USE FULL-BODY 
MOVEMENT FOR 
EACH ANIMAL  
BE AWARE OF 
SPACE AND 
OTHERS  
LARGE GROUP GAME  
ANIMAL RELAY  
Divide students into teams. Each student performs 
a speciﬁc animal movement (e.g., frog jump, bear 
crawl) down a lane, tags the next teammate, and 
joins at the end. Teams encourage one another to 
ﬁnish together, promoting coordination and 
teamwork.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
32
ANIMAL MOVEMENTS

fiches.title: NATURE & OUTDOOR EXPLORATION
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Cones, Bean Bags, Hoops  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
BALANCE 
COORDINATION
OBSERVATION
CURIOSITY 
SPATIAL 
AWARENESS  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
LOG BALANCE 
WALK  
Students pretend to 
walk along a “fallen 
log” (a line of cones 
or chalk line on the 
ground), balancing 
with arms 
outstretched to avoid 
“falling.”
LEAF JUMP  
Scatter leaves or small 
markers around the ﬁeld. 
Students jump from leaf to 
leaf, practicing agility and 
balance as they explore the 
outdoor space.
WIND RUN  
When the teacher 
calls “wind,” 
students run with 
arms out, 
pretending to be 
blown by the wind. 
Call “freeze” to 
make them stop 
and balance in 
place.
ROCK HOP  
Place hoops or 
markers on the 
ground as “rocks.” 
Students hop from 
rock to rock, moving 
around the yard and 
avoiding the “water” 
in between.
SUNFLOWER 
STRETCH 
Students stand with feet 
apart and arms reaching up 
like sunﬂowers. On 
“sunshine,” they sway from 
side to side, bending and 
stretching toward the “sun” 
(a speciﬁc direction).
SKILL BUILDERS  
NATURE 
TREASURE 
HUNT  
TREE POSE 
RELAY  
 
ANIMAL 
TRACKING PATH  
Hide small objects 
(pinecones, rocks, or 
bean bags) around 
the ﬁeld. Students 
work in pairs or 
teams to ﬁnd them, 
practicing teamwork 
and observation.
Divide students into 
teams. Each student 
runs to a designated 
spot, holds a tree pose 
for ﬁve seconds, then 
runs back to tag the 
next teammate.
  
 
 
Set up a winding path 
with cones or natural 
markers. Students 
pretend to be animal 
trackers, carefully 
following the “trail” 
without straying off, 
practicing focus and 
coordination.
MOVE CAREFULLY ON 
NATURAL SURFACES  
 
OBSERVE 
DETAILS IN THE 
ENVIRONMENT  
  
RESPECT EACH 
OTHER’S SPACE 
AND NATURE
LARGE GROUP GAME  
OUTDOOR OBSTACLE 
EXPLORATION  
Set up an obstacle course on the ﬁeld with natural 
elements: weave through trees, hop over “logs” 
(cones), or crawl under “branches” (ropes or pool 
noodles). Students work through the course 
individually or in small groups, practicing agility, 
balance, and body control.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
33
NATURE & OUTDOOR EXPLORATION

individually or in small groups, practicing agility,
balance, and body control.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
33
NATURE & OUTDOOR EXPLORATION

fiches.title: HEALTHY HABITS & NUTRITION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
34
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
K-2
Cones, Bean Bags, Hoops, Food, 
Flashcards  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
HEALTHY 
CHOICES 
BODY AWARENESS 
COORDINATION  
 TEAMWORK 
KNOWLEDGE 
OF 
NUTRITION  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FRUIT & VEGGIE 
RELAY  
Place fruit and veggie 
ﬂashcards at one end 
of the gym. Students 
run, pick a card, and 
bring it back to their 
team. Discuss why it’s a 
healthy choice.
FOOD GROUP 
SORTING  
Scatter food cards 
around the gym. 
Students move 
around, picking up 
cards and placing 
them in labeled 
hoops (e.g., fruits, 
vegetables, 
grains), learning 
about food groups.
HEALTHY PLATE 
HOP  
Draw circles with 
chalk or use hoops 
as “plates” around 
the gym, labeled 
with food groups. 
Students hop to the 
right “plate” when a 
food item is called 
out.
RAINBOW RUN  
Students jog in a 
circle, and when 
you call out a 
color, they name a 
healthy food of 
that color before 
continuing. Helps 
build awareness of 
diverse, colorful 
foods.
 
 
GO & WHOA FOODS  
Students move around the 
gym as if in a “grocery store.” 
When you call “Go,” they 
move quickly toward healthy 
foods; on “Whoa,” they 
pause near less nutritious 
options.  
SKILL BUILDERS  
BALANCED 
MEAL MATCH  
SNACK 
CHOICE SORT
  
  
HEALTHY HABITS 
OBSTACLE 
COURSE  
  
 
Provide each student 
with food cards 
representing different 
food groups. They 
work in small groups 
to create a balanced 
“meal,” explaining 
why each item was 
chosen.
Set up “healthy” and 
“unhealthy” snack 
piles. Students sort 
food cards into the 
correct pile, then 
discuss why certain 
snacks are better 
choices.
Set up a course with 
stations labeled with 
healthy habits 
(hydration, sleep, 
balanced meals). 
Students complete 
each “habit” by doing 
simple movements 
(e.g., drinking water 
station = three 
jumps).
CHOOSE 
NUTRITIOUS 
FOODS DAILY  
 
IDENTIFY FOOD 
GROUPS AND 
BALANCED 
MEALS  
UNDERSTAND 
HEALTHY HABITS 
BEYOND FOOD
LARGE GROUP GAME  
NUTRITION TAG  
Select a few students as “sugar” or “junk food” 
taggers, while others are “fruits” and “vegetables.” 
When tagged, students freeze until another “fruit” 
or “vegetable” unfreezes them by naming a 
healthy habit or food choice. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
34
HEALTHY HABITS & NUTRITION

fiches.title: INTRODUCTION
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
3
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to PE Curriculum Maps for Grades 6-8, your ultimate resource for planning engaging, effective, 
and dynamic physical education units!
Whether you’re a PE teacher, coach, parent, fitness instructor, or after-school program director, this 
resource provides ready-to-go unit plans designed to challenge students, build skills, and foster teamwork 
in fun and meaningful ways.
While these curriculum maps are specifically created for grades 6-8, they are flexible and can easily 
be adapted for younger or older students. Each unit includes age-appropriate activities that blend skill 
development, physical fitness, and social-emotional learning, ensuring all students are active, engaged, 
and progressing.
The curriculum is organized into 
1.	 Seasonal Units – Themed activities that connect movement to seasons, holidays, and creative 
traditions.
2.	 Sports Skills Units – Units that develop fundamental sports skills like soccer, volleyball, and 
basketball with an emphasis on strategy and gameplay.
3.	 Fitness & Conditioning Units – Activities that focus on strength, endurance, agility, and 
cardiovascular fitness to build lifelong healthy habits.
4.	 Teamwork & SEL Units – Cooperative challenges that emphasize leadership, communication, 
problem-solving, and sportsmanship in a supportive environment.
5.	 Lifetime Activities Units – Fun, accessible activities like disc golf, bocce ball, and yoga that promote 
lifelong physical recreation and wellness.
6.	 Backyard Games Units – Classic and creative games like cornhole, ladder ball, spikeball, and 
kickball that combine skill-building with friendly competition.
Each curriculum map is carefully structured into the following components:
•	
Learning Objectives – Five clear goals for the unit.
•	
Instant Activities – Five quick, engaging warm-ups to get students moving right away.
•	
Skill Builders – Three focused activities to teach and develop key skills.
•	
Large Group Game – A fun and active culminating game that reinforces the skills of the unit while 
encouraging teamwork and strategy.
With 30 comprehensive units, this resource equips you with everything you need to plan an engaging, 
challenging, and rewarding year of PE classes. 
Each lesson is designed to be adaptable for different spaces, group sizes, and ability levels, giving you the 
tools to motivate middle school students to move, improve, and have fun!
Let’s dive in and make this year of PE the best yet—there’s movement, fitness, and skill-building ahead!

fiches.title: BACK TO SCHOOL KICKOFF
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, jump ropes, hula hoops, pool 
noodles, small balls, bean bags
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
FITNESS 
INTEGRATION
COMMUNICATION 
COORDINATION 
PROBLEM-
SOLVING
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FAST FIVE 
FITNESS TAG
Students must complete 
5 exercises (e.g., 5 
jumping jacks, squats, 
lunges) before returning 
to the game after being 
tagged.
HUMAN 
CONVEYOR 
BELT 
Teams line up and 
pass a small ball 
down the line 
using only speciﬁc 
body parts (e.g., 
shoulders, elbows, 
feet).
OVER-UNDER 
RACE
Teams race to pass 
a ball over their 
heads and under 
their legs in line 
formation. Fastest 
team wins.
TEAM SHAPE 
BUILDER  
Teams use ropes or 
cones to create 
shapes (e.g., 
square, triangle) as 
quickly and 
accurately as 
possible.
FITNESS 
TIC-TAC-TOE    
Teams complete 
exercises (e.g., 5 
squats) to place a 
marker on a giant 
tic-tac-toe grid.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
CONE 
COLLECTORS
BALL BALANCE 
WALK
COMMUNICATION 
CIRCLE
 
Teams race to collect 
cones placed around 
the gym, completing 
10 jumping jacks 
every time they 
return one to base.
 
 
Pairs balance a small 
ball between their 
backs or hands and 
walk a set course 
together.
 
Students pass a hula 
hoop around a circle 
without letting go of 
each other’s hands, 
encouraging clear 
communication and 
strategy.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
EQUIPMENT BUILDERS RELAY
Scatter pieces of equipment (cones, ropes, 
bean bags, hoops) around the gym. Create 
four stations where students must 
complete ﬁtness tasks (e.g., 10 push-ups, 
15 jumping jacks, 10-second plank). Teams 
send one player at a time to complete a 
task and retrieve a piece of equipment. 
Once all the equipment is collected, teams 
must work together to build the tallest or 
most creative structure possible using the 
items they earned. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
6

fiches.title: WINTER SPORTS SKILLS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, pool noodles, foam balls, 
scooters, mats, poly spots, hula hoops
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
BALANCE
AGILITY
COORDINATION 
ACCURACY
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
SCOOTER SLALOM
Students navigate 
scooters through a 
zig-zag cone course, 
focusing on speed and 
balance.
HULA CURLING
Students slide hula 
hoops or rings 
across the gym 
ﬂoor to land on 
poly spots, aiming 
for accuracy and 
control.
SNOWBALL 
RELAY�Students carry 
foam balls 
("snowballs") on 
pool noodles as 
they weave 
through cones.
SPEED SKATER 
RELAY
 Students place 
both feet inside 
hula hoops and 
slide along the ﬂoor 
like a speed skater.
PENGUIN WALK 
RELAY 
Students squeeze a 
foam ball (“egg”) 
between their knees 
while waddling through 
a relay course.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
SLALOM SKI 
STEPS
SCOOTER 
BOBSLED PAIRS�TARGET TOSS 
BIATHLON
 
Students perform 
quick lateral steps 
through a series of 
cones, mimicking a 
slalom ski course. 
Focus on low, 
controlled 
In pairs, one student 
sits on a scooter 
while the other 
pushes. Switch roles 
halfway through as 
they complete a short 
marked course.
Students throw foam 
balls into targets 
(hula hoops or 
buckets) after 
completing a short 
cardio challenge, 
such as running or 
jumping jacks.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
SNOWBALL CASTLE 
DEFENSE
Divide the class into two teams. Each 
team builds a “castle” using cones, hula 
hoops, and mats as shields. Teams 
throw foam balls ("snowballs") at the 
opposing team’s castle to knock it down 
while protecting their own. If a player is 
hit by a snowball, they must complete a 
ﬁtness task (e.g., 10 jumping jacks) 
before returning to the game. Add a 
time limit for each round and rebuild the 
castles between rounds. The team with 
the fewest knocked-down pieces at the 
end wins.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
7
WINTER SPORTS SKILLS

fiches.title: OUTDOOR ADVENTURE GAMES
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, hula hoops, ropes, pool 
noodles, bean bags, small balls, 
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK
PROBLEM-
SOLVING
STRATEGY
NAVIGATION
 
ENDURANCE
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
TRAIL MARKER 
RELAY
Teams follow a set of 
cone-marked pathways 
or trails while 
completing quick 
physical challenges at 
checkpoints.
FITNESS 
SCAVENGER 
HUNT
Students locate 
ﬁtness task cards 
hidden around a 
ﬁeld and perform 
the assigned 
exercises.  
COMPASS 
CHALLENGE
Students use a 
simple map and 
compass to locate 
cones or markers 
placed around the 
ﬁeld.
TEAM 
BOULDER 
ROLL
 Teams work 
together to roll a 
large yoga ball 
(“boulder”) through 
an obstacle course.
LOG CARRY
Teams carry a pool 
noodle (“log”) on their 
shoulder while running 
through an obstacle 
course.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
TENT-BUILDING 
RELAY
TEAM BRIDGE 
CHALLENGE
NATURE 
SCAVENGER 
HUNT
 
Teams race to 
assemble and 
disassemble a “tent” 
structure using hoops, 
cones, or poles
Teams use equipment 
(ropes, cones, pool 
noodles) to build a 
“bridge” across a 
designated area. The 
bridge must support 
all teammates 
without falling.
Teams search for 
natural items (e.g., 
leaves, rocks) based 
on a checklist while 
following a marked 
trail.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
SURVIVOR CHALLENGE COURSE
Set up a multi-station challenge course that blends 
ﬁtness tasks with problem-solving. Each team starts 
at a designated station:
1.
Balance Crossing – Students walk across 
“logs” (pool noodles or tape lines) without 
stepping off.
2.
Obstacle Crawl – Teams crawl under ropes or 
through hula hoops on the ground.
3.
Bean Bag Rescue – Teams collect and toss 
bean bags into a target zone for accuracy 
points.
4.
Rope Bridge Build – Teams use ropes to 
create a “bridge” for teammates to cross 
safely.
Teams must complete each challenge together 
before moving to the next station. Time each team, 
and reward cooperation and completion rather than 
speed alone.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
8
OUTDOOR ADVENTURE GAMES

fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, pool noodles, foam balls, 
scooters, mats, poly spots, hula hoops
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAM SPIRIT
BALANCE
AGILITY
FITNESS 
CHALLENGES
 
COOPERATION
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
4-WAY 
TUG-OF-WAR
Use a cross-shaped 
rope for a 
multi-directional 
tug-of-war competition 
where teams compete 
simultaneously.
HULA HOOP 
TOSS 
CHALLENGE
Students toss hula 
hoops over cones 
or buckets, scoring 
points for longer or 
harder throws.
HULA HOOP 
ROLLING 
RELAY
Teams roll hula 
hoops through a 
marked course, 
then hand it off to 
the next teammate 
in line.
THREE-LEGGED 
RACE
Partners tie their 
legs together and 
race to the ﬁnish 
line.
EGG AND SPOON 
RELAY
Students balance a ball 
or egg (bean bag) on a 
spoon while racing to 
complete a marked 
course.
SKILL BUILDERS  
OBSTACLE 
RELAY
TEAM BUCKET 
FILL
SACK RACE 
RELAY
 
Students navigate an 
obstacle course with 
tasks like crawling 
under noodles, 
hopping over cones, 
and balancing on a 
taped line.
Teams work together 
to transfer bean bags 
or small balls into a 
bucket placed at a 
distance, using only 
speciﬁc equipment 
like pool noodles or 
hoops.
Teams compete in 
relay-style sack 
races, hopping from 
start to ﬁnish.
  
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
WATER BRIGADE RELAY
Set up two large buckets at opposite 
ends of the play area. Teams line up with 
a smaller bucket or sponge. Students 
take turns ﬁlling the small bucket with 
water from one end and running it to 
dump into the bucket at the other end. 
The team with the most water transferred 
by the end of the game wins. Add 
obstacles (e.g., cones to weave through) 
for an extra challenge. This game 
emphasizes teamwork and coordination 
while adding a refreshing element to the 
activity.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
9
FIELD DAY FUN FEST

fiches.title: SUMMER SPORTS SKILLS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
10
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Beach balls, pool noodles, cones, small 
paddles, frisbees, bean bags, hula 
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION
TEAMWORK
 
AGILITY
ACCURACY
 
MOVEMENT 
CONTROL
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BEACH BALL 
KEEP-UP
Students work in small 
groups to keep a beach 
ball in the air using only 
their hands, heads, or 
feet. 
FRISBEE 
TARGET TOSS
Students practice 
throwing frisbees 
to land in targets 
(hula hoops or 
cones) placed at 
various distances.
NOODLE BALL 
BALANCE
Students balance a 
small ball on a pool 
noodle while 
walking carefully 
across a 
designated path 
without dropping it.
BEACH BALL 
VOLLEYBALL
Teams rally a 
beach ball over a 
low net, focusing on 
controlled hits and 
keeping the ball in 
play.
WATER BALLOON 
RELAY
Students race to pass a 
water balloon down 
their team line without 
dropping it.
SKILL BUILDERS  
PADDLE TAP 
CHALLENGE
KAYAK RELAYS 
  
NOODLE 
VOLLEYBALL
 
Students practice 
gently tapping a 
foam ball or bean 
bag with a paddle to 
keep it in the air, 
focusing on control 
and accuracy.
Students sit on 
scooters and use pool 
noodles as “paddles” 
to move through a 
cone-marked course.
Students use pool 
noodles to “bump” a 
beach ball over a low 
net or rope, 
emphasizing 
teamwork and 
control.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
FRISBEE BASEBALL
Set up a baseball diamond using cones. 
The batting team takes turns throwing a 
frisbee from home plate into the ﬁeld 
while the ﬁelding team retrieves it as 
quickly as possible and passes it back to a 
designated “pitcher” at home plate to 
stop the play. If the frisbee is caught in the 
air, the batter is out; otherwise, they run 
the bases just like in traditional baseball. 
The batting team advances runners and 
scores by reaching home plate. Add hula 
hoops or zones in the ﬁeld for bonus 
points if the frisbee lands inside. Rotate 
teams after three outs.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
10
SUMMER SPORTS SKILLS

fiches.title: SOCCER SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Soccer balls, cones, poly spots, 
pinnies, small goals (or cones for goals)
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
DRIBBLING
PASSING 
SHOOTING
DEFENSIVE 
POSITIONING
TEAMWORK 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CONE DRIBBLE 
CHALLENGE   
Students weave a 
soccer ball through a 
series of cones using 
controlled dribbling, 
focusing on small, 
quick touches.
PASS AND 
MOVE    
Pairs pass the ball 
back and forth while 
moving through a 
designated area. 
Increase the 
challenge with 
distance or limited 
touches.
PROTECT THE 
BALL
Students partner up, 
and one student 
tries to shield their 
ball from their 
partner’s attempts 
to steal it. Switch 
roles after 30 
seconds.
TARGET 
PASSING    
Students pass the 
ball to knock over 
cones or hit targets 
(poly spots) from 
varying distances.
 
KEEP IT ALIVE
Groups of 4-5 pass a 
soccer ball 
continuously within a 
designated area, 
aiming to keep 
possession while 
maintaining control.
SKILL BUILDERS  
TRIANGLE 
PASSING DRILL  
1V1 DRIBBLE AND 
DEFEND
SHOOTING 
ACCURACY 
CHALLENGE 
 
Students form groups 
of three and pass the 
ball in a triangle, 
moving to a new 
position after each 
pass. Focus on 
communication and 
control.
 
Partners alternate 
roles as attacker and 
defender. The attacker 
dribbles toward a 
cone goal while the 
defender works on 
proper positioning to 
prevent scoring.
 
Students take turns 
shooting at small 
goals or targets from 
increasing distances, 
emphasizing proper 
form and placement.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
4-CORNER SOCCER 
CHALLENGE  
Set up four small goals or cone targets in 
the corners of the gym or ﬁeld. Divide the 
class into four teams. Each team works to 
pass and dribble the ball to score in any 
of the other three team’s goals while 
protecting their own corner. Players must 
focus on offensive passing, defensive 
positioning, and spatial awareness. Play 
in short rounds (3-4 minutes), then rotate 
starting positions to keep the game 
dynamic.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
12
SOCCER SKILLS & DRILLS

fiches.title: BASKETBALL SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Basketballs, cones, poly spots, hula hoops, 
pinnies, small goals or marked shooting areas
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
DRIBBLING
PASSING 
SHOOTING
DEFENSIVE 
POSITIONING
TEAMWORK 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
DRIBBLE FREEZE  
Students dribble 
around the gym. 
When the teacher 
calls “freeze,” they 
stop and hold their 
ball. Add variations 
like switching hands 
or using crossovers.
AROUND THE 
WORLD 
DRIBBLE   
Students dribble 
the ball around 
different parts of 
their body (e.g., 
waist, legs, feet), 
focusing on control 
and coordination.
PASS AND 
FOLLOW
Pairs pass the ball 
to each other while 
moving through a 
designated space, 
cutting after each 
pass to practice 
offensive 
movement.
SHOOTING 
SPOTS
Place poly spots at 
different distances 
and assign point 
values to each 
one. Students take 
shots, rotating 
through as many 
spots as possible 
in 2 minutes.
CROSSOVER 
RELAY
Students dribble 
through a cone path 
using crossovers, 
emphasizing speed 
and ball control.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
2-BALL DRIBBLE 
CHALLENGE  
PARTNER 
PASSING DRILL
LAYUP LINES 
  
 
Students practice 
dribbling two 
basketballs at the 
same time (one in 
each hand), focusing 
on control and 
rhythm.
 Partners stand facing 
each other and 
alternate chest 
passes, bounce 
passes, and overhead 
passes. Use 2 balls 
simultaneously 
distance for a 
challenge.
 
Students practice 
layups on both the 
right and left sides of 
the hoop. Focus on 
proper footwork, and 
using the backboard.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
NUMBERS BASKETBALL
Divide the class into two teams and have 
each team line up on opposite sidelines. 
Assign each student a number (e.g., 1-10) 
for their team. Place a basketball at 
center court. When the teacher calls out a 
number (or a combination of numbers), 
the corresponding players from each 
team sprint to the center, grab the ball, 
and play 1v1 until a score. Increase the 
challenge by calling multiple numbers at 
once for 2v2 or 3v3 play.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
13
BASKETBALL SKILLS & DRILLS

fiches.title: VOLLEYBALL SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
14
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Volleyballs (lightweight options like beach balls 
for beginners), cones, hula hoops, poly spots, 
jump ropes, pool noodles
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
PASSING 
SERVING
ACCURACY
COURT 
POSITIONING
TEAM 
COMMUNICATION
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
VOLLEYBALL 
BALLOON TAP  
Students keep a balloon in 
the air using bump or set 
motions, focusing on 
controlled, upward hits. 
Add challenges like moving 
through a cone-marked 
area while keeping the 
balloon in play.
WALL PASS 
CHALLENGE 
Students work in 
pairs to bump or 
set the ball 
against a wall, 
counting 
consecutive hits 
while maintaining 
proper form.
SERVE AND 
SPRINT
Students practice 
underhand or 
overhand serves 
from the serving 
line. After serving, 
they sprint to 
retrieve their ball 
and reset.
TARGET 
PASSING
Set up hula hoops 
or cones as 
targets. Students 
practice passing 
(bump or set) to 
land the ball in the 
designated targets.
CIRCLE BUMP 
RELAY
In groups of 4-5, 
students form a circle 
and pass the ball using 
bumping motions. The 
team aims to keep the 
ball in play as long as 
possible.
SKILL BUILDERS  
BUMP, SET, 
CATCH 
SERVE TO 
TARGET
MOVEMENT AND 
PASS DRILL
 
Students partner up 
and practice bumping 
the ball to their 
partner, who catches 
and sets it back. 
Gradually progress to 
continuous bump-set 
rallies.
 
Students serve the 
ball over the net, 
aiming to land it 
inside speciﬁc areas 
marked by cones or 
poly spots.
 
Students move 
through a 
cone-marked course 
and must bump or set 
the ball back to a 
partner after 
completing the 
movement path.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
4-SQUARE VOLLEYBALL
Divide the court into four equal sections 
using nets (or cones with ropes). Each 
section is occupied by a team of 3-4 
players. The game begins with a serve 
from one team into an opposing square. 
The receiving team must pass the ball 
(bump or set) within their square before 
sending it to another square. If a team 
fails to control or return the ball, they are 
eliminated for the round, and play 
continues until one team remains. Use a 
beach ball or balloon to make it easier.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
14
VOLLEYBALL SKILLS & DRILLS

fiches.title: FLAG FOOTBALL SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Flag belts, footballs, cones, pinnies, hula hoops
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
AND 
CATCHING
ROUTE 
RUNNING
AGILITY
DEFENSIVE 
POSITIONING
TEAMWORK
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FLAG GRAB RELAY
Students sprint through a 
cone-marked course 
while teammates attempt 
to pull their ﬂags. Focus 
is on speed, agility, and 
evasive movements.
PARTNER ROUTE 
PASSING 
In pairs, one student 
runs a basic route 
(e.g., slant, curl, out), 
and their partner 
throws a pass. 
Switch roles after 
3-5 passes.
END ZONE 
ACCURACY 
TOSS
Students practice 
throwing footballs 
into hula hoops or 
targets placed in an 
“end zone” to 
develop passing 
accuracy.
FLAG CHASE
One student is “it” 
and attempts to 
grab as many ﬂags 
as possible within 
a set area while 
others evade. 
Students with 
pulled ﬂags 
perform quick 
ﬁtness tasks before 
rejoining.
QUICK FEET 
AGILITY LADDER
Students run through 
an agility ladder (or 
taped lines on the 
ﬂoor), alternating quick 
foot patterns like high 
knees or lateral steps.
SKILL BUILDERS  
ROUTE TREE 
PRACTICE
FLAG PULLING 
DRILL
QUARTERBACK 
TARGET 
 
Teach students basic 
ﬂag football routes 
(e.g., slant, post, out, 
go). Players run 
designated routes 
while partners throw 
passes.
 
Students practice 
defensive ﬂag pulling 
in pairs. One partner 
runs while the other 
works on footwork 
and pulling the ﬂag 
without contact.
 
Students aim to throw 
passes to teammates 
moving within 
designated areas 
(marked by cones) to 
develop throwing 
accuracy under light 
pressure.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
3-PLAY DRIVE CHALLENGE
Divide students into small teams of 5-8 
players. Each team gets three plays to 
advance the football to a designated 
scoring zone (end zone) marked with 
cones. Teams must pass or run the ball 
while the defense works to pull ﬂags. If 
the offense scores, they rotate to 
defense, and a new team begins. If they 
fail to score, the defense takes over 
possession. Rotate teams frequently to 
ensure full participation. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
15
FLAG FOOTBALL SKILLS & DRILLS

others evade.

pulled ﬂags
perform quick
ﬁtness tasks before
rejoining.
QUICK FEET
AGILITY LADDER
Students run through
an agility ladder (or
taped lines on the
ﬂoor), alternating quick
foot patterns like high
knees or lateral steps.
SKILL BUILDERS
ROUTE TREE
PRACTICE
FLAG PULLING
DRILL
QUARTERBACK
TARGET
Teach students basic
ﬂag football routes
(e.g., slant, post, out,
go). Players run
designated routes
while partners throw
passes.
Students practice
defensive ﬂag pulling
in pairs. One partner
runs while the other
works on footwork
and pulling the ﬂag
without contact.
Students aim to throw
passes to teammates
moving within
designated areas
(marked by cones) to
develop throwing
accuracy under light
pressure.
LARGE GROUP GAME
3-PLAY DRIVE CHALLENGE
Divide students into small teams of 5-8
players. Each team gets three plays to
advance the football to a designated
scoring zone (end zone) marked with
cones. Teams must pass or run the ball
while the defense works to pull ﬂags. If
the offense scores, they rotate to
defense, and a new team begins. If they
fail to score, the defense takes over
possession. Rotate teams frequently to
ensure full participation.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
15
FLAG FOOTBALL SKILLS & DRILLS

fiches.title: ULTIMATE DISC SKILLS & DRILLS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, frisbees, pinnies, hula hoops
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
ACCURACY
CATCHING
MOVEMENT OFF 
THE DISC
DEFENSIVE 
POSITIONING
TEAMWORK
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
PARTNER 
ACCURACY 
THROWS
Partners stand at 
increasing distances and 
practice forehand and 
backhand throws, aiming 
to hit a target or land the 
disc inside a hula hoop.
DISC 
KEEP-AWAY
Students work in 
small groups of 4-5 to 
play a game of 
keep-away, passing 
the disc among 
teammates while one 
player tries to 
intercept it.
END ZONE 
QUICK PASS
Students partner up 
and stand on 
opposite sides of a 
cone-marked “end 
zone.” They 
complete quick 
passes while the 
partner stays inside 
the end zone.
MIRROR 
MOVEMENT
Students work in 
pairs where one is 
the “thrower” and 
the other mirrors 
their movements, 
simulating 
offensive and 
defensive 
positioning.
SPRINT & TOSS 
RELAY
Students sprint to a 
cone, throw a disc to a 
teammate, and run 
back to tag the next 
player. The team that 
completes the 
sequence fastest wins.
SKILL BUILDERS  
THROW-CATCH 
TRANSITION
DEFENSIVE 
SHADOW DRILL
TRIANGLE 
PASSING
 
Students practice 
throwing to a moving 
partner who must 
catch the disc while 
continuing to move 
into space. Focus on 
timing and lead 
passes.
 
Students pair up, with 
one player as the 
“offense” and the 
other as the 
“defender,” working 
to mirror their 
partner’s cuts and 
movements.
 
Students form groups 
of three and pass the 
disc in a triangle 
while continuously 
moving to new 
positions to 
emphasize movement 
off the disc.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
ZONE ULTIMATE CHALLENGE
Divide the playing area into three zones 
(defensive, midﬁeld, and attacking). 
Teams of 5-7 players work to advance the 
disc through all three zones to score in an 
end zone. Players must complete at least 
one pass in each zone before 
progressing. Defensive players work to 
intercept the disc and force turnovers. If 
the disc hits the ground or is intercepted, 
possession changes.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
16
ULTIMATE DISC SKILLS & DRILLS

17
LOCOMOTOR SKILLS
FITNESS & 
CONDITIONING UNITS

fiches.title: CARDIO CIRCUIT TRAINING
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Equipment: Cones, jump ropes, agility ladders, 
poly spots, small hurdles, medicine balls 
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
SPEED  
AGILITY 
COORDINATION
CARDIOVASCULAR 
ENDURANCE
FITNESS GOAL 
SETTING
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
HIGH KNEES 
CONE RUN
Students run in place 
performing high 
knees while weaving 
through a series of 
cones.
JUMP ROPE 
CHALLENGE
Students complete 
a set number of 
jumps (e.g., 50) 
with a jump rope, 
focusing on rhythm 
and endurance.
AGILITY LADDER 
SPRINTS
Students run 
through an agility 
ladder using various 
patterns: high knees, 
lateral steps, and 
double-foot hops.
 
4-CORNER 
CARDIO DASH
Each corner has an 
exercise (High 
Knees, Fast Feet, 
Squat Jumps, 
Burpees). Rotate to 
a new corner every 
30 seconds.
SPOT SPRINT 
INTERVALS
Place poly spots around 
the gym. Students sprint 
to a designated spot 
and perform a task (e.g., 
10 jumping jacks, 5 
squats) before moving 
to the next.
SKILL BUILDERS  
CARDIO 
STATIONS
PARTNER SPRINT 
& REST
CARDIO 
LADDER
 
Set up stations that 
emphasize cardio 
endurance (Jump 
Rope, Line Hops, 
Burpees, Cone 
Sprints). Rotate 
through every 60 
seconds.
 
In pairs, one student 
sprints the length of 
the gym while the 
other rests. Switch 
roles after each sprint. 
Gradually increase 
the number of sprints.
 
Students complete a 
progression of tasks 
(e.g., 5 squats, 10 
push-ups, 15 jumping 
jacks, 20 lunges), 
then repeat in 
reverse.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
LINE TAG
Use the existing gym court lines as the 
playing area (or use chalk or tape to 
make your own). Designate 2-3 students 
as taggers while the rest of the class 
spreads out on the lines. Players must 
stay on the lines as they move to avoid 
being tagged. If a player is tagged, they 
step off the lines and complete a ﬁtness 
task (e.g., 10 jumping jacks or 5 squats) 
before rejoining the game. Rotate taggers 
every 2-3 minutes to ensure everyone has 
a chance to participate. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
18
CARDIO CIRCUIT TRAINING

fiches.title: STRENGTH & POWER TRAINING
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, mats, resistance bands, medicine balls 
(optional), small hurdles, poly spots(optional)
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
MUSCULAR 
STRENGTH
  
EXPLOSIVE 
POWER
 
BODY CONTROL
ENDURANCE
PROPER FORM
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
MEDICINE BALL 
RELAY
Students pass a 
medicine ball (or 
weighted ball) down 
their team line, 
alternating overhead 
passes and chest 
passes.
POWER HOP 
LADDER
Students perform 
explosive 
two-footed hops 
through an agility 
ladder, focusing on 
height and control. 
Add variations like 
single-leg or lateral 
hops.
PUSH-UP 
PATHWAY
Mark a series of poly 
spots on the ground. 
Students perform 1-2 
push-ups at each 
spot as they move 
along the path.
BROAD JUMP 
CHALLENGE
Students perform 
broad jumps for 
maximum distance, 
landing in a squat 
position
PARTNER PUSH-UP 
HIGH FIVES
Partners face each other 
and complete push-ups, 
high-ﬁving after each 
rep.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
RESISTANCE 
BAND CIRCUIT
HURDLE JUMPS
  
PARTNER PLANK 
PUSH
 Students rotate 
through resistance 
band exercises 
targeting major 
muscle groups: 
squats, band rows, 
overhead presses, 
and lateral band 
walks.
 Using small hurdles 
or cones, students 
practice controlled, 
explosive jumps, 
landing softly in a 
squat position to 
develop power and 
balance.
 Students hold a 
plank position while 
their partner provides 
light resistance by 
gently pressing on 
their back or 
shoulders. 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
POWER TOWER
Divide the class into teams and provide 
each team with a set of cones, bean bags, 
or small objects. At the far end of the gym, 
set up “Power Towers” (cones stacked in 
increasing heights). Students take turns 
running to the tower station, completing a 
power-focused task (e.g., 5 burpees, 3 
explosive squat jumps, or 10 push-ups), and 
adding one object to their team’s tower. 
Teams compete to build the tallest, most 
stable tower within a set time limit. If their 
tower falls, they must return to the start to 
rebuild. This game focuses on explosive 
movement, endurance, and teamwork.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
19
STRENGTH & POWER TRAINING

fiches.title: AGILITY & SPEED TRAINING
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, agility ladders (or tape), poly spots, 
hurdles, jump ropes, pool noodles
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
QUICKNESS
  
EXPLOSIVE 
POWER
 
DIRECTIONAL 
CHANGES
COORDINATION
REACTION 
TIME
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CONE REACTION 
SPRINT
Students react to the 
teacher’s directional 
call, sprinting or 
shufﬂing to nearby 
cones as quickly as 
possible.
MIRROR 
AGILITY
Partners mimic 
each other’s quick 
movements (e.g., 
sprints, shufﬂes, 
backpedals). 
Switch leader and 
follower every 30 
seconds.
HURDLE HOP 
CHALLENGE
Students hop over 
hurdles or cones 
continuously, 
focusing on quick, 
controlled landings 
without stopping.
CONE 
REACTION 
SHUFFLE
Place different 
color cones around 
the perimeter. Call 
a color. Students 
side shufﬂe to the 
cone and back.
5-DOT DRILL
Place poly spots in a 
”dice ﬁve” pattern. 
Students hop between 
each spot for 20 
seconds.
SKILL BUILDERS  
AGILITY LADDER 
PATTERNS
SHUTTLE SPRINT 
DRILL
4-CORNER 
QUICK STEPS
Students move 
through ladders using 
high knees, lateral 
steps, single-leg 
hops, or quick 
in-and-out footwork.
Students sprint 
between two cones, 
practicing sharp turns 
and quick 
accelerations. 
Increase distance for 
added difﬁculty.
Students follow 
teacher directions to 
sprint, shufﬂe, or 
backpedal quickly to 
all four corners of a 
marked square.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
NOODLE TAG SHOWDOWN
Divide students into a Red team and Blue 
team and ﬁnd a partner on the other team. 
Give each pair a pool noodle. Place a cone 
or poly spot as a “home base” for each 
player 10-15 feet behind them. Partners 
stand facing each other in the middle of the 
play area, holding the pool noodle. 
Teacher will signal identify the tagging 
team by yelling Red or Blue. The tagger 
tries to tag their partner with the pool 
noodle before they can make it back to 
their home base. Randomly switch tagging 
teams and repeat.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
20
AGILITY & SPEED TRAINING

fiches.title: TABATA TRAINING FOR TEENS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, mats, jump ropes, small hurdles, 
resistance bands, poly spots, timers
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
STRENGTH 
DEVELOPMENT
MOVEMENT 
CONTROL
EXPLOSIVE 
POWER
CARDIOVASCULAR 
ENDURANCE
FITNESS GOAL 
SETTING
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
QUICK TABATA 
BURSTS
Do high knees, 
squats, and jumping 
jacks for 20 seconds. 
Rest 10 seconds 
between sets.
LADDER 
SPEED DRILL
Use an agility 
ladder (or tape) to 
do fast footwork 
patterns for 20 
seconds,. Rest 10 
seconds between 
sets.
CORE TABATA 
COUNTDOWN
Do planks, sit-ups 
and mountain 
climbers for 20 
seconds. Rest 10 
seconds between 
sets.
SQUAT TO 
STAR JUMPS
Do a squat, then 
jump into a star 
position and land. 
Repeat for 20 
seconds, then rest 
for 10 seconds.
SPEED SKATERS
Leap laterally from side 
to side, landing on the 
outside foot for 20 
seconds. Rest 10 
seconds between sets.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
4-STATION 
TABATA CIRCUIT
RESISTANCE 
BAND POWER
TABATA PARTNER 
CHALLENGE
 
Rotate through four 
stations: jump squats, 
push-ups, mountain 
climbers, and lateral 
shufﬂes. Each station 
follows 20/10 timing.
 
Students perform 
squats, rows, and 
overhead presses 
with resistance bands, 
holding each 
movement for 20 
seconds of work.
 
Partners alternate 
exercises (e.g., one 
does burpees while 
the other rests). 
Switch roles every 20 
seconds for 2 
minutes.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
TABATA RELAY RACE
Divide the class into teams and set up 4 
stations around the gym or ﬁeld. Each 
station features a Tabata-style task such 
as jump rope, burpees, lunges, or plank 
holds. On the teacher’s signal, one player 
from each team completes 20 seconds of 
work before sprinting back to tag the next 
teammate. Teams rotate through all 
stations in relay fashion. The game 
emphasizes teamwork, speed, and 
endurance while keeping the energy high 
with quick intervals of activity.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
21
TABATA TRAINING FOR TEENS

fiches.title: HIIT (HIGH-INTENSITY INTERVAL TRAINING)
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
22
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, mats, jump ropes, medicine balls 
(optional), agility ladders, poly spots, timers
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
STRENGTH 
DEVELOPMENT
SPEED AND 
AGILITY
EXPLOSIVE 
POWER
CARDIOVASCULAR 
ENDURANCE
MENTAL 
TOUGHNESS
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
SPRINT AND 
REST INTERVALS
Students sprint 
between cones for 30 
seconds, then rest for 
30 seconds.
JUMP ROPE 
HIIT
Students jump 
rope as fast as 
possible for 30 
seconds, then rest 
for 30 seconds. 
MOUNTAIN 
CLIMBER BLITZ�Do mountain 
climbers for 30 
seconds, followed 
by 30 seconds of 
rest. 
SQUAT JUMPS
Do explosive squat 
jumps for 30 
seconds, then rest 
for 30 seconds.
LATERAL 
SHUFFLES
Shufﬂe side-to-side 
between cones for 30 
seconds, then rest for 
30 seconds.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
FULL-BODY HIIT 
BLAST
PUSH-UP 
PYRAMID
CRUNCH TO 
SPRINT
 
Do 30 seconds each: 
jumping jacks, 
lunges, planks, side 
shufﬂes. Rest for 1 
minute between 
circuits.
 
Students perform 
increasing sets of 
push-ups (e.g., 2, 4, 6) 
with a 10-second 
break in between 
sets.
 
Do 5 crunches, then 
immediately sprint 
across the gym and 
back. Rest for 30 
seconds and repeat.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
PARTNER HIIT SHOWDOWN
Students pair up and work together to 
complete a series of HIIT exercises. At the 
teacher’s call, one partner completes 30 
seconds of an activity (e.g., burpees, 
plank-to-push-up, or high knees), while 
the other rests and encourages them. 
After the interval, partners switch roles. 
Continue for 4-5 rounds, alternating 
between exercises like squat jumps, 
shuttle sprints, and mountain climbers. To 
add excitement, track total repetitions 
completed as a pair and set team goals. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
22
HIIT (HIGH-INTENSITY INTERVAL TRAINING)

fiches.title: CORE STRENGTH & STABILITY
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Mats, cones, resistance bands, medicine balls 
(optional), poly spots
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
STRENGTH 
DEVELOPMENT
CORE 
STABILITY
BALANCE
ENDURANCE
BODY 
CONTROL
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
PLANK CIRCLE 
WALK
Students hold a plank 
and “walk” their 
hands and feet 
around a cone or 
spot, maintaining a 
tight core.
BEAR CRAWL 
CHALLENGE
Bear crawl through 
a cone-marked 
path while 
maintaining steady 
core engagement 
and proper form.
BALANCE HOLD 
COUNTDOWN�Balance on one foot, 
holding for 10 
seconds. Switch feet 
and progress to 
adding arms or 
small movements.
SIT-UP TO 
PASS
Pairs sit 
back-to-back, 
performing sit-ups 
while passing a 
ball or bean bag 
between them 
after each rep.
SIDE PLANK 
HIP DIPS
Hold a side plank and 
lower their hips to the 
ﬂoor, then lift back up 
for controlled reps.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
PLANK 
VARIATIONS
MEDICINE BALL 
PASS
SUPERMAN 
HOLDS
 
Students rotate 
through a series of 
planks: standard, side 
planks, plank with 
shoulder taps, and 
plank-to-push-ups.
 
In pairs, students sit 
back-to-back and 
twist to pass a 
medicine ball or small 
weighted object side 
to side.
 
Students lie on their 
stomachs and lift 
their arms and legs 
simultaneously, 
holding for 10-15 
seconds to 
strengthen lower 
back muscles.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
CORE FREEZE CHALLENGE
All students move freely around the gym 
(e.g., jogging, shufﬂing, skipping) while 
music plays. When the music stops, the 
teacher calls out a core position (e.g., 
“Plank!” “Side plank!” “Superman!”). 
Students quickly freeze and hold the 
position for 10-15 seconds. Gradually 
increase the challenge by adding 
variations like balancing on one arm or 
lifting a leg while holding the position. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
23
CORE STRENGTH & STABILITY

TEAMWORK & SEL UNITS

fiches.title: TEAM BUILDING FITNESS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, hula hoops, pool noodles, jump 
ropes, poly spots, bean bags, scooters
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK 
 PROBLEM-
SOLVING 
LEADERSHIP
COMMUNICATION
COOPERATION 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
TEAM 
COUNT-OFF
Teams must count out 
loud from 1-20. Only 
one player speaks at 
a time, and no 
planning is allowed. 
GROUP 
JUGGLE 
CHALLENGE
Teams pass 
multiple balls to 
each other in a set 
sequence, adding 
more as they 
succeed.
TEAM PLANK 
CHAIN
Teams line up in a 
plank position. One 
at a time, students 
crawl under 
teammates to reach 
the end. 
GROUP SQUAT 
HOLD
Teams form a circle 
and hold a 
synchronized 
squat, passing a 
ball around the 
group without 
standing up.
PUSH-UP PASS 
RELAY
Push-Up Pass Relay
Teams pass a ball 
down the line while 
holding a push-up 
position. Drop the 
ball? Restart from the 
beginning.
SKILL BUILDERS  
TARP FLIP 
CHALLENGE
DESIGN A 
WORKOUT
HUMAN 
MACHINES
 
Teams stand on a 
tarp and must work 
together to ﬂip it over 
without stepping off.
 
Teams work together 
to design a 5 minute 
ﬁtness circuit, then 
demonstrate it for the 
rest of the class.
 
 
Teams work together 
to form creative 
“machines” (e.g., 
clock, car, blender) 
using only their 
bodies.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
CROSS THE RIVER
Set up a “river” using two long lines of 
cones, with poly spots or mats as 
stepping stones in between. Divide the 
class into teams, and give each team the 
same equipment (poly spots, jump ropes, 
pool noodles, scooters etc). Teams must 
use the equipment to get their entire 
group across the river without touching 
the “water” (ﬂoor). If anyone steps off a 
stone, the team must restart.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
25
TEAM BUILDING FITNESS

fiches.title: LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
26
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, hula hoops, pool noodles, jump 
ropes, poly spots, bean bags, scooters
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK 
 PROBLEM-
SOLVING 
LEADERSHIP 
SKILLS
COMMUNICATION
DECISION-
MAKING
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
CIRCLE 
STRETCH
Form a large circle. 
Students take turns 
walking to the middle 
and leading a stretch.
CIRCLE 
FITNESS
Form a large 
circle. Students 
take turns walking 
to the middle and 
leading an 
exercise.
HUMAN 
ALPHABET
A leader verbally 
directs teammates 
to form speciﬁc 
letters or words 
using their bodies.
TOWER OF 
POWER
A leader verbally 
directs their team 
to build the tallest 
free-standing 
structure using 
using provided 
equipment.
DANCING WITH 
THE STARS
A leader choregraphs 
a short dance routine 
for their team. Each 
team performs for the 
class.
SKILL BUILDERS  
BLINDFOLD 
MAZE
OBSTACLE 
COURSE 
BUILDERS
BLINDFOLDED 
PYRAMID
 
A leader verbally 
directs their 
blindfolded 
teammates through a 
maze made of cones.
 
Each team builds a 
short obstacle course 
for the rest of the 
class and explains 
how to navigate it.
 
A leader verbally 
directs their 
blindfolded 
teammates to stack 
blocks into a 
pyramid.
 
LARGE GROUP GAME  
TEAM GRID ESCAPE
Set up a 6x6 grid of cones across the gym 
or ﬁeld. Teams start at one corner of the 
grid with the goal of reaching the 
opposite corner. The teacher knows the 
“safe path” through the grid but does not 
share it. The captain sends one player at 
a time to step into a square. If the square 
is correct, the student advances; if it’s 
wrong, they return to the start, and the 
captain sends another player. Teams 
must communicate and strategize to 
remember the safe route. The ﬁrst team 
to successfully get all players through the 
grid wins.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
26
LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT

fiches.title: COOPERATIVE GAMES
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, hula hoops, poly spots, bean bags, 
gym mats, parachutes, and blindfolds
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
TEAMWORK 
 PROBLEM-
SOLVING 
TRUST-BUILDING
COMMUNICATION
COLLABORATION
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
MEMORY 
JUGGLE
Teams toss a ball in a 
sequence, 
remembering the 
order. 
PARTNER 
DODGEBALL
Players partner 
up. If one is hit, the 
other performs a 
ﬁtness task to 
“revive” them. 
DESERT 
ISLAND
Teams strategize to 
get all students 
standing on a small 
“desert island” mat 
without falling off. 
TUNNEL BALL�Students line up 
with feet spread. 
One student rolls a 
ball through the 
“tunnel” of legs, 
aiming for the end.
BLOB TAG�Tagged players join 
hands with the “blob” 
(tagger). The blob 
grows larger, working 
together to tag 
remaining players.
SKILL BUILDERS  
MEDIC TAG
PARACHUTE 
SWITCH
MINEFIELD
 
Taggers freeze 
players. A “medic” 
unfreezes teammates 
by tagging them. 
Teams protect their 
medic while 
strategizing to stay 
active.
 
Students lift a 
parachute into the 
air. When the teacher 
calls a category (e.g., 
“everyone wearing 
blue”), those 
students switch 
places underneath.
 
Students direct a 
blindfolded partner 
through a cone maze, 
avoiding poly spot 
“mines” along the 
way.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
ROCK PAPER SCISSORS 
SHOWDOWN
Set up a lines of 10-20 hula hoops with 
teams starting at opposite ends. One 
player from each team hops through their 
hoops, meeting the opposing player in 
the middle for a Rock Paper Scissors 
showdown. The winner continues 
hopping forward while the losing player 
returns to their team to send in a new 
challenger. The ﬁrst team to get a player 
all the way to the opposing team’s end 
scores a point. 
 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
27
COOPERATIVE GAMES

fiches.title: LIFETIME ACTIVITIES
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
28
LIFETIME ACTIVITIES 
UNITS

fiches.title: YOGA & MINDFULNESS
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Yoga mats (or towels), cones, scarves, 
calming music (optional)
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
STRESS 
MANAGEMENT
 
FLEXIBILITY
   
FOCUS AND 
BREATHING
BALANCE
MIND-BODY 
AWARENESS
 
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
BREATHING 
SQUARE
 Inhale, hold, exhale, 
and pause for 4 
counts each, tracing 
an imaginary square 
with your ﬁnger.
 
CAT-COW 
STRETCH
On hands and 
knees, arch your 
back (Cat) then 
drop your belly 
(Cow), focusing on 
slow, steady 
breathing.
 
CHILD’S POSE
Sit back on your 
heels, stretch arms 
forward on the 
ﬂoor, and rest 
forehead down, 
breathing deeply.
 
 
MOUNTAIN 
POSE
Stand tall with feet 
together, arms at 
your sides, and 
focus on breathing 
deeply while 
maintaining good 
posture. 
TREE POSE
Balance on one foot, 
placing the other foot 
on your inner calf, 
with hands together 
overhead.
SKILL BUILDERS  
SUNRISE FLOW
GROUNDING 
FLOW
ENERGIZING 
FLOW
  
 
Start in Mountain 
Pose > Forward Fold 
> Halfway Lift > 
Downward Dog > 
Cobra Pose > Return 
to Mountain Pose, 
focusing on breath.
 Begin in Child’s Pose 
> Cat-Cow Stretch > 
Downward Dog > 
Warrior II > Tree Pose 
> End in Mountain 
Pose with deep 
breaths.
 
 
Start in Mountain 
Pose > Forward Fold 
> Lunge > Warrior I > 
Plank Pose > 
Downward Dog> 
Finish in Child’s Pose 
to relax.
LARGE GROUP GAME 
MINDFUL MOVEMENT RELAY
Divide the class into teams and set up a 
course of poly spots or cones. Each spot 
features a yoga pose or mindful 
challenge (e.g., Warrior Pose, 10 deep 
breaths, or 15 seconds of stillness). One 
student at a time moves calmly to each 
spot, performs the task, and returns to 
tag the next teammate. Teams focus on 
completing the course with patience, 
control, and mindfulness, rather than 
speed.  
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
29
YOGA & MINDFULNESS

fiches.title: DANCE & MOVEMENT
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Music player, scarves, video player, pool 
noodles, exercise balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
RHYTHM 
AWARENESS
COORDINATION
   
CREATIVE 
EXPRESSION
GROUP 
SYNCHRONIZATION
CARDIOVASCULAR 
FITNESS
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FOLLOW THE 
LEADER DANCE 
LINE
Students follow a 
leader in a line, 
mimicking dance 
moves as they move 
across the gym or 
studio ﬂoor.
 
FOLLOW THE 
BEAT
Students perform 
movements (claps, 
jumps, steps) to 
match the rhythm 
of the music 
played by the 
teacher. 
 
MIRROR 
DANCE
In pairs, one 
student leads 
dance moves while 
the partner mirrors. 
 
 
DANCE SIMON 
SAYS
Teacher calls out 
dance moves with 
“Simon says.” 
Students only 
move when “Simon 
says” is included.
 
MUSICAL 
STATUES
Students hold a 
creative dance pose 
when the music 
stops. Those who 
wobble do a ﬁtness 
task and rejoin.
SKILL BUILDERS  
SCARVES FLOW 
MOVEMENT
DANCE MOVE 
TUTORIAL
DRUM CIRCLE
  
  
 
Students use scarves 
to perform slow, 
controlled 
movements to 
calming music. 
  
Find a dance move 
tutorial online, play it 
for the kids and learn 
it.
 
  
 
Students tap rhythms 
on exercise balls with 
pool noodles, 
creating a group 
drumbeat. Rotate 
leaders to set the 
rhythm.
LARGE GROUP GAME 
DANCE BATTLE CIRCLES
Divide the class into 4-5 groups, each 
forming a circle. Groups take turns 
sending one dancer to the center to 
perform a move, which the rest of the 
group mimics in rhythm. Rotate through 
all group members to keep the energy 
ﬂowing. Use upbeat music to inspire fun, 
and emphasize creativity and 
participation over competition. This 
activity builds conﬁdence, rhythm 
awareness, and team synchronization.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
DANCE & MOVEMENT

sections.Equipment:
6-8
Music player, scarves, video player, pool
noodles, exercise balls
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
RHYTHM
AWARENESS
COORDINATION
CREATIVE
EXPRESSION
GROUP
SYNCHRONIZATION
CARDIOVASCULAR
FITNESS
INSTANT ACTIVITIES
FOLLOW THE
LEADER DANCE
LINE
Students follow a
leader in a line,
mimicking dance
moves as they move
across the gym or
studio ﬂoor.
FOLLOW THE
BEAT
Students perform
movements (claps,
jumps, steps) to
match the rhythm
of the music
played by the
teacher.
MIRROR
DANCE
In pairs, one
student leads
dance moves while
the partner mirrors.
DANCE SIMON
SAYS
Teacher calls out
dance moves with
“Simon says.”
Students only
move when “Simon
says” is included.
MUSICAL
STATUES
Students hold a
creative dance pose
when the music
stops. Those who
wobble do a ﬁtness
task and rejoin.
SKILL BUILDERS
SCARVES FLOW
MOVEMENT
DANCE MOVE
TUTORIAL
DRUM CIRCLE
Students use scarves
to perform slow,
controlled
movements to
calming music.
Find a dance move
tutorial online, play it
for the kids and learn
it.
Students tap rhythms
on exercise balls with
pool noodles,
creating a group
drumbeat. Rotate
leaders to set the
rhythm.
LARGE GROUP GAME
DANCE BATTLE CIRCLES
Divide the class into 4-5 groups, each
forming a circle. Groups take turns
sending one dancer to the center to
perform a move, which the rest of the
group mimics in rhythm. Rotate through
all group members to keep the energy
ﬂowing. Use upbeat music to inspire fun,
and emphasize creativity and
participation over competition. This
activity builds conﬁdence, rhythm
awareness, and team synchronization.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
30
DANCE & MOVEMENT

fiches.title: FITNESS KNOWLEDGE
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Cones, poly spots, whiteboards (optional), timers, 
jump ropes, resistance bands, heart rate charts
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
UNDERSTANDING 
COMPONENTS 
OF FITNESS
GOAL SETTING
EXERCISE 
BENEFITS
PERSONAL 
HEALTH 
AWARENESS
HEART RATE 
ZONES
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
HEART RATE 
DASH
Students sprint to a 
cone, stop to count 
their pulse for 10 
seconds, and 
determine their heart 
rate. Compare before 
and after activity.
 
EXERCISE 
DICE
Teacher picks an 
exercise. Students 
roll dice to 
determine how 
many sets and 
reps they will 
perform.
LABEL THE 
MUSCLE GAME
Students perform 
exercises (e.g., 
push-ups, lunges) 
and identify which 
major muscle group 
is being used.
 
FITNESS 
TRIVIA
Teams compete to 
answer health and 
ﬁtness questions. 
Winning team 
chooses the game 
to play next.
 
FITNESS BINGO
Students complete a 
ﬁtness bingo card 
with tasks like “10 
push-ups” or 
“20-second plank.”
SKILL BUILDERS  
HEART RATE 
TRAINING
CIRCUIT 
CHALLENGE 
CREATION
FITNESS 
COMPONENT 
SORT
 
Students learn about 
target heart rate 
zones and complete 
exercises to elevate 
their pulse to speciﬁc 
ranges. 
In small groups, 
students design a 
mini-circuit targeting 
one ﬁtness 
component and lead 
their peers through it.
 
 
Place activity cards 
around the gym 
labeled “Cardio,” 
“Strength,” 
“Flexibility,” and 
“Endurance.” Call out 
an exercise. Students 
run to the correct 
category and perform 
it.
LARGE GROUP GAME 
CARDIO ZONE QUEST
Students work in teams to complete a 
gym-wide challenge involving heart rate 
zones. Teams start at “resting” and move 
through levels of activity to reach speciﬁc 
heart rate zones (measured by pulse 
checks or heart rate monitors). Activities 
include light jogging, jump rope, squats, 
and burpees. Teams record their pulse 
readings at each level and decide 
together when to move to the next 
challenge. The ﬁrst team to hit all zones 
and return to “cool down” wins. This 
game emphasizes goal setting, heart rate 
monitoring, and teamwork.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
31

fiches.title: PICKLEBALL FUNDAMENTALS
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
32
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8
Pickleball paddles, wiﬄe balls, cones, poly spots, 
portable nets (or ropes as substitutes), chalk/tape 
for court boundaries
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
PADDLE 
CONTROL
SERVING 
ACCURACY
HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION
COURT 
AWARENESS
TEAMWORK
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
PADDLE 
BALANCE WALK
Students balance a 
wifﬂe ball on their 
paddle while walking 
through a 
cone-marked path 
without dropping it.
 
PADDLE TAP
Students tap a ball 
upward on their 
paddle as many 
times as possible 
without it dropping 
to the ground.
 
WALL RALLY
Students perform 
Students rally the 
ball off a wall using 
controlled forehand 
or backhand hits. 
Count consecutive 
hits.
SERVE TO 
TARGET
Students practice 
underhand serves, 
aiming to land the 
ball into marked 
zones on the 
opposite side of 
the court.
SKILL BUILDERS  
DROP SHOT
PARTNER PADDLE 
PASS
LOB SHOT
  
  
 
Students hit soft shots 
to land the ball inside 
a “no volley” zone 
(kitchen), focusing on 
control and precision.
  
Pairs hit the ball back 
and forth, focusing on 
control and 
consistency. Start 
close together, then 
increase the 
distance.
 
Students practice 
hitting high-arching 
shots over a net or 
line, aiming to land 
the ball deep in the 
court.
LARGE GROUP GAME 
PICKLEBALL MINI MATCHES
Divide the class into small teams and set 
up multiple mini courts (use cones or tape 
to create boundaries if needed). Teams 
play short matches to 5 points, rotating 
partners and opponents every 5 minutes. 
Matches use modiﬁed rules to emphasize 
fundamental skills: underhand serving, no 
volleys in the “kitchen” (near the net), 
and controlled returns. Encourage 
students to focus on accuracy and 
teamwork rather than power. This game 
builds skills, court awareness, and 
strategy in a fun, competitive format.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
32
PICKLEBALL FUNDAMENTALS

fiches.title: DISC GOLF
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
3-5
Cones, mats, jump ropes, foam balls, timers  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
ACCURACY
TARGET 
AWARENESS
DISTANCE 
CONTROL
STRATEGIC 
THINKING
PROBLEM-
SOLVING
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FOREHAND 
FLICK 
CHALLENGE
Practice forehand 
throws, aiming to 
land discs in 
cone-marked zones 
for control and 
accuracy.
 
BACKHAND 
ACCURACY 
TOSS
Focus on 
backhand throws, 
hitting targets like 
hula hoops or 
cones at varying 
distances.
   
HAMMER 
THROW 
PRACTICE
Students throw 
overhead 
“hammer” shots, 
aiming for high arcs 
into designated 
target areas.
DISTANCE LINE 
DRILL
Students throw 
discs progressively 
farther while 
maintaining proper 
backhand form and 
consistent 
technique.
CURVE SHOT 
PRACTICE
Throw discs around 
obstacles (cones or 
noodles) to practice 
curved throws and 
improve disc control.
 
SKILL BUILDERS  
DISC GOLF 
RELAY
TARGET 
TIC-TAC-TOE
CLOSEST TO 
THE PIN
  
 
In teams, students 
alternate throws to 
move a disc toward a 
target. The ﬁrst team 
to “hole out” wins the 
round.
  
 
Teams aim to land 
discs in a tic-tac-toe 
grid made of hula 
hoops, competing to 
connect three zones 
in a row.
 
Partners take turns 
throwing discs toward 
a target (e.g., cone or 
bucket). The closest 
disc scores a point for 
the team.
 
  
LARGE GROUP GAME  
DISC GOLF COURSE 
CHALLENGE
Create a disc golf course using cones, hula 
hoops, and buckets as “holes.” Mark out 6-9 
holes, each with increasing difﬁculty). 
Students play in small groups, taking turns 
throwing their disc toward the target. Like 
traditional golf, students take the next throw 
from where their frisbee lands. The group 
with the lowest total throws after completing 
the course wins. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
33
DISC GOLF

fiches.title: CORNHOLE
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Cornhole boards, bean bags, 
cones for boundaries  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
ACCURACY  
HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION  
STRATEGIC 
THINKING  
TEAMWORK  
 
FOCUS  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
UNDERHAND 
ACCURACY TOSS
Students practice 
underhand throws, 
aiming to land bean 
bags on a cone or 
marked target area.
ARC THE SHOT
Students practice 
higher-arching 
underhand throws, 
focusing on 
controlled height 
and landing on a 
designated zone.
DISTANCE 
CHALLENGE
Students increase 
throwing distance 
gradually while 
maintaining 
accuracy to land 
bean bags on the 
cornhole board.
 
TIMED TOSS 
DRILL
Students aim to 
land as many bean 
bags on the board 
as possible in 1 
minute.
ONE-STEP 
TOSS
Students take one 
step forward before 
tossing, focusing on 
balance and 
follow-through for 
consistent accuracy.
SKILL BUILDERS  
BEAN BAG 
RELAY
H-O-R-S-E 
CORNHOLE
ZONE SCORING 
CHALLENGE
 
  
Partners alternate 
throwing bean bags 
toward the cornhole 
board, racing to score 
a set number of 
points ﬁrst.
Partners take turns 
calling and 
completing 
challenging shots. 
Miss the shot? Get a 
letter. First to spell 
“HORSE” loses.
Teams assign point 
values to zones on 
the board. Players 
strategize to 
maximize points in a 
set number of throws.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
CORNHOLE ROUND ROBIN
Set up multiple cornhole boards around the 
gym or ﬁeld, dividing students into pairs or 
small teams. Each team rotates to a new 
board every 3-4 minutes, competing in 
quick rounds to score points by landing 
bean bags on the board (1 point) or through 
the hole (3 points). Teams keep track of 
their scores, and the pair with the highest 
total after all rotations wins. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
35
CORNHOLE

fiches.title: LADDER BALL
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
36
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Cornhole boards, bean bags, 
cones for boundaries  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
ACCURACY  
HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION  
SCORING 
STRATEGIES  
TEAMWORK  
 
FOCUS  
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
ONE-RUNG 
FOCUS
Students aim for a 
speciﬁc rung (top, 
middle, bottom), 
practicing precision 
and consistency with 
each throw.
DISTANCE 
LADDER TOSS
Students increase 
throwing distance 
gradually, 
maintaining control 
and accuracy to hit 
targeted rungs 
consistently.
ANGLE 
ADJUSTMENT 
DRILL
Students experiment 
with release angles 
to see how 
trajectory affects the 
bolas landing on 
different rungs.
TIMED LADDER 
HITS
Students aim to hit 
as many rungs as 
possible in 1 minute, 
focusing on speed 
and accuracy.
POINT 
CHALLENGE 
TOSS
Students aim to 
score an exact point 
total (e.g., 5 points) 
using strategy to 
land on speciﬁc 
rungs.
SKILL BUILDERS  
RUNG RACE 
RELAY
BOLA BATTLE
  
LADDER ZONE 
CHALLENGE
 
  
Teams alternate 
throws, aiming to hit 
each rung (bottom, 
middle, top) in order. 
First team to 
complete the 
sequence wins.
Teams compete to 
“cancel out” points 
by matching 
opponents’ throws on 
the same rung while 
scoring their own.
 
Assign different point 
values to rungs. 
Teams strategize to 
reach a set score 
(e.g., 21 points) in the 
fewest throws.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
LADDER BALL 
TOURNAMENT
Set up multiple ladder ball stations with 
score-keeping sheets. Divide the class into 
small teams (2-3 players) and rotate teams 
every 4-5 minutes, playing to a quick point 
total (e.g., 11 or 21). Teams earn points 
based on the ladder rung hit: bottom (1 
point), middle (2 points), and top (3 points). 
The focus is on consistency, teamwork, and 
strategy. Announce winning teams at the 
end of the tournament.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
36
LADDER BALL

fiches.title: SPIKEBALL
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Spikeball sets, cones for boundaries  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
REACTION 
TIME  
  
HAND-EYE 
COORDINATION  
STRIKING 
SKILLS  
TEAMWORK  
 
MOVEMENT 
AGILITY  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
INSTANT 
BOUNCE AND 
CATCH
Students practice 
gently spiking the 
ball onto the net and 
catching it, focusing 
on controlled contact.
FOREARM PASS 
PRACTICE
Pairs pass the ball to 
each other using 
forearm bumps, 
working on 
consistency and 
controlled direction.
WALL SPIKE 
CHALLENGE
Students spike the 
ball off a wall to 
themselves, 
developing accuracy 
and proper hand 
contact for spiking.
NET TAP 
CONTROL
Students tap the 
ball off the net 
softly, trying to 
bounce it no higher 
than shoulder level.
SKILL BUILDERS  
AROUND 
THE NET
3-TOUCH TEAM 
RALLY
  
NET ZONE 
CHALLENGE
 
  
Teams of 2-3 rotate 
around the net, 
spiking and 
controlling the ball 
continuously without 
missing a bounce.
Teams work together 
to complete three 
touches (bump, set, 
spike) before hitting 
the ball onto the net 
successfully.
Place cones or zones 
around the net. 
Teams score points 
by spiking the ball 
into a speciﬁc, 
designated target 
zone.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
SPIKEBALL TOURNAMENT
Set up multiple Spikeball nets and divide 
students into teams of 2-3 players. Each 
match is played to a set point total (e.g., 11 
or 15). Teams rotate every 4-5 minutes, 
allowing new matchups and maximizing 
playtime. The game focuses on accurate 
hitting, ball control, and quick reﬂexes. 
Teams keep track of their scores, and those 
with the most wins or points overall can be 
recognized at the end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
37
SPIKEBALL

working on
consistency and
controlled direction.
WALL SPIKE
CHALLENGE
Students spike the
ball off a wall to
themselves,
developing accuracy
and proper hand
contact for spiking.
NET TAP
CONTROL
Students tap the
ball off the net
softly, trying to
bounce it no higher
than shoulder level.
SKILL BUILDERS
AROUND
THE NET
3-TOUCH TEAM
RALLY
NET ZONE
CHALLENGE
Teams of 2-3 rotate
around the net,
spiking and
controlling the ball
continuously without
missing a bounce.
Teams work together
to complete three
touches (bump, set,
spike) before hitting
the ball onto the net
successfully.
Place cones or zones
around the net.
Teams score points
by spiking the ball
into a speciﬁc,
designated target
zone.
LARGE GROUP GAME
SPIKEBALL TOURNAMENT
Set up multiple Spikeball nets and divide
students into teams of 2-3 players. Each
match is played to a set point total (e.g., 11
or 15). Teams rotate every 4-5 minutes,
allowing new matchups and maximizing
playtime. The game focuses on accurate
hitting, ball control, and quick reﬂexes.
Teams keep track of their scores, and those
with the most wins or points overall can be
recognized at the end.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
37
SPIKEBALL

fiches.title: KICKBALL
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
38
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Kickball, cones, bases, pinnies    
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
KICKING 
ACCURACY  
BASE RUNNING  
THROWING AND 
CATCHING  
TEAMWORK  
 
STRATEGY   
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
TARGET KICKING
Students aim to kick a 
ball toward cone 
targets, focusing on 
accuracy, control, 
and directional 
kicking.
BASE RUNNING 
SPRINTS
Students practice 
sprinting around 
bases as quickly as 
possible, working on 
speed and sharp 
turns.
ROLLING & 
FIELDING DRILL
Students roll ground 
balls to a partner, 
who practices 
scooping and 
throwing with proper 
form to a target.
 
POP FLY 
CATCHING
Partners toss high 
balls for each other 
to catch, practicing 
hand-eye 
coordination and 
tracking the ball.
KICK AND STOP 
CHALLENGE
Students kick a ball 
and run to stop it 
within a marked 
zone, improving 
control and 
directional kicking.
SKILL BUILDERS  
KICKBALL 
ACCURACY 
RELAY
BASE HIT 
CHALLENGE
  
SMALL-SIDED 
KICKBALL
 
  
Teams take turns 
kicking a ball toward 
targets (cones or 
hoops). Points are 
earned for accuracy 
and teamwork.
Teams kick the ball 
into speciﬁc zones 
(e.g., left ﬁeld, right 
ﬁeld) while ﬁelders 
work on quick throws 
to bases.
Play modiﬁed 3v3 or 
4v4 kickball on a 
smaller ﬁeld, 
emphasizing kicking, 
base running, and 
communication skills.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
KICKBALL SCRAMBLE
Play traditional kickball with a twist. Divide 
the playing ﬁeld into point-based zones 
marked by cones. If a kicked ball lands in a 
speciﬁc zone (e.g., 3 points, 5 points), the 
kicker earns those points. Fielders must 
quickly return the ball to the pitcher to stop 
play. This scoring variation encourages 
students to strategize their kicks and 
ﬁelding. Rotate teams after three outs to 
ensure everyone participates.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
38
KICKBALL

fiches.title: BOCCE BALL
fiches.body:
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Bocce ball sets, poly spots, cones  
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
THROWING 
ACCURACY  
  
STRATEGIC 
THINKING  
 
DISTANCE 
CONTROL  
TEAMWORK  
 
PRECISION    
  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
TARGET ROLL 
CHALLENGE
Students aim to roll 
bocce balls toward 
cones or poly spots, 
focusing on accuracy 
and control.
DISTANCE ROLL 
DRILL
Students practice 
rolling the ball at 
increasing distances, 
focusing on power 
and consistency.
OBSTACLE 
ROLLING PATH
Students roll balls 
through a 
cone-marked 
pathway, improving 
precision, direction, 
and controlled force.
STOP THE BALL
Students roll bocce 
balls, trying to stop 
them as close as 
possible to a 
speciﬁc line or 
marker.
ANGLE 
PRACTICE
Students roll balls at 
an angle to knock a 
ball or hit a target 
behind obstacles 
(e.g., cones).
SKILL BUILDERS  
CLOSEST TO 
THE MARKER
  
BOCCE 
BOWLING
  
ZONE CONTROL 
CHALLENGE
  
  
Partners take turns 
rolling balls toward a 
pallino (target ball). 
The player whose 
ball is closest scores 
a point.
Teams roll bocce 
balls to knock over 
cones placed in a 
triangular formation. 
Count points based 
on fallen cones.
Divide the play area 
into scoring zones. 
Partners or teams aim 
to land balls in zones 
for varying points, 
focusing on strategy.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
BOCCE BALL BATTLES
Teams of 4-5 players take turns rolling 
bocce balls toward a central target 
(pallino). Points are awarded for balls 
landing closest to the pallino, with 
additional points for knocking opposing 
team balls away. Play multiple rounds, 
adjusting the target location to add variety. 
Teams must strategize their throws to 
outscore opponents. The game promotes 
precision, spatial awareness, and team 
collaboration in a relaxed, competitive 
format.
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
39
BOCCE BALL

fiches.title: CAPTURE THE FLAG
fiches.body:
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
40
GRADE LEVEL
EQUIPMENT  
6-8  
Cones, pinnies, ﬂags (bandanas, bean 
bags), hula hoops    
LEARNING OBJECTIVES
SPEED AND 
AGILITY  
  
STRATEGY 
 
EVASION 
SKILLS  
TEAMWORK  
 
COMMUNICATION  
INSTANT ACTIVITIES  
FLAG GRAB 
SPRINT
Students practice 
sprinting to grab ﬂags 
from cones and 
quickly return to the 
starting point.
DIRECTIONAL 
CHANGE DRILL
Students run through 
a cone-marked 
course, practicing 
quick turns, pivots, 
and changes in 
direction.
TAGGING 
TECHNIQUE 
PRACTICE
In pairs, one 
student runs while 
the other practices 
controlled, safe 
tagging techniques 
for effective 
defense.
FLAG DODGE
Students practice 
shielding their 
ﬂags using 
side-to-side 
movement while a 
partner tries to 
grab them.
SAFE ZONE
Students sprint from 
“safe zone” to “safe 
zone,” practicing 
awareness, speed, 
and timing to avoid 
being tagged.
SKILL BUILDERS  
MINI CAPTURE 
RELAY
  
SHARKS AND 
MINNOWS
  
DEFENDER 
DODGE ZONE
  
  
Teams compete to 
grab ﬂags placed in a 
central zone and 
return them to their 
side without being 
tagged.
“Minnows” sprint 
across the play area 
while avoiding 
“sharks” who are 
trying to pull their 
ﬂags. Minnows who 
have their ﬂags 
pulled become 
sharks.
Small teams attempt 
to pass through a 
cone-marked zone 
guarded by 
“defenders” to 
retrieve and return 
their team’s ﬂag.
LARGE GROUP GAME  
MULTI-FLAG MADNESS
Scatter multiple ﬂags or bean bags 
across the playing area. Teams work 
together to collect as many ﬂags as 
possible and return them to their zone 
while preventing the other team from 
doing the same. Players must 
strategize roles (e.g., runners, 
defenders, ﬂag protectors) to 
maximize points. Set a time limit, and 
at the end, count each team’s ﬂags to 
determine the winner. 
© AmericanCoachingAcademy.com
40
CAPTURE THE FLAG
